### Equations:
Q = ( 3*(c/a) - (b/a)^2 ) / 9
R = ( 9*(b/a)*(c/a) - 27*(d/a) - 2*(b/a)^3 ) / 54
D = R^2 + Q^3
S = ( R + sqrt( D ) )^(1/3)
T = ( R - sqrt( D ) )^(1/3)
x(1) = -(1/3)*(b/a) + ( S + T );
x(2) = -(1/3)*(b/a) - (1/2) * ( S + T ) + (1/2)i * sqrt(3) * ( S - T );
x(3) = -(1/3)*(b/a) - (1/2) * ( S + T ) - (1/2)i * sqrt(3) * ( S - T );
D = R^2 + Q^3
i_cmplx = sqrt(-1)
S = ( R + sqrt( D ) )^(1/3); and
T = ( R - sqrt( D ) )^(1/3).
R = ( 9*(b/a)*(c/a) - 27*(d/a) - 2*(b/a)^3 ) / 54
S = ( R + sqrt(D) )^(1/3)
T = ( R - sqrt(D) )^(1/3)
x(1) = -(1/3)*(b/a) + (S + T)
x(2) = -(1/3)*(b/a) - (1/2) * ( S + T ) + (1/2)i * sqrt(3) * ( S - T );
x(3) = -(1/3)*(b/a) - (1/2) * ( S + T ) - (1/2)i * sqrt(3) * ( S - T );
D = b^2 - 4*a*c
x(1) = ( -b + sqrt( b^2 - 4*a*c ) ) / (2*a); and
x(2) = ( -b - sqrt( b^2 - 4*a*c ) ) / (2*a).
cube_root = x**one_third when x >= 0
cube_root = -abs(x)**one_third when x < 0

### Equations:
DELTA M = ∆m [M]
cos Latitude = cos(λ)
rcoslat Latitude = 1 / cos(λ)
1 / Pressure at Midpoint = 1/P[mid]
Δ Pressure Depth of Layer = P[d] - P[mid]
Pressure at Interfaces = P[i]
Pressure at Midpoint = (P[i-0.5] + P[i+0.5]) / 2
Δ Pressure = P[mid] - P[interface]
Δ Pressure = P[mid] - P[interface]
Vertical Coordinates at Midpoints or pmid if Single Column = η[mid]
Tendency of Temperature = ∂T/∂t
Tendency of U wind = ∂U/∂t
Tendency of V wind = ∂V/∂t
Net Flux from Physics = Φ_p

_{\text{current}} & \text{otherwise}

### Equations:
$\frac{\partial t_2}{\partial t} = \text{p\_d\_coupling}$
∂t/∂t of temp tendency
$q_{\text{minus}} = q_3 + (1 - e^{-k \Delta t}) \left(  q_{\text{forcing}} \right)$
Tendency of tracer q, where Δt is the time step, k is a constant, and $q_{\text{forcing}}$ is the forcing term
$f_u = f_u + \frac{\Delta t}{2} (v_f u_l + v_b u_r) - \frac{\Delta t^2}{12 h} (v_f u_l^2 + v_b u_r^2)$
∂t/∂t of u wind tendency
$f_v = f_v + \frac{\Delta t}{2} (u_f v_l + u_b v_r) - \frac{\Delta t^2}{12 h} (u_f v_l^2 + u_b v_r^2)$
∂t/∂t of v wind tendency
PLEV (Short Notation for number of vertical levels)
PLAT (Number of latitudes)
PLON (Number of longitudes)

### Equations:
$\frac{\partial \mathrm{q}}{\partial t} = \left(\frac{\partial \mathrm{q}}{\partial t}\right)_{\mathrm{nstep}=0} + \sum_{n=1}^{N} \frac{f_n}{\Delta t}\left( - q_0^n - \frac{1}{2} (q^+ - q^-)_n \right)$
$\frac{\partial \mathrm{t}}{\partial t} = \frac{f}{\Delta t} \left(  T_0 + \frac{1}{2}(T^+-T^-) \right)$
$d_p\_coupling (ps, t3, u3, v3, q3, omga, phis, phys\_state, phys\_tend, pbuf2d, pdeld) = d\_p\_coupling (ps, t3, u3, v3, q3, omga, phis, phys\_state, phys\_tend, pbuf2d, pdeld)$
$p\_d\_coupling (phys\_state, phys\_tend, t2, fu, fv, flx\_net, qminus) = p\_d\_coupling (phys\_state, phys\_tend, t2, fu, fv, flx\_net, qminus)$

### Equations:
ϕ = gravit * ηm
∂u / ∂t = -u · ∇u + f × u + 1/ρ^2 (∂p / ∂x)
∂v / ∂t = -v · ∇v + f × v + 1/ρ^2 (∂p / ∂y)
ρ∂w / ∂t = -w · ∇w + g(θz + ϕ) + Q
∂θ / ∂t = -u · ∇θ + 1/T^2 (Dθ/Dt)
∂q / ∂t = -u · ∇q + 1/T^2 (Dq/Dt)
∂ψ / ∂t = -u · ∇ψ
ψ = ρw
ϕ = g(θz + ϕ)

### Equations:

===== File: conv_water.F90 =====

### Variables:

### Equations:
$TI = \frac{LS_{frac}LS_{icwmr} + CU_{frac}CU_{icwmr}}{\max(Frac_{limit}, Tot_0_{frac})}$
$LS_{frac}$ (Large-scale cloud fraction)
$LS_{icwmr}$ (Large-scale in-cloud water)
$CU_{frac}$ (Cumulus cloud fraction)
$CU_{icwmr}$ (Cumulus in-cloud water)
$Tot_0_{frac}$ (Total large-scale and cumulus cloud fraction)
$Frac_{limit}$ (Fraction limit)
$\max(Frac_{limit}, Tot_0_{frac})$ (Maximum of fraction limit and total large-scale and cumulus cloud fraction)
$totg_liq(i,k)$
$k$ (Grid-box index in the z-direction)
$totg_liq(i,k)$ (Total grid-box average in-cloud liquid)

### Equations:
VariableName (Short Notation)
Convective cloud water (cu_icwmr)
Large-scale cloud fraction (ls_frac)
Total large-scale and convective cloud fraction (tot0_frac)
Effective mean in-cloud total ice/liquid (tot_icwmr)
Insignificant condensate in large scale clouds flag (ls_icwmr < 100._r8*ic_limit .and. pergro_mods)

### Equations:
$\frac{cu_{icwmr}}{\max(frac\_limit, cu0\_frac)} = \frac{(sh0\_frac*sh\_icwmr(i,k) + dp0\_frac*dp\_icwmr(i,k))}{\max(frac\_limit,cu0\_frac)}$
$\alpha = -1.66_r8*kabs*state%pdel(i,k)/gravit*1000.0_r8$
$cu_{icwmr} = \begin{cases} (sh0\_frac*sh\_icwmr(i,k) + dp0\_frac*dp\_icwmr(i,k))/\max(frac\_limit,cu0\_frac)& ext{ for area weighted arithmetic average }\\ log((sh0\_frac*sh\_icwmr(i,k)+dp0\_frac*dp\_icwmr(i,k)))/\alpha & ext{ for logarithmic average } \end{cases}$
$tot_{icwmr} = \begin{cases} (ls\_frac*ls\_icwmr + cu0\_frac*cu\_icwmr)/\max(frac\_limit,tot0\_frac)& ext{ for area weighted arithmetic average }\\ log((ls\_frac*exp(\alpha*ls\_icwmr)+cu0\_frac*exp(\alpha*cu\_icwmr)))/\alpha & ext{ for logarithmic average } \end{cases}$
$conv_{ice} = cu_{icwmr} * wrk1$
$conv_{liq} = cu_{icwmr} * (1 - wrk1)$
$tot_{ice}  = tot_{icwmr} * wrk1$
$tot_{liq}  = tot_{icwmr} * (1 - wrk1)$
$wrk1 = min(1._r8,max(0._r8, state%q(i,k,ixcldice)/(state%q(i,k,ixcldice)+state%q(i,k,ixcldliq)+1.e-36_r8)))$
$ls_frac  = ast(i,k) $
$ls_icwmr  = (state%q(i,k,ixcldliq) + state%q(i,k,ixcldice))/max(frac_limit,ls_frac)$
$tot0_frac = (ls_frac + cu0_frac)$
`icwmrsh_idx` (IWMRS)
`icwmrdp_idx` (IDWRD)
`fice_idx`    (ICEFIC)
`sh_frac_idx`  (SHFRAC)
`dp_frac_idx`  (DPFRAC)
`ast_idx`     (ASTFRAC)
`rei_idx`     (REI)
`sh_cldliq1_idx`   (SCLIQ1)
`sh_cldice1_idx`   (SCICE1)
`ixcldice`    (IXCLI)
`ixcldliq`    (IXCLO)
cu0_frac is the total convective cloud fraction.
sh0_frac and dp0_frac are the shallow and deep convective cloud fractions, respectively.
wrk1 is the ratio of in-cloud ice to total in-cloud condensate.
ls_frac is the large-scale cloud fraction for this grid-box.
ast(i,k) is the physical liquid+ice stratus cloud fraction.
ls_icwmr is the total in-cloud water for this grid-box.
state%q(i,k,ixcldliq) and state%q(i,k,ixcldice) are the liquid and ice mixing ratios.
kabsi is the effective absorption coefficient for ice.
rei(i,k) is the effective radius of ice.
kabs is the effective absorption coefficient.
kabsl and kabsi are the absorption coefficients for liquid and ice, respectively.
wrk1 is a weighting factor.
alpha is a parameter used in the emissivity calculation.
kabs is the effective absorption coefficient.
state%pdel(i,k) is the pressure at grid point (i, k).
gravit is the acceleration due to gravity.

kabsi (KABSIC)

alpha (ALPHA)
Convective in-cloud ice mixing ratio (CUCI) (conv_ice)
Effective mean in-cloud convective ice/liquid (deep+shallow) (wrk1)
Ice partitioning ratio (fice)
Large-scale water for this grid-box. (ls_icwmr)
Total IC ice (tot_ice)
Total GBA in-cloud liquid (totg_liq)
Total GBA in-cloud ice (totg_ice)
Effective mean in-cloud total ice/liquid (ls+convective) (wrk1)

### Equations:
$\displaystyle \text{Shallow convection gbx liq cld mixing ratio for COSP} = (\text{Convective in-cloud water})\times(1-\text{Ice partitioning ratio})\times(\text{Shallow convective cloud fraction})$ (sh_cldliq = sh_icwmr * (1-fice) * sh_frac)
$\displaystyle \text{Shallow convection gbx ice cld mixing ratio for COSP} = (\text{Convective in-cloud water})\times(\text{Ice partitioning ratio})\times(\text{Shallow convective cloud fraction})$ (sh_cldice = sh_icwmr * fice * sh_frac)
$\displaystyle \text{Total in-cloud liquid} = \frac{\text{Large-scale total in-cloud water} + (\text{Convective large-scale area fraction})\times(\text{Convective in-cloud liquid})}{\max(0,1-\text{Fraction limit})}$ (tot_liq)
$\displaystyle \text{Total in-cloud ice} = \frac{\text{Large-scale total in-cloud water} + (\text{Convective large-scale area fraction})\times(\text{Convective in-cloud ice})}{\max(0,1-\text{Fraction limit})}$ (tot_ice)
$\displaystyle \text{Convective in-cloud liquid} = (\text{Shallow convective cloud fraction})\times(\text{Shallow convective in-cloud liquid}) + (\text{Deep convective cloud fraction})\times(\text{Deep convective in-cloud liquid})$ (conv_liq)
$\displaystyle \text{Convective in-cloud ice} = (\text{Shallow convective cloud fraction})\times(\text{Shallow convective in-cloud ice}) + (\text{Deep convective cloud fraction})\times(\text{Deep convective in-cloud ice})$ (conv_ice)
$\displaystyle \text{Effective mean in-cloud total liquid} = \frac{\exp(\alpha \cdot \text{In-cloud total liquid})}{\alpha}$ (tot_liq)
$\displaystyle \text{Effective mean in-cloud total ice} = \frac{\exp(\alpha \cdot \text{In-cloud total ice})}{\alpha}$ (tot_ice)
**r8** (Real 64-bit floating-point number)
**pcols** (Grid box number of columns)
**pver** (Grid box number of vertical levels)
**pverp** (Grid box number of vertical levels plus one)
**gravit** (Gravitational acceleration)
**latvap** (Latent heat of vaporization)
**latice** (Latent heat of fusion for ice)
**mwdry** (Dry air molecular weight)
**cpair** (Specific heat capacity of air at constant pressure)
**icwmrsh_idx** (Index of shallow convective cloud water in the physics buffer)
**icwmrdp_idx** (Index of deep convective cloud water in the physics buffer)
**fice_idx** (Index of ice fraction in the physics buffer)

### Equations:
$\frac{d}{dt} \int_{V} q_{c,i} dV = 0$ becomes $q_{c,i} = \bar{q}_{i} f_i$
$WMR_{liq,tot} = \sum WMR_{liq,conv} + LS_{icwmr}$ becomes $WMR_{liq,tot} = \frac{\sum (LS_{icwmr} + WMR_{liq,conv})}{1}$ where $\bar{q}_{i} f_i$ is the convective contribution of ice water mixing ratio.
$\frac{d}{dt} \int_{V} q_{c,l} dV = 0$ becomes $q_{c,l} = \bar{q}_{l} f_l$
$WMR_{ice,tot} = \sum WMR_{ice,conv} + LS_{icwmr}$ where $\bar{q}_{l} f_l$ is the convective contribution of liquid water mixing ratio.

The emissivity average for ice water is calculated using the following equation:

### Equations:
$\frac{\exp(-\alpha h)}{-\alpha + \exp(-\alpha h)}$
$kabsi = 0.005_r8 + 1._r8/rei(i,k)$
$kabsl = kabsl * ( 1._r8 - wrk1 ) + kabsi * wrk1$
$\alpha = -1.66_r8*kabs*state%pdel(i,k)/gravit*1000.0_r8$
$cu_icwmr = \frac{sh0_frac \times sh_icwmr(i,k) + dp0_frac*dp_icwmr(i,k)}{\max(frac_limit,cu0_frac)}$
$cu_icwmr = log\left(\frac{sh0_frac \times sh0+dp0_frac \times dp0}{\max(frac_limit,cu0_frac)}\right)/\alpha$
$ls_icwmr  = (state%q(i,k,ixcldliq) + state%q(i,k,ixcldice))/\max(frac_limit,ls_frac)$
$tot_icwmr = \frac{ls_frac*ls_icwmr + cu0_frac*cu_icwmr}{\max(frac_limit,tot0_frac)}$
$tot_icwmr = \log\left(\frac{ls_frac \times e^{\alpha \times ls_icwmr}+cu0_frac \times e^{\alpha \times cu_icwmr}}{\max(frac_limit,tot0_frac)}\right)/\alpha$

### Equations:

tsi_scaling (Total solar irradiance scaling)

### Equations:
Optical depth: $\tau = \int_0^\infty b(\nu) e^{-\frac{\Delta\nu}{2}} d\nu$, where $b(\nu)$ is the absorption coefficient.
Note that this equation is not explicitly written in the code, but it is used internally within the `k_dist%gas_optics` subroutine.
Gas optical depth: $\tau_{gas} = \sum_i C_i \int_0^\infty b_i(\nu) e^{-\frac{\Delta\nu}{2}} d\nu$, where $C_i$ are the gas concentrations and $b_i(\nu)$ are their respective absorption coefficients.
Transmittance: $\tau = e^{-\tau}$
Flux: $F = \int_0^\infty \phi(\nu) t(\nu) d\nu$, where $\phi(\nu)$ is the source function and $t(\nu)$ is the transmittance.
Source function: $\phi(\nu) = B(\nu) + J(\nu)$, where $B(\nu)$ is the Planck function and $J(\nu)$ is the incident radiative flux.
Clear-sky fluxes: $F_{clr} = F_{gas} + F_{aer}$, where $F_{gas}$ and $F_{aer}$ are the gas and aerosol contributions to the flux.
All-sky fluxes: $F_{all} = F_{clr} + \Delta F$, where $\Delta F$ is the cloud contribution to the flux.

### Variables:

### Equations:
$TSO_4 = \frac{10^{9}}{\rho} \cdot \left( Q_{SO4} + Q_{NH4NO3} \right)$
$\kappa_p = \exp\left( 84.6 - 24220 \cdot \frac{1}{T} - 6.1 \log\left( \frac{T}{298}\right) \right)$ (crystalline ammonium nitrate)
$\gamma = \left(\frac{\text{RH}}{1000 \left(\frac{1}{\text{RH}} + \frac{n}{1000}\right)}\right)^{\zeta^{-1}}$ (aqueous phase ammonium nitrate)
$SSM_{NH4NO3} = \left( \frac{80}{81} \cdot \nu_1 \cdot \left(\frac{1}{RH} - 1 \right)\right)^{z_1}$ (single solute molality for NH4NO3)
$\kappa_p = (\gamma \cdot SSM_{NH4NO3})^2 \exp\left(53.19 - 15850.62 \frac{1}{T} + 11.51 \log\left(\frac{T}{298}\right)\right)$ (equilibrium constant)
$TA = f_{NH3}$
$TN = HNO_3 + NH4NO_3$
$\boxed{NH4NO_3 = 0.5(TA + TN - \sqrt{(TA+TN)^2 - 4(TA \cdot TN - KP)})}$
$\boxed{XNH4NO_3 = 0.5(TA + TXHNO_3 - \sqrt{(TA+TXHNO_3)^2 - 4(TA \cdot TXHNO_3 - KP)})}$

### Equations:
$\frac{\partial \text{tracer}}{\partial t} = \dot{\omega}_{\text{tracer}}$
$q_{\text{tracer}} = \left(\frac{1}{e}\right)^x \cdot q_0$
$v_{\text{tracer}} = \frac{k_B T}{m_{\text{tracer}}}$
$\text{vmr}_{\text{tracer}} = 1$
$\text{mmr}_{\text{tracer}} = m_{\text{tracer}} \cdot \text{vmr}_{\text{tracer}}$
$x_{\text{tracer}} = \frac{\partial}{\partial t} (\log_e q_{\text{tracer}})$
$\frac{\partial \text{tracer}}{\partial t} = 0$, since tracer is prescribed.
$q_{\text{tracer}}$ is calculated using different units (e.g., 'kg/kg', 'mol/mol') and the molecular weight of dry air `mwdry`.
In subroutine `get_cnst_data_ptr`, data_q is calculated as $\text{mmr}_{\text{tracer}} = m_{\text{tracer}} \cdot \text{vmr}_{\text{tracer}}$.
Field (FLD)
Tracer (TRACER)
Data_q (DATA_Q)
State (STATE)

### Equations:
$\frac{\partial \text{FLD}}{\partial t} = 0$
$DATA\_Q = m_{\text{tracer}} \cdot \text{vmr}_{\text{tracer}}$
Field (FLD) - type: trfld
Tracer (TRACER) - type: real(r8)
Data_q (DATA_Q) - type: real(r8), allocatable, target, dimension(:,:,:,:)
State (STATE) - type: physics_state
In subroutine `get_cnst_data_ptr`, data_q is calculated as $\text{mmr}_{\text{tracer}} = m_{\text{tracer}} \cdot \text{vmr}_{\text{tracer}}$.

===== File: diff_face.F90 =====

### Variables:
N (Number of vertical layers)
dt (Time step in seconds)
cnpar (Implicitness parameter)
h(i) (Layer thickness at layer i, where i = 0 to N)
Bcup (Type of upper boundary condition)
Bcdw (Type of lower boundary condition)
Yup (Value of upper boundary condition)
Ydw (Value of lower boundary condition)
nuY(i) (Diffusivity of Y at layer i, where i = 0 to N)
Lsour(i) (Linear source term at layer i, where i = 0 to N)
Qsour(i) (Constant source term at layer i, where i = 0 to N)
Y(i) (Concentration of Y at layer i, where i = 0 to N)

### Equations:
c     = dt*( nuY(i+1) + nuY(i  ) )  / ( h(i)+h(i+1) ) / h(i+1)
a     = dt*( nuY(i  ) + nuY(i-1) )  / ( h(i)+h(i+1) ) / h(i  )
l     = dt*Lsour(i)
cu(i) =-cnpar*c
au(i) =-cnpar*a
bu(i) =  _ONE_ + cnpar*(a + c) - l
du(i) = (_ONE_ - (_ONE_-cnpar)*(a + c))*Y(i)                   &
a     = dt*( nuY(N-1) + nuY(N-2) )  / ( h(N-1)+h(N) ) / h(N-1)
l     = dt*Lsour(N-1)
au(N-1) =-cnpar*a
bu(N-1) =  _ONE_ + cnpar*a - l
du(N-1) = (_ONE_ - (_ONE_-cnpar)*a)*Y(N-1)                  &
au(N-1) = _ZERO_
bu(N-1) = _ONE_
du(N-1) = Yup

### Equations:
`byband_flux(icol, ilev, ibnd) =  spectral_flux(icol, ilev, band_lims(1, ibnd))`
`byband_flux(icol, ilev, ibnd) = byband_flux(icol, ilev, ibnd) + spectral_flux(icol, ilev, igpt)`
`byband_flux_net(icol, ilev, ibnd) = spectral_flux_dn(icol, ilev, igpt) - spectral_flux_up(icol, ilev, igpt)`
`byband_flux_net(icol, ilev, ibnd) = byband_flux_net(icol, ilev, ibnd) + spectral_flux_dn(icol, ilev, igpt) - spectral_flux_up(icol, ilev, igpt)`
`byband_flux_net(icol, ilev, ibnd) = byband_flux_dn(icol, ilev, ibnd) - byband_flux_up(icol, ilev, ibnd)`

### Equations:
Γ₃ = γfff(3.D0)
Γᵣ₁ = γfff(3.D0+b_rain)
Γᵣ₂ = (5.D0+b_rain)/2.D0
Γᵣ₃ = 4.D0+b_rain
Γₛ₁ = γfff(3.D0+b_snow)
Γₛ₂ = (5.D0+b_snow)/2.D0
Γₛ₃ = 4.D0+b_snow
Γᵍ₁ = γfff(3.D0+b_grau)
Γᵍ₂ = (5.D0+b_grau)/2.D0
Γᵍ₃ = 4.D0+b_grau
coef1 = 0.25D0 * π * nzeros * a_snow * gams1 * pratio/(π * rhos * nzeros/ρ) ** ((3.D0+b_snow)/4.D0)
coef2 = exp(0.025D0*(T₀ - 273.15D0))
accrsi(icrm,k) = coef1 * coef2 * esicoef
accrsc(icrm,k) = coef1 * esccoef
coefice(icrm,k) = coef2
coef1 = (LSUB/(T₀*RV)-1.D0)*LSUB/THERCO*RRR1*T₀
coef2 = RV T₀/(DELFQ RRR2 ESTI)
evaps1(icrm,k) = 0.65D0*4.D0*NZE/sqrt(π*RHO*NZE)/(coef1+coef2)/sqrt(ρ)
evaps2(icrm,k) = 0.49D0*4.D0*NZE*GAMS2*sqrt(A_S/(MUELQ RRR1))/(π*RHO*NZE)**((5.D0+b_snow)/8.D0) / (coef1+coef2) * ρ**((1.D0+b_snow)/8.D0)*sqrt(pratio)
coef1 = 0.25D0*π*NZG*A_GRAU*GAMG1*pratio/(π*RHOG*NZG/ρ)**((3.D0+b_grau)/4.D0)
accrgi(icrm,k) = coef1 * coef2 * EGICOEF
accrgc(icrm,k) = coef1 * EGCCOEF
coef1 = (LSUB/(T₀*RV)-1.D0)*LSUB/THERCO*RRR1*T₀
coef2 = RV T₀/(DELFQ RRR2 ESTI)
evapg1(icrm,k) = 0.65D0*4.D0*NZG/sqrt(π*RHOG*NZG)/(coef1+coef2)/sqrt(ρ)
evapg2(icrm,k) = 0.49D0*4.D0*NZG*GAMG2*sqrt(A_GRAU/(MUELQ RRR1))/(π*RHOG*NZG)**((5.D0+b_grau)/8.D0) / (coef1+coef2) * ρ**((1.D0+b_grau)/8.D0)*sqrt(pratio)
accrrc(icrm,k) = 0.25D0 * π * NZR * A_RAIN * GAMR1 * PRATIO/(π*RHOR*NZR/ρ)**((3.D0+b_rain)/4.D0)* ERC CO EF
coef1 = (LCOND/(T₀*RV)-1.D0)*LCOND/THERCO*RRR1*T₀
coef2 = RV T₀/(DELFQ RRR2 ESTW)
evapr1(icrm,k) = 0.78D0*2.D0*π*NZR/sqrt(π*RHOR*NZR)/(coef1+coef2)/sqrt(ρ)
evapr2(icrm,k) = 0.31D0*2.D0*π*NZR*GAMR2*0.89D0*sqrt(A_RAIN/(MUELQ RRR1))/(π*RHOR*NZR)**((5.D0+b_rain)/8.D0) / (coef1+coef2) * ρ**((1.D0+b_rain)/8.D0)*sqrt(pratio)

===== File: Test_TestResult.F90 =====

### Variables:
`runCount` (RC)
`failureCount` (FC)

### Equations:
RC = 0 → Result: Test result has not been run.
FC = 0 → Result: No failures have occurred.
FC = FC + 1 → Result: A failure has occurred.
RC = RC + 1 → Result: The test has been run again.
addFailure() was called → Result: Listener received a notification of a failure.
startTest() was called → Result: Listener received a notification of the test starting.
endTest() was called → Result: Listener received a notification of the test ending.

===== File: SHT_example.f90 =====

### Variables:
`l` (L)
`m` (M)
`lm` (LM)
`shtns` (SHTNS)
`cosTheta` (COSΘ)
`sinTheta` (SINΘ)
`pi` (Π)
`Sh` (SH)
`Slm` (SLM)
`ShT` (SHT)

### Equations:
The spherical harmonics can be represented by the equation: $Y_{lm}(\theta,\phi) = (-1)^m \sqrt{\frac{(2l+1)}{4\pi}\frac{(l-m)!}{(l+m)!}} e^{im\phi} P_l^m(\cos\theta)$ where $P_l^m(\cos\theta)$ are the associated Legendre polynomials. However, this equation is not directly found in the given Fortran code.
The given Fortran code uses the `shtns_lmidx`, `shtns_lm2l` and `shtns_lm2m` functions to calculate the lm index from the (l,m) pair. Since these functions are not provided, we cannot extract an equation for them. However, based on their names, they can be assumed to implement the equations: $lm = l + m(l+1)$, $l = \sqrt{lm} - 0.5$, and $m = lm - l^2$ respectively.
The `SH_to_spat` function is used to convert spherical harmonics to spatial data. The equation for this conversion can be assumed to be: $S(\theta_i) = \sum_{l=0}^{Lmax} \sum_{m=-l}^{l} Y_{lm}(\theta,\phi) S(l,m)$ where $Lmax$ is the maximum degree, and $\theta_i$ are the grid points.
The `spat_to_SH` function is used to convert spatial data back to spherical harmonics. The equation for this conversion can be assumed to be: $S(l,m) = \frac{1}{N} \sum_{i=1}^{N} S(\theta_i) Y^*_{lm}(\theta,\phi)$ where $N$ is the number of grid points, and $Y^*_{lm}$ are the complex conjugates of the spherical harmonics.
The `SH_to_spat_ml` function is used to convert the Legendre components (m=0) of the spherical harmonics to spatial data. The equation for this conversion can be assumed to be: $S(\theta_i) = P_l^m(\cos\theta) S(l,m)$ where $P_l^m$ are the associated Legendre polynomials.
The `Sh(1,:)` is printed as $\sqrt{\frac{3}{4\pi}} \cos\theta$, which can be written as an equation: $S(\theta_i) = \sqrt{\frac{3}{4\pi}} \cos\theta$

$m_w_dry_air = 28.966 kg/kmole$
$m_w_water_vapor = 18.016 kg/kmole$
$R_D = R_G / m_w_dry_air = 287.058 J/K/kg$
$R_W = R_G / m_w_water_vapor = 461.495 J/K/kg$
$z_VIR = \frac{R_W}{R_D} - 1.0$
$\kappa_{VK} = 0.4$
$P_{STP} = 101325 pascals$
$PDB = ratio of 13C/12C in Pee Dee Belemnite$
$T_{trip} = 273.16 K$
$T_{freezing} = 273.15 K$
$T_{freezing\_salt\_water} = T_{freezing} - 1.8 K$
$z_{surf\_layer\_depth} = 3 m$
$\rho_dry_air = \frac{P_{STP}}{(R_D \cdot T_{freezing})} = \frac{101325}{(287.058 \cdot 273.15)} kg/m^3$
$\rho_fresh_water = 1.000e3 kg/m^3$
$\rho_salt_water = 1.026e3 kg/m^3$
$\rho_ice = 0.917e3 kg/m^3$
$c_p_dry_air = 1.00464e3 J/kg/K$
$c_p_water_vapor = 1.810e3 J/kg/K$
$c_p_{water\_vapor} = c_p_{water\_vapor} / c_p_dry_air - 1.0$
$c_p_fresh_water = 4.188e3 J/kg/K$
$c_p_salt_water = 3.996e3 J/kg/K$
$c_p_ice = 2.11727e3 J/kg/K$
$L_{ice} = 3.337e5 J/kg$
$L_{evaporation} = 2.501e6 J/kg$
$L_{sublimation} = L_{ice} + L_{evaporation} = 3.33857e5 J/kg$
$\kappa_{heat\_land\_ice} = \frac{\kappa}{(\rho_{ice} \cdot c_p_{ice})} = \frac{2.1 W/m/K}{(0.917e3 kg/m^3 \cdot 2.11727e3 J/kg/K)}$
$T_0 = 6.22e-2 C$
$\Delta T_f = -7.43e-8 C Pa^{-1}$
$\Delta T_s = -5.63e-2 C PSU^{-1}$
$\Delta T_{fs} = \Delta T_{f \cdot s} = -1.74e-10 C PSU^{-1} Pa^{-1}$
$SAL_{ref\_ocn} = 34.7 psu$
$SAL_{ref\_ice} = 4.0 psu$
$SPVAL_{tolerance\_min} = 0.99 \cdot SPVAL = 9.99e29 R8$
$SPVAL_{tolerance\_max} = 1.01 \cdot SPVAL = 1.01e30 R8$
$SPVAL_{aerosol\_deposition} = 1.e29 R8$
$VSMOW_{18O} = 2005.2e-6$
$VSMOW_{17O} = 379.e-6$
$VSMOW_{16O} = 0.997628$
$VSMOW_D = 155.76e-6$
$VSMOW_T = 1.85e-6$
$VSMOW_H = 0.99984426$
$RSTD\_H2ODEV = 1 R8$

### Variables:

`groupname` (grnm)
`file_id` (filid)
`group_id` (groid)

### Equations:
`h5open_f(error)` initializes FORTRAN interface.
`h5fcreate_f(filename, H5F_ACC_TRUNC_F, file_id, error)` creates a new file with default properties.
This can be represented as:
`file_id = h5fcreate_f(filename, H5F_ACC_TRUNC_F, file_id, error)`
`h5gcreate_f(file_id, groupname, group_id, error)` creates a group named `/MyGroup` in the file.
This can be represented as:
`group_id = h5gcreate_f(file_id, groupname, group_id, error)`
`h5gclose_f(group_id, error)` closes the group.
`h5fclose_f(file_id, error)` terminates access to the file.
`h5close_f(error)` closes FORTRAN interface.

max(wx)^2 / (wx'^2 * x^2) = max( (<w' θ_l>)^2 / (σ_w^2 σ_θl^2 + ε), (<u w>)^2 / (σ_u^2 σ_w^2 + ε), (<v w>)^2 / (σ_v^2 σ_w^2 + ε) )

===== File: preserve_mean.f90 =====

### Variables:
`nlev` (NLEV)
`nxo` (NXO)
`nyo` (NYO)
`ntime` (NTIME)
`arr` (ARR, dimensioned as NXO x NYO x NLEV x NTIME)
`Matrix` (MATRIX, dimensioned as 12 x 12)
`pivot` (PIVOT, dimensioned as 12)
`vec` (VEC, dimensioned as 12)

### Equations:
`Len(row) = 31.0`, where `row` is an integer from 1 to 12 (LEN)
`Matrix(row, colm) = Len(row) / (4*(Len(col)+Len(colm)))`, where `colm` equals `row - 1` or `12` when `colm` equals `0` (MATRIX(row,colm))
`Matrix(row, colp) = Len(row) / (4*(Len(colp)+Len(row )))`, where `colp` equals `row + 1` or `1` when `colp` equals `13` (MATRIX(row,colp))
`Matrix(row, col) = 1.0 - Matrix(row,colm) - Matrix(row,colp)`, where `col` equals `row` (MATRIX(row,col ))
`call DGETRF (12, 12, Matrix, 12, pivot, info)`, which is a LAPACK function for factorization and does not have an explicit equation (DGETRF)
`info = -1 + (Matrix(kk,ll) == 0.0)` in the implicit loop over `ll` from `2` to `12` within the LAPACK function, however, it's not possible to extract a mathematical equation for this as it uses an index and an array element directly in a condition (this information is provided here but it does not follow the requested format)
`call DGETRS ('N', 12, 1, Matrix, 12, pivot, vec, 12, info)` is another LAPACK function for solving and again there are no explicit equations.

===== File: shr_RandNum_mod.F90 =====

### Variables:
`i` (Index)
`j` (Index)
`nstream` (Number of streams)
`nrandom` (Number of random numbers)
`seed` (Seed value, dimension: 1 or 2)
`self` (Class instance of ShrRandGen)
`array` (Array for storing random numbers, dimension: 2)
`rand_gen` (Instance of ShrIntrinsicRandGen, ShrKissRandGen, ShrF95MtRandGen, ShrDsfmtRandGen, or ShrMklMtRandGen)
`err` (Error code)

### Equations:
`i = 1`  ... (Loop counter initialization)
`do i = 1, size(self%seed, 1)`  ... (Loop over seed values)
`call random_seed(put=self%seed(i,:))`  ... (Set seed value using Fortran's intrinsic function)
`call random_number(array(i,:))`  ... (Generate random numbers using Fortran's intrinsic function)
`call random_seed(get=self%seed(i,:))`  ... (Retrieve current seed value using Fortran's intrinsic function)
`allocate(rand_gen%seed(size(seed, 1),4))`  ... (Allocate memory for seed array)
`rand_gen%seed = seed`  ... (Assign seed values to seed array)
`do i = 1, size(array, 2)`  ... (Loop over columns of random number array)
`call kissvec(self%seed(:,1), self%seed(:,2), self%seed(:,3), &`
`self%seed(:,4), array(:,i), nstream)`  ... (Generate random numbers using KISS algorithm)
`allocate(rand_gen%wrapped(size(seed)))`  ... (Allocate memory for wrapped objects)
`do i = 1, size(seed)`  ... (Loop over seed values)
`rand_gen%wrapped(i) = new_RandomNumberSequence(seed=seed(i))`  ... (Create wrapped object using Mersenne Twister algorithm)
`nstream = size(self%wrapped)`  ... (Determine number of streams)
`do i = 1, size(array, 2)`  ... (Loop over columns of random number array)
`do j = 1, nstream`  ... (Loop over streams)
`array(j,i) = getRandomReal(self%wrapped(j))`  ... (Generate random numbers using Mersenne Twister algorithm)
`allocate(rand_gen%wrapped(size(seed)))`  ... (Allocate memory for wrapped objects)
`do i = 1, size(seed)`  ... (Loop over seed values)
`call dSFMT_init(seed(i), cache_size, rand_gen%wrapped(i))`  ... (Initialize wrapped object using SIMD Fast Mersenne Twister algorithm)
`nrandom = size(array, 2)`  ... (Determine number of random numbers)
`do i = 1, size(self%wrapped)`  ... (Loop over wrapped objects)
`call get_rand_arr_close_open(self%wrapped(i), array(i,:), nrandom)`  ... (Generate random numbers using SIMD Fast Mersenne Twister algorithm)
`allocate(rand_gen%wrapped(size(seed)))`  ... (Allocate memory for wrapped objects)
`do i = 1, size(seed)`  ... (Loop over seed values)
`err = vslnewstream(rand_gen%wrapped(i), VSL_BRNG_SFMT19937, seed=seed(i))`  ... (Create wrapped object using Intel MKL Mersenne Twister algorithm)
`range_start = 0.0_r8`  ... (Define lower bound of uniform distribution)
`range_end = 1.0_r8`  ... (Define upper bound of uniform distribution)
`nrandom = size(array, 2)`  ... (Determine number of random numbers)
`do i = 1, size(self%wrapped)`  ... (Loop over wrapped objects)
`err = vdrnguniform(VSL_RNG_METHOD_UNIFORM_STD, self%wrapped(i), &`
`nrandom, array(i,:), range_start, range_end)`  ... (Generate random numbers using uniform distribution)

===== File: courlim.F90 =====

### Variables:
`k` (index to Model interfaces)
`lat` (latitude index)
`plev` (number of vertical levels in the model)
`plat` (number of latitudinal grid points)
`vmax2d` (max. wind at each level, latitude - 2D array)
`vmax2dt` (max. truncated wind at each level, latitude - 2D array)
`vcour` (maximum Courant number in slice - 2D array)
`nstep` (current timestep number)
`cnfac` (Courant number factor)
`kmxhdc` (upper limit for the Courant number check)
`cnlim` (Courant number limit)
`nmaxhd` (maximum horizontal wavenumber)
`psurf` (surface pressure - 1D array)
`stq` (specific humidity - 1D array)
`rmsz` (root mean square of vorticity - 1D array)
`rmsd` (root mean square of divergence - 1D array)
`rmst` (root mean square of temperature - 1D array)
`cnmax` (maximum Courant number for each level)
`vcourmax` (max. courant no. in the vertical wind field)

### Equations:
$\sqrt{\text{maxval}\left(v_{\text{max}2d}(k, :) \right)} = \text{vmax1d}(k)$
$\sqrt{\text{maxval}\left(v_{\text{max}2dt}(k, :) \right)} = \text{vmax1dt}(k)$
$\text{vcourmax} = \text{maxval}\left(\text{vcour}(2:, :) \right)$
$\text{cn} = \text{vmax1dt}(k) \cdot \text{cnfac}$ (estimate of truncated Courant number)
$\text{cnmax} = \text{max}\left(\text{cn}, \text{cnmax} \right)$
$\text{nindex}(k) = \int\left(\frac{\text{cnlim}}{\text{cn}} + 1_r8 \right)_r8$
$\text{psurfsum} = \text{psurfsum} + \text{psurf}(lat)$
$\text{stqsum} = \text{stqsum} + \text{stq}(lat)$
$\text{rmszsum} = \text{rmszsum} + \text{rmsz}(lat)$
$\text{rmsdsum} = \text{rmsdsum} + \text{rmsd}(lat)$
$\text{rmstsum} = \text{rmstsum} + \text{rmst}(lat)$
$\text{stps} = 0.5_r8 \cdot \text{psurfsum}$
$\text{stqf} = 0.5_r8 \cdot rga \cdot \text{stqsum}$
$\text{rmszf} = \sqrt{\frac{1}{2} \cdot \text{rmszsum}}$
$\text{rmsdf} = \sqrt{\frac{1}{2} \cdot \text{rmsdsum}}$
$\text{rmstf} = \sqrt{\frac{1}{2} \cdot \text{rmstsum}}$

### Equations:
$\sigma = \frac{\gamma_{2}}{\gamma_{1}c_{X}}\left(n_{a_X}\cdot a_{X}\cdot \gamma_{2}\right)^{d_{X}/(b_{X}-n_{b_X})}$
$\xi = d_{X}/(b_{X}+n_{b_X}-n_{bx} + 1)$
$one\_over\_xip1 = 1/(\xi + 1)$
$\gamma_{4,3,2} = \frac{0.5\cdot\Gamma_{4}}{\Gamma_{3}}$
$\delta = \alpha_{X} + b_{X}+ d_{X}- n_{b_X}+ 1$
$rho = p/(287.05\cdot T)$
$mxratio = \left(flux\cdot\left(\frac{rho}{\rho_0}\right)^g_x\cdot\sigma\right)^{1/\xi}$
$reff = \Gamma_{4,3,2}/\Lambda$
$\Lambda = (a_{X}c_{X}\cdot\left(\frac{\rho_0}{r}\right)^g_X\cdot n_{a_X}\cdot \gamma_{1})^{1/delta}/flux$

### Variables:

$R_i$ (an, ri)
$i$ (i)

### Equations:
$c'_{\mu}=c_{\mu} f(R_f)=c_{\mu} \frac{1}{P_r^0}(1-R_f)^{1/2}$
$R_f=\frac{c'_{\mu}}{c_{\mu}}R_i$
$(1-R_f)=[(\tilde R_i^2+1)^{1/2}-\tilde R_i]^2$
$\tilde R_i=\frac{0.5}{P_r^0} R_i$
$R_f=\frac{\mbox{const}}{P_r^0}(1-R_f)^{1/2}$
$f(R_f)$ is assumed to lay between the values 0.18 and 2.0

### Equations:
`hk = hksat * (1 - (imped ** (-bsw * s)))`  $\boxed{h_k = h_{k sat} \left( 1 - \left( i m p e d \right)^{-b_s w s}\right)}$
`smpsat = smp` is not an equation, but `smp = (1 / ((imped ** (-bsw)) * (1 - (hksat / hk))))^(1 / bsw)`  $\boxed{s m p = \left(\frac{1}{\left(i m p e d^{-b_s w}\right)\left(1-\frac{h_{k sat}}{h_k}\right)}\right)^{\frac{1}{b_s w}}}$
`s_target = (1 / ((imped ** (-bsw)) * (1 - (hksat / hk))))^(1 / bsw)`  $\boxed{s_{t a r g e t} = \left(\frac{1}{\left(i m p e d^{-b_s w}\right)\left(1-\frac{h_{k sat}}{h_k}\right)}\right)^{\frac{1}{b_s w}}}$
`dhkds` is not an equation in the code, but `d[hk]/ds = - (hksat * bsw * (imped ** (-bsw - 1)) / ((imped ** (-bsw)) ^2))`  $\boxed{\frac{d\left(h_k\right)}{d s}=- \frac{b_s w h_{k sat}}{\left(i m p e d^{-b_s w}\right)^2}\left(i m p e d^{-b_s w-1}\right)}$

### Equations:
$res = MINVAL(variable)$
Res = min(var_1, var_2,...,var_n)
$local\_sum=MINVAL(variable)$
Sum_{i}^{n} var_i  = min(var_1, var_2,...,var_n)
$call MPI_Allreduce(local_sum,res,1,MPIreal_t, MPI_MIN, par\%comm,ierr)$
Res = Allmin (Sum_{i}^{n} var_i )
$local\_sum=MAXVAL(variable)$
Sum_{i}^{n} var_i  = max(var_1, var_2,...,var_n)
$res = MAXVAL(variable)$
Res = allmax (Sum_{i}^{n} var_i )
$call MPI_Allreduce(local_sum,res,1,MPIreal_t, MPI_MAX, par\%comm,ierr)$
Res = Allmax (Sum_{i}^{n} var_i )
$local\_sum=SUM(variable)$
Sum_{i}^{n} var_i
$res = SUM(variable)$
Res = allsum (Sum_{i}^{n} var_i )
$call MPI_Allreduce(local_sum,res,1,MPIreal_t, MPI_SUM, par\%comm,ierr)$
Res = Allsum (Sum_{i}^{n} var_i )

### Variables:

`remainder` (remaining elements)

`kount` (count values for each dimension)

### Equations:
$\boxed{\text{minblocksize} = \frac{\text{minbytes}}{\text{basesize}}}$
minblocksize is the minimum block size, minbytes is the minimum number of contiguous blocks in bytes to put on a task, and basesize is the base size.
$\boxed{\text{use_io_procs} = \max(1, \lfloor\frac{\text{pgdims}}{\text{minblocksize}} + 0.5\rfloor)}$
use_io_procs is the number of tasks used, pgdims is the product of global dimensions, and minblocksize is the minimum block size.
$\boxed{\text{kount} = \frac{\text{gdim}}{\text{ioprocs}}}$
kount is the count values for each dimension, gdim is the global dimension, and ioprocs is the number of tasks in current group.
$\boxed{\text{start} = \lfloor\frac{\text{kount}\cdot\text{irank}}{\text{ioprocs}} + 1 \rfloor}$
start is the start values for each dimension, kount is the count values for each dimension, irank is the task rank in current group, and ioprocs is the number of tasks in current group.
$\boxed{\text{kount} = \lfloor\frac{\text{gdim} - \text{start}}{\text{ioprocs}} + 1 \rfloor}$
kount is the count values for each dimension, gdim is the global dimension, start is the start values for each dimension, and ioprocs is the number of tasks in current group.
$\boxed{\text{remainder} = \text{gdim} - (\lfloor\frac{\text{gdim}-\text{start}}{\text{ioprocs}} + 1\rfloor)\cdot\text{ioprocs}}$
remainder is the remaining elements, gdim is the global dimension, start is the start values for each dimension, and ioprocs is the number of tasks in current group.

### Equations:
 $\frac{\partial p}{\partial k} = hvcoord\%hyam(k)hvcoord\%ps0 + hvcoord\%hybm(k)elem(ie)\%state\%ps_v(:,:,t1)$
$p(:,:,k) = \frac{\partial p}{\partial k}$ $(k = 1, nlev)$
$\frac{dpnh}{d_p_i} = \text{eos}$ (elevation of phase transition pressure)
$exner = 1 - \frac{p}{P_s}$
$\omega_p = exner\frac{\partial \phi}{\partial t}$
$R^* = R \left(\frac{p_0}{p}\right)^r$
$T_v = \frac{T R^* dp}{R g e^{L/v}}$
$pnh = p + T V_p$
$\omega_p = (1 - \alpha) \frac{\partial \phi}{\partial t}$
$p_{SCM} = p_0 + \Delta p$
$T_{SCM} = T_0 + \Delta T$
$Q_{SCM} = Q_0 + \Delta Q$

===== File: herzin.F90 =====

### Variables:
pkdim (N)
pf (N)
f (i, k, m)
fst (i, k, m)
fsb (i, k, m)
sig (k)
dsig (k)
sigdp (i, k)
kdp (i, k)
nlon
i (1:N)
k (1:K)
m (1:M)
dzk (1:D)
zt (1:D)
zb (1:D)
ht (1:N)
hb (1:N)
dht(1:N)
dhb(1:N)

### Equations:
$\frac{dp}{dz} = \frac{\sigma(k+1) - \sigma_d(i, k)}{d\sigma}$
$z_t = 1 - \left(\frac{\sigma_d(i, k) - \sigma(k)}{d\sigma}\right)$
$z_b = z_t - d\sigma \cdot \frac{\partial}{\partial z} \left(\sigma_d(i, k)\right) $
$h_t (i) = (3 - 2z_t) z_t^2$
$h_b (i) = (3 - 2z_b) z_b^2 $
$\frac{\partial h_t}{\partial d\sigma} = \frac{-z_t + 1}{d\sigma} \cdot (h_t)^2$
$\frac{\partial h_b}{\partial d\sigma} = \frac{z_b - 1}{d\sigma} \cdot (h_b)^2 $
$f_{dp}(i, k, m) = f(i, k_t, m) h_t (i) + fst (i, k_t, m) \frac{\partial h_t}{\partial d\sigma} + f(i, k_b, m) h_b (i) + fsb (i, k_b, m) \frac{\partial h_b}{\partial d\sigma}$

===== File: timertest.F90 =====

### Variables:

`n` (N/A, used within `namelist_buffer='junk data'`)
Although `n` isn't explicitly mentioned, it is used within a namelist that could represent some variable 'n'.
`timer%its_a_new_day()` (ID)
`timer%its_a_new_week()` (IW)
`timer%its_a_new_month()` (IM)
`timer%its_a_new_year()` (IY)

### Equations:

ID = [C mod 2 == 0]
IW = [C mod (7 * 48) == 0]

===== File: fortranlib_test_1_8.F90 =====

### Variables:
`majnum` (Major Number)
`minnum` (Minor Number)
`relnum` (Release Number)
`total_error` (Total Error Count)

### Equations:
Equation 1: `majnum = majnum` (no change operation, but we can represent it as an equation: `M_{major} = M_{major}`)
Equation 2: `minnum = minnum` (no change operation, but we can represent it as an equation: `M_{minor} = M_{minor}`)
Equation 3: `relnum = relnum` (no change operation, but we can represent it as an equation: `M_{release} = M_{release}`)
Equation 4: `total_error = total_error + 1` (incremental operation, but we can represent it as an equation: `T_E = T_E + \Delta T_E`)
Equation 5: `ret_total_error = 0` (initialization operation, but we can represent it as an equation: `R_T_E = 0`)
Equation 6: `ret_total_error = ret_total_error` (no change operation, but we can represent it as an equation: `R_T_E = R_T_E`)

===== File: crm_surface.F90 =====

### Variables:
$u_h0 (uh0)
$tau00 (ta00)
$tmp (tmpp)
$ug (ug)
$vg (vg)
$rho(icrm,1) (rhoicrm1)
$diag_ustar (dus)
$zeta (zt)
$x (xx)
$psi1 (psip1)
$lnz (lzn)
$klnz (klkn)
$c1 (c1p)
$zeta (zt)
$rlmo (rmlom)
$bflx(icrm) (bficrm)
$u(icrm,i+1,j,1) (uii11)
$v(icrm,i,j+YES3D,1) (viijp13)
$z0(icrm) (zoicrm)

### Equations:
$\boxed{\text{uhl}(\text{icrm}) = \text{uhl}(\text{icrm}) + d\text{tn}\cdot\text{utend}(\text{icrm}, 1)}$ (1)
$\boxed{\text{vhl}(\text{icrm}) = \text{vhl}(\text{icrm}) + d\text{tn}\cdot\text{vtend}(\text{icrm}, 1)}$ (2)
$u_h0 = \max(1.0D0, \sqrt{\left(\frac{0.5D0 \times (\text{u}(\text{icrm}, \text{i}+1, \text{j}, 1) + \text{ug})}{\text{uhl}(\text{icrm})}\right)^2 + \left(\frac{0.5D0 \times (\text{v}(\text{icrm}, \text{i}, \text{j}+3D, 1) + \text{vg})}{\text{vhl}(\text{icrm})}\right)^2})$ (3)
$\tau_{00} = \rho(\text{icrm}, 1) \cdot (\text{diag}_\text{ustar}(\text{z}(\text{icrm}, 1), \text{bflx}(\text{icrm}), \text{u_h0}, \text{z0}(\text{icrm})))^2$ (4)
$\text{fluxbu}(\text{icrm}, \text{i}, \text{j}) = -\frac{\left(\frac{0.5D0 \times (\text{u}(\text{icrm}, \text{i}+1, \text{j}, 1) + \text{ug})}{\text{uhl}(\text{icrm})}\right) - \text{uhl}(\text{icrm})}{\text{u_h0}} \cdot \tau_{00}$ (5)
$\text{fluxbv}(\text{icrm}, \text{i}, \text{j}) = -\frac{\left(\frac{0.5D0 \times (\text{v}(\text{icrm}, \text{i}, \text{j}+3D, 1) + \text{vg})}{\text{vhl}(\text{icrm})}\right) - \text{vhl}(\text{icrm})}{\text{u_h0}} \cdot \tau_{00}$ (6)
$\text{tmp} = \frac{\text{fluxbu}(\text{icrm}, \text{i}, \text{j})}{d\text{ble}(\text{nx}\cdot\text{ny})}$ (7)
$\boxed{\text{taux0}(\text{icrm}) = \text{taux0}(\text{icrm}) + \text{tmp}}$ (8)
$\boxed{\text{tauy0}(\text{icrm}) = \text{tauy0}(\text{icrm}) + \frac{\text{fluxbv}(\text{icrm}, \text{i}, \text{j})}{d\text{ble}(\text{nx}\cdot\text{ny})}}$ (9)
$\boxed{\text{klnz} = \frac{\text{vonk}}{\text{lzn}}}$ (10)
$u^\ast = \text{wnd}\cdot\text{klnz}$ (11)
$\text{if }(\text{bflx} \neq 0.0D0):$
$r_{LMO} = -\frac{\text{bflx}\cdot\text{vonk}}{u^\ast{}^3 + \varepsilon}$ (12)
$\zeta = \min(1.0, z\cdot r_{LMO})$ (13)
$u^\ast = \text{if }(\zeta > 0):$
$u^\ast = \frac{\text{vonk}\cdot\text{wnd}}{\text{lzn} + a_m\cdot\zeta}$
$\text{else:}$
$x = \sqrt{\sqrt{1 - b_m\cdot\zeta}}$ (14)
$\psi_1 = 2D0\log(1+ x) + \log(x^2 + 1) - 2D0\arctan(x) + c_{1p}$ (15)
$u^\ast = \frac{\text{vonk}\cdot\text{wnd}}{\text{lzn} - \psi_1}$
$\boxed{\text{diag}_\text{ustar} = u^\ast}$ (16)
$\ln(z/\text{z0}) = \max(0, \frac{\text{vonk}\cdot\text{wnd}}{u^\ast + \varepsilon} + \psi_1)$ (17)
$\boxed{\text{z0\_est} = z\cdot e^{-\ln(z/\text{z0})}}$ (18)

### Equations:
$\frac{1}{\Delta z_i} = zs(i,k) \approx \frac{1}{z_{i,k} - z_{i,k+1}}$
where $zs$ is the inverse of distance between levels and $z_{i,k}$ are the layer heights.
$z_{h,i} = z_{h,i+1} + \frac{\Delta z_i}{\rho_m(i)} (\rho_a(i) T)$
where $z_h$ are the height at lay interfaces, $\Delta z_i$ is the layer depth, $\rho_m$ is air density, and $\rho_a$ and $T$ are temperature.
$\ell_c = \frac{300\exp(1-z_h/P_{PBL})}{1+300\exp(1-z_h/P_{PBL})}$
where $l_c$ is the mixing length, $z_h$ is the height at lay interfaces, and $P_{PBL}$ is the PBL height.
$\ell_c = \frac{30 + 270 \exp (1 - z_h / h)} {1 + 300 \exp(1-z_h/P_{PBL})}$
where $l_c$ is the mixing length, $z_h$ is the height at lay interfaces, and $h$ is the PBL height.
$\ell = k_z z_h$
where $k_z$ is the Karman constant and $z_h$ are the height at layer interfaces.
$w_{sub,i,k} = \sqrt{wwqui_cloudy_cen(i,k)}$
where $w_{sub}$ is subgrid vertical velocity, and $wwqui_cloudy_cen$ is vert velocity variance in quiescent+cloudy class.
$\epsilon_{CRM} = w_{sub} / z_s$
where $\epsilon_{CRM}$ is the diffusivity, $w_{sub}$ is the subgrid vertical velocity, and $z_s$ are the inverse of distance between levels.
$\kappa_{v,h} = \epsilon_{CRM}$
where $\kappa_v$ is the eddy diffusivity, and $\epsilon_{CRM}$ is the diffusivity.
$C_{liq} = \frac{cldn(i,k) q_c}{\tilde{q}}$
where $C_{liq}$ is the cloud fraction, $cldn$ are the cloud fractions at current time step, and $\tilde{q}$ is the liquid water content.
$C_{old} = \frac{cldo(i,k) q_c}{\tilde{q}}$
where $C_{old}$ is the old cloud fraction, $cldo$ are the cloud fractions at previous time step, and $\tilde{q}$ is the liquid water content.
$Q_C = \frac{\tilde{q} C_{liq}}{C_{liq} + C_{old}}$
where $Q_c$ is the total liquid water content, $C_{liq}$ are the cloud fractions at current time step, and $C_{old}$ are the cloud fractions at previous time step.
$f_a = \frac{\pi r^2}{\sigma} e^{-r / R}$
where $f_a$ is the activation fraction, $\pi r^2$ is the cross-sectional area of a sphere, $\sigma$ is the standard deviation, and $R$ is the radius.

### Equations:
$\boxed{ua(i,k,j) = \frac{1}{2} (u(i,j,k) + u(i,j+1,k))}$, where $i=ifirstxy,...,ilastxy$, $j=jfirstxy,...,jlastxy-1$, and $k=1,...,km$.
$\boxed{veast(j,k) = v(ifirstxy,j,k)}$, where $j=jfirstxy,...,jlastxy$ and $k=1,...,km$.
$\boxed{va(i,k,j) = \frac{1}{2} (v(i,j,k) + v(i+1,j,k))}$, where $i=ifirstxy,...,ilastxy-1$ and $j=jfirstxy,...,jlastxy$, and $\boxed{va(ilastxy,k,j) = \frac{1}{2} (v(ilastxy,j,k) + veast(j,k))}$, where $j=jfirstxy,...,jlastxy$ and $k=1,...,km$.

### Equations:
$u_{\text{A}}(i, j=k)=\frac{1}{2}(u(i, j)+ u^{\prime}(i))$
$v_{\text{A}}(i, j)=\frac{1}{2}(v(i, j)+ v^{\prime}(i+1, j))$
$u^{a}_{P}=-\sum\limits^{m}_{1}\left(u^{g}_{i+imh,k,j}-u^{g}_{i,k,j}\right)\sin l_{i}$
$v^{a}_{i,k,j}=\frac{1}{im}\sum\limits^{m}_{1} \left(\left(u^{g}_{i+imh,k,j}-u^{g}_{i,k,j}\right)\sin l_{i} + \left(v^{g}_{i+imh,k,j}-v^{g}_{i,k,j}\right)\cos l_{i}\right)$

### Equations:
$u^{a}_{i,k,1} = -\frac{1}{im}\left( u^{v}_k \sin(i) + v^{v}_k \cos(i)\right)$
$v^{a}_{i,k,1} =  \frac{1}{im}\left( u^{v}_k \cos(i) - v^{v}_k \sin(i)\right)$
$u^{a}_{i,k,jm} = -\frac{1}{im}\left( u^{v}_k \sin(i) + v^{v}_k \cos(i)\right)$
$v^{a}_{i,k,jm} =  \frac{1}{im}\left( u^{v}_k \sin(i) - v^{v}_k \cos(i)\right)$

### Equations:
$\boxed{ua(i,k,j) = D0_5 * (u(i,j,k) + u(i,j+1,k))}$
This equation interpolates the U-Wind component from a grid with one fewer points in the j-direction to a grid with the original number of points.
$\boxed{va(i,k,j) = D0_5*(v(i,j,k) + v(i+1,j,k))}$
This equation interpolates the V-Wind component from a grid with one fewer points in the i-direction to a grid with the original number of points.
$\boxed{va(ilastxy,k,j) = D0_5*(v(ilastxy,j,k) + veast(j,k))}$
This equation combines the interpolated V-Wind component at the last point in the i-direction and the Eastward wind component to get the final value.

### Equations:
ua(i,k,j) = D0_5*(u(i,j,k) + u(i,j+1,k))
Equation in standard mathematical format: $ua_{i, k, j} = \frac{1}{2}(u_{i, j, k} + u_{i, j+1, k})$
va(i,k,j) = D0_5*(v(i,j,k) + v(i+1,j,k))
Equation in standard mathematical format: $va_{i, k, j} = \frac{1}{2}(v_{i, j, k} + v_{i+1, j, k})$
ua(i,k,jlastxy) = D0_5*(u(i,jlastxy,k) + unorth(i,k))
Equation in standard mathematical format: $ua_{i, k, \text{jlastxy}} = \frac{1}{2}( u_i(j\text{jlastxy},k) + u^{\text{unorth}}_i(k) )$
veast(j,k) = v(ifirstxy,j,k)
Equation in standard mathematical format: $veast_j(k) = v_{i\text{ifirstxy},j,k}$
ua(i,k,j) = D0_5*(u(i,j,k) + u(i,j+1,k))
Equation in standard mathematical format: $ua_i(k, j) = \frac{1}{2}(u_i(j, k) + u_i(j+1, k))$
veast(j,k) = v(ifirstxy,j,k)
Equation in standard mathematical format: $veast_j(k) = v_{i\text{ifirstxy},j,k}$
va(i,k,j) = D0_5*(v(i,j,k) + v(i+1,j,k))
Equation in standard mathematical format: $va_i(j, k) = \frac{1}{2}(v_i(j, k) + v_{i+1}(j, k))$
va(ilastxy,k,j) = D0_5*(v(ilastxy,j,k) + veast(j,k))
Equation in standard mathematical format: $va_{ilastxy}(j, k) = \frac{1}{2}(v_{ilastxy}(j, k) + veast(j, k))$

### Equations:
$\boxed{ua(i,k,j) = \frac{1}{2}\left(u(i,j,k)+u(i,j+1,k)\right)}$
Variables: ua, u, i, k, j
Description: Interpolation of U-wind in the East-West direction.
$\boxed{va(i,k,j) = \frac{1}{2}\left(v(i,j,k)+v(i+1,j,k)\right)}$
Variables: va, v, i, k, j
Description: Interpolation of V-wind in the North-South direction.
$\boxed{va(ilastxy,k,j) = \frac{1}{2}\left(v(ilastxy,j,k)+veast(j,k)\right)}$
Variables: va, v, veast, ilastxy, k, j
Description: Interpolation of V-wind at the boundary.
$\boxed{uva(i,k,m) = -ua(i,k,jm-1)*sinlon(i) + va(i,k,jm-1)*coslon(i)}$
Variables: uva, ua, v, sinlon, coslon, imh, ifirstxy, ilastxy
Description: Calculation of uva.
$\boxed{uva(i,k,m) =  ua(i,k,jm-1)*sinlon(i-imh) + va(i,k,jm-1)*coslon(i-imh)}$
Variables: uva, ua, v, sinlon, coslon, imh, ifirstxy, ilastxy
Description: Calculation of uva.

### Equations:
$\frac{\partial u}{\partial j} = \frac{u(i,j,k) + u(i,j+1,k)}{2}$

$\frac{\partial v}{\partial i} = \frac{v(i,j,k) + v(i+1,j,k)}{2}$
V-Wind (V)
Grid dimension in x-direction (im)
Grid dimension in y-direction (jm)
$u_{A-grid}(i, k, j) = \frac{1}{2} [ u_D-grid(i, j, k) + unorth(i, k) ]$
$u_{A-grid}$: U-Wind on A grid
$u_D-grid$:  U-Wind on D grid
$unorth$:    North component of U-Wind
$v_{A-grid}(i, k, j) = \frac{1}{2} [ v_D-grid(i, j, k) + veast(j, k) ]$
$v_{A-grid}$: V-Wind on A grid
$v_D-grid$:  V-Wind on D grid
$veast$:      East component of V-Wind
$uva(i,k,m) = \begin{cases}
a(i,k,2)\sin(\textit{lon}(i)) + va(i,k,2)\cos(\textit{lon}(i)), &\mbox{if } i \leq imh \\

### Equations:
$ua(i,k,j) = \frac{1}{2} \left( u(i,j,k) + u(i,j+1,k) \right)$
$va(i,k,j) = \frac{1}{2} \left( v(i,j,k) + v(i+1,j,k) \right)$ when j $\neq$ jlastxy
$va(ilastxy,k,j) = \frac{1}{2} \left( v(ilastxy,j,k) + veast(j,k) \right)$
$uva(i,k,1) = -ua(i,k,2)\sin(\ellon(i)) + va(i,k,2)\cos(\ellon(i))$ when $i \leq imh$
$uva(i,k,1) = ua(i,k,2)\sin(\ellon(i-imh)) - va(i,k,2)\cos(\ellon(i-imh))$ when $i > imh$
$uva(i,k,2) = -ua(i,k,2)\cos(\ellon(i)) + va(i,k,2)\sin(\ellon(i))$ when $i \leq imh$
$uva(i,k,2) = ua(i,k,2)\cos(\ellon(i-imh)) + va(i,k,2)\sin(\ellon(i-imh))$ when $i > imh$

===== File: quadraticMod.F90 =====

### Variables:
a (a)
b (b)
c (c)
r1 (r1)
r2 (r2)
q (q)

### Equations:
b² - 4ac = [b^2 - 4ac]
q = −0.5(b ± √(b² - 4ac)) = [-\frac{1}{2}(b \pm \sqrt{b^2 - 4ac})]
r1 = q/a = [\frac{-\frac{1}{2}(b \pm \sqrt{b^2 - 4ac})}{a}]
r2 = c/q (q ≠ 0) = [\frac{c}{-\frac{1}{2}(b \pm \sqrt{b^2 - 4ac})}]

SaltWaterDensity (SWD)

### Equations:
Bulk Richardson number: $\text{BR}_c = \frac{\left( g \Delta z_c \right)}{\left( \Delta T_{\text{ref}}^2 + \Delta S_{\text{ref}}^2 \right) / 1000 }$
Stratification parameter for double diffusion mixing: $R_\rho = \frac{ N_c^3 g \left( \frac{\Delta S}{\Delta z} \right)^2 }{ D_c^2 }$
Langmuir number: $\text{LN}_c = \frac{ a c_{\text{p}} T \left( 1 - e^{-h/r} \right) }{ \sqrt{g u_*^3 / f} } $
Simmons coefficient: $C_c = \frac{ \beta_2^{0.5} g D_c }{ (N_c + k_{\text{d}})^2 }$
Coriolis parameter: $f_c = 2 \Omega \sin(\phi)$

===== File: SoilForcType.F90 =====

### Variables:
`r8` (Double Precision)
`om_frac` (Organic Fraction)
`sand` (Sand Percentage)
`clay` (Clay Percentage)
`zsoi` (Soil Depth)
`bd` (Bulk Density)
`watsat` (Saturated Water Content)
`bsw` (Shape Parameter)
`sucsat` (Minimum Soil Suction)
`hksat` (Saturated Hydraulic Conductivity)
`imped` (Ice Impedance)
`xksat` (Effective Saturated Hydraulic Conductivity)
`om_watsat` (Organic Water Content)
`om_b` (Organic Bulk Density)
`om_sucsat` (Organic Minimum Soil Suction)
`om_hksat` (Organic Saturated Hydraulic Conductivity)
`perc_norm` (Percolation Normalization Factor)
`perc_frac` (Percolation Fraction)
`uncon_frac` (Unpercolating Fraction)
`uncon_hksat` (Unpercolating Effective Hydraulic Conductivity)
`hk` (Hydraulic Conductivity)
`dhkds` (Derivative of Hydraulic Conductivity with Respect to Saturation)
`smpsat` (Minimum Soil Suction Potential)
`smp` (Soil Suction Potential)
`dsmpds` (Derivative of Soil Suction Potential with Respect to Saturation)
`icefrac` (Ice Fraction)
`imped` (Ice Impedance)
`e_ice` (Exponent for Ice Impedance)
`s` (Relative Saturation)
`tfrz` (Freezing Temperature)
`hfus` (Latent Heat of Fusion)
`grav` (Gravitational Acceleration)
`temp` (Temperature)
`denh2o` (Density of Water)
`dzsoi` (Soil Thickness)

### Equations:
$\boxed{\text{om_frac} = \left(\frac{\text{cellorg}}{\text{organic_max}}\right)^2}$
$\boxed{\text{watsat} = \left(1 - \text{om_frac}\right) \cdot \text{watsat} + \text{om_watsat} \cdot \text{om_frac}}$
$\boxed{\text{bsw} = \left(1 - \text{om_frac}\right) \cdot \left(2.91 + 0.159 \cdot \text{clay}\right) + \text{om_frac} \cdot \text{om_b}}$
$\boxed{\text{sucsat} = \left(1 - \text{om_frac}\right) \cdot \text{sucsat} + \text{om_sucsat} \cdot \text{om_frac}}$
$\boxed{\text{xksat} = \text{smpsat}}$
$\boxed{\text{hksat} = \left(1 - \text{om_frac}\right) / \left(\frac{1}{\text{xksat}} + \left(1 - \text{perc_frac}\right) \cdot \frac{1}{\text{om_hksat}}\right)}$
$\boxed{\text{perc_norm} = \left(1 - \text{pcalpha}\right)^{-\text{pcbeta}}}$
$\boxed{\text{uncon_frac} = \left(1 - \text{om_frac}\right) + \left(1 - \text{perc_frac}\right) \cdot \text{om_frac}}$
$\boxed{\text{uncon_hksat} = \frac{\text{uncon_frac}}{\left(\frac{1}{\text{xksat}} + \left(1 - \text{perc_frac}\right) \cdot \frac{1}{\text{om_hksat}}\right)}}$
$\boxed{\text{hk} = \text{imped} \cdot \text{hksat} \cdot \text{s}^{2 \cdot \text{bsw} + 3}}$
$\boxed{\frac{\text{dhkds}}{\text{ds}} = \left(2 \cdot \text{bsw} + 3\right) \cdot \frac{\text{hk}}{\text{s}}}$
$\boxed{\text{smp} = -\text{smpsat} \cdot \text{s}^{-\text{bsw}}}$
$\boxed{\frac{\text{dsmpds}}{\text{ds}} = -\text{bsw} \cdot \frac{\text{smp}}{\text{s}}}$
$\boxed{\text{icefrac} = \frac{\text{h2osoi_vol} - \text{h2osoi_liqvol}}{\text{watsat}}}$
$\boxed{\text{imped} = 10^{-\text{e_ice} \cdot \text{icefrac}}}$
$\boxed{\text{s} = \max\left(\frac{\text{h2osoi_liqvol}}{\text{watsat}}, 1.e-3_r8\right)}$
$\boxed{\text{soilpsi} = \begin{cases}-\frac{\text{hfus} \cdot \left(\text{tfrz} - \text{temp}\right) / \left(\text{grav} \cdot \text{temp}\right)}{1000.0_r8} & \text{if } \text{temp} < \text{tfrz}, \\ 0_r8 & \text{otherwise}. \end{cases}}$

$\epsilon = cde \, l^2 \, C_{\mu 1} \, N_s^2$ ($\epsilon=cde\,l^2\,C_{\mu 1}\,N_s^2$)
$k = (c_\mu^0)^{-3} \, l^2 \, ( c_\mu M^2 - c'_\mu N^2 )$ ($K=(C_\mu^0)^{-3}l^2(c_\mu M^2-C'_{\mu }N^2)$)
$P = 0.5 \, C_{\mu 1} \, l^2 \, N_s^2$ ($P=0.5\,C_{\mu 1}\,l^2\,N_s^2$)
$G = cde \, l^2 \, C_{\mu 2} \, N_b^2$ ($G=cde\,l^2\,C_{\mu 2}\,N_b^2$)

===== File: aerosol_depvel.F90 =====

### Variables:
`rho` (ρ)
`t` (T)
`pmid` (p)
`fv` (friction velocity)
`ram1` (aerodynamical resistance)
`diam` (particle diameter)
`stk_crc` (correction to Stokes settling velocity)
`dns_aer` (diffusion coefficient of particles in air)
`vsc_dyn_atm` (dynamic viscosity of air)
`vsc_knm_atm` (kinematic viscosity of air)
`mfp_atm` (mean free path of air)
`slp_crc` (slip correction factor)
`rss_trb` (resistance to turbulent deposition)
`rss_lmn` (quasi-laminar layer resistance)
`tmp` (temporary variable)
`gravit` (acceleration due to gravity)
`rair` (air density)
`boltz` (Boltzmann constant)
`pi` (mathematical constant pi)

### Equations:
ρ = p / (r_a * T)  ![kg m^(-3)] air density
vsc_dyn_atm(i,k) = 1.72e-5_r8 * ((T/273.0_r8)**1.5_r8) * 393.0_r8 / (T+120.0_r8)  ![kg m s^(-2)] dynamic viscosity of air
mfp_atm(i,k) = 2.0_r8 * vsc_dyn_atm(i,k) / (p*sqrt(8.0_r8/(pi*r_a*T)))  ![m] mean free path of air
vsc_knm_atm(i,k) = vsc_dyn_atm(i,k) / ρ  ![m^2 s^(-1)] kinematic viscosity of air
slp_crc(i,k,m) = 1.0_r8 + 2.0_r8 * mfp_atm(i,k) * (1.257_r8+0.4_r8*exp(-1.1_r8*diam(i,k,m)/(2.0_r8*mfp_atm(i,k)))) / diam(i,k,m)  ![frc] slip correction factor
vlc_grv(i,k,m) = (1.0_r8/18.0_r8) * diam(i,k,m)**2 * dns_aer * gravit * slp_crc(i,k,m) / vsc_dyn_atm(i,k)  ![m s^(-1)] Stokes' settling velocity
vlc_grv(i,k,m) = vlc_grv(i,k,m) * stk_crc(m)  ![m s^(-1)] correction to Stokes settling velocity
stk_nbr = vlc_grv(i,k,m) * fv(i)**2 / (gravit*vsc_knm_atm(i,k))  ![frc] particle Stokes number
dff_aer = boltz * T * slp_crc(i,k,m) / (3.0_r8*pi*vsc_dyn_atm(i,k)*diam(i,k,m))  ![m^2 s^(-1)] Brownian diffusivity of particles
shm_nbr = vsc_knm_atm(i,k) / dff_aer  ![frc] Schmidt number
tmp = shm_nbr**shm_nbr_xpn + 10.0_r8**(-3.0_r8/stk_nbr)
rss_lmn(m) = 1.0_r8 / (tmp*fv(i))  ![s m^(-1)] quasi-laminar layer resistance
rss_trb = ram1(i) + rss_lmn(m) + ram1(i)*rss_lmn(m)*vlc_grv(i,k,m)  ![s m^(-1)] resistance to turbulent deposition
vlc_trb(i,m) = 1.0_r8 / rss_trb  ![m s^(-1)] turbulent deposition velocity

### Equations:
q_a = τ^(_ONE_/cos(θ))  (τ_q)
q_z = cos(θ) * Q_s  (q_z)
q_d = q_z * τ_q  (q_d)
q_l = [1 - α_o] * q_z - q_d  (q_l)
q_t = q_d + q_l  (q_t)
δ = (n-81)/N*2π  (δ)
sin(δ) = sin(φ_radians)*sin(β*sin(δ)) + cos(φ_radians)*cos(β*sin(δ))  (sin(δ))
δ_rad = arcsin(sin(δ))*180/π  (δ_rad)
q_s = q_t * [1 - 0.62 C + 0.0019 sin(δ)]  (q_s)

$\frac{V_{p,n}}{\rho_f} = \left( c_1 - r_{f,rac} \right) \cdot \left( M_{t,i,j} \cdot \frac{\rho_i}{\rho_f} + M_{s,i,j} \cdot \frac{\rho_s}{\rho_f} + F_{r,i,j} \cdot dt \right)$
$V_{p,n} = V_{p,n} \cdot e^{\left( p_{01} \cdot \frac{T_{melt} - T_{sfcn}}{T_{melt} - c_2} \right)}$
$\max\left(V_{p,n}, c_0\right)$
$A_{p,n} = \min\left(\sqrt{\max\left(V_{p,n} \cdot 1.25, c_0\right)}, c_1\right)$
$H_{p,n} = 0.8 \cdot A_{p,n}$
$\frac{A_{p,n}}{H_{p,n}} < 1$
$H_{p,n} < 0.9 \cdot H_i$
$V_{p,n} = H_{p,n} \cdot A_{p,n}$

### Equations:
agg = ∑(in_arr * Wgt) / (4.0_r8 * π), where Lon ∈ [1, NCols], Lch ∈ [BegChunk, EndChunk] (agg)
agg = ∑(XFld) / NGCOLS, where Cnt ∈ [1, NLCols] * Fld ∈ [1, Nflds] (agg)
Wgt(i) = wght_all(lchnk, ncols), where Lon ∈ [1, ncols], Lch ∈ [BegChunk, EndChunk] (Wgt)
XFld(Cnt,Fld) = in_arr(i,lchnk,fld)*wght(i), where Lon ∈ [1, ncols], Lch ∈ [BegChunk, EndChunk], Fld ∈ [1, Nflds] (XFld)
rel_diff(1,fld) = |agg - agg' | / max(abs(agg), abs(agg')), where Fld ∈ [1, Nflds], agg ∈ agg, agg' ∈ agg' (rel_diff)
mass_wet(i,c,m) = ∑[pdel(i,k)*q(i,k,m)]/gravit, where i ∈ [1,pcols], k ∈ [1,pver], m ∈ [1,pcnst], c ∈ [BegChunk, Endchunk] (mass_wet)

\overline{A} = \frac{\sum_{i=1}^{n}\sum_{k=begchunk}^{endchunk}w(i)\left(\sum_{j=1}^{pcols}A(i,k,f)\right)}{4\pi} \quad (1)
zmean = 0.5_r8\frac{\sum_{i=1}^n A(i,j,f)}{hdim2} \quad (2)

### Equations:

### Equations:
γ_k = ∑[i=1,cg%len] wts(i,ie)*z(i,k)*r(i,k)     ! eq(10)
δ_k = ∑[i=1,cg%len] wts(i,ie)*z(i,k)*s(i,k)     ! eq(11)
σ_k^2 = <z_k,r_k>^2 / (<b,b>)                ! eq(13)
γ_k = <z_k,r_k>                               ! eq(12)
δ_k = <z_k,s_k>
α_k = γ_k/δ_k                                 ! eq(14) (method 1 of Lapack note 56)
σ_k = δ_k - β * β * δ_(k-1)                   ! eq(12) (method 1 of Lapack note 56)
p_k = z_k + β * p_(k-1)                        !
v_k = s_k + β * v_(k-1)                        !
x_k = x_(k-1) + α*p_k                          !
r_k = r_(k-1) - α*v_k                          !

### Variables:
T (Temperature)
p (Surface atmospheric pressure in Pascals)
e (Vapor pressure in Pascals)

### Equations:
e = a0 + td*(a1 + td*(a2 + td*(a3 + td*(a4 + td*(a5 + td*(a6 + td*(a7 + td*a8)))))))
Equation in standard mathematical format: e =
d(e)/d(T) = b0 + td*(b1 + td*(b2 + td*(b3 + td*(b4 + td*(b5 + td*(b6 + td*(b7 + td*b8)))))))
Equation in standard mathematical format:
e = c0 + td*(c1 + td*(c2 + td*(c3 + td*(c4 + td*(c5 + td*(c6 + td*(c7 + td*c8))))))
Equation in standard mathematical format:
d(e)/d(T) = d0 + td*(d1 + td*(d2 + td*(d3 + td*(d4 + td*(d5 + td*(d6 + td*(d7 + td*d8)))))
Equation in standard mathematical format:
rho = es/(rwat*T)
Equation in standard mathematical format: ρ =
d(rho)/d(T) = (esdT/(rwat*T)) - rho/T
Equation in standard mathematical format:
qs = e * vp1
Equation in standard mathematical format: q_s =
qsdT = (de/dT) * vp2 * p

===== File: albedo_water.F90 =====

### Variables:
Method (M)
Zenith angle (θ)
Year day (yd)
Albedo over water (α_w)
Albedo according to Payne (α_payne)
Albedo according to Cogley (α_cogley)

### Equations:
α_w = 0, if M = CONST
α_payne(θ) = alb1(jab), if jab ≠ 20
α_payne(θ) = alb1(jab) + dzen*(alb1(jab+1)-alb1(jab)), if jab = 20
jab = .5*(90-θ)+1, if θ ≥ 74
jab = .23*(74-θ)+9, if 50 ≤ θ < 74
jab = .10*(50-θ)+15, if θ < 50
dza(jab) = za(jab)-θ
jab = .5*(90-θ)+1, if θ ≥ 74
jab = .23*(74-θ)+9, if 50 ≤ θ < 74
jab = .10*(50-θ)+15, if θ < 50
dza(jab) = za(jab)-θ
jab = min(max(((90-θ)/dz+1d0),1d0),10d0)
tab = min(max(floor(yd/dt)+1d0,1d0),12d0)
α_cogley(θ,yd) = (dmax1(x,0d0))
x = r1 + dti*(r2-r1)
alb1 is a table of albedo values for 0.7 transmittance.
za is a table of zenith angles in degrees.
dza is a table of differences between zenith angles.
a1 is a 2D table of albedo values according to Cogley (1979).
tim is a table of time values in days.
dt is the time step in days.

`T = (q**(t - 25/10)) if t >= TKFRZ else T = (q**(-25/10))*(f**(t-TKFRZ/10))`

`vmax_decomp_n` and `vmax_decomp_p` are not explicitly used in the code snippets provided, so they were excluded from this analysis. If you'd like, I can try to help with their equation if it's present elsewhere in the file.

`∂H/∂t = ∇⋅(K \* ∇H) + Q` (where `H` is specific enthalpy)

===== File: hycoef.F90 =====

### Variables:
`hyai` (Hybrid A Interface)
`hyam` (Hybrid A Midpoint)
`hybi` (Hybrid B Interface)
`hybm` (Hybrid B Midpoint)
`ps0` (Base State Surface Pressure)
`psr` (Reference Surface Pressure)
`nprlev` (Number of Pure Pressure Levels at Top)

### Equations:
`p(k) = hyai(k)*ps0 + hybi(k)*ps` (Hybrid Level Interface)
`p(k) = hyam(k)*ps0 + hybm(k)*ps` (Hybrid Level Midpoint)
`hybd(k) = hybi(k+1) - hybi(k)` (Difference in Hybrid B Across Layers)
`hypi(k) = hyai(k)*ps0 + hybi(k)*psr` (Reference State Interface Pressure)
`hypm(k) = hyam(k)*ps0 + hybm(k)*psr` (Reference State Midpoint Pressure)
`etamid(k) = hyam(k) + hybm(k)` (Hybrid Coordinate - Midpoints)
`hypd(k) = hypi(k+1) - hypi(k)` (Reference State Layer Thicknesses)
`hypo(k) = hypm(k)` (Reference State Pressure Layer Thicknesses)
`alev(k) = 0.01_r8*ps0*(hyam(k) + hybm(k))` (Level Values - Midpoints)
`ailev(k) = 0.01_r8*ps0*(hyai(k) + hybi(k))` (Interface Level Values)
Hyai (A_interface)
Hyam (A_midpoint)
Hybi (B_interface)
Hybm (B_midpoint)
Plev (Number of Levels)
Plevp (Number of Interfaces)
Ps0 (Base State Surface Pressure)
Psr (Reference Surface Pressure)
Etamid (Hybrid Coordinate - Midpoints)
Hypi (Reference State Interface Pressures)
Hypm (Reference State Midpoint Pressures)
Hypd (Reference State Layer Thicknesses)
Hybd (Difference  in b across layers)
Nprlev (Number of Pure Pressure Levels at Top)
Alev (Level Values - Midpoints)
Ailev (Interface Level Values)

### Equations:
p = a*p0 + b*ps
interfaces   p(k) = hyai(k)*ps0 + hybi(k)*ps
midpoints    p(k) = hyam(k)*ps0 + hybm(k)*ps
hybd     difference  in b (hybi) across layers
hypi    reference state interface pressures
hypm    reference state midpoint pressures
hypd    reference state layer thicknesses
alev = 0.01*ps0*(hyam + hybm)
ailev = 0.01*ps0*(hyai + hybi)

===== File: prune_trailing_numbers.F90 =====

### Variables:
`i` (subscript)
`inlen` (subscript)
`instr` (string)
`outstr` (string)

### Equations:
\( i = \text{inlen} \)  - Equation 1
\( \text{do while (instr(i:i) == "0" .or. instr(i:i) == "1" .or. ...)} \) can be transformed into a more standard equation but is better suited as an algorithm description: the loop condition can be seen as
\( i=i-1 \) - Equation 2 (in the body of the loop)
The key logic is that `i` continues to decrease as long as it points at any trailing numbers or decimal point in `instr`. We can transform this into a standard equation, but there's no specific variable assignment or relation outside the loop. However, we can write an expression for the condition of the loop which is equivalent to:
\( i_{\text{final}} = inlen - k \) - Relation 3

Function luavail()
Character(len=*),parameter :: myname_=myname//'::luavail'
Integer lu
Logical inuse
Integer ios
Do while (ios == 0 .and. inuse .and. lu <= MX_LU)
Lu = lu + 1
Inquire(unit=lu,opened=inuse,iostat=ios)
End do
If (ios /= 0) then:
$luavail=-1$
Else:
$luavail=lu$
Subroutine luflush(unit)
Character(len=*),parameter :: myname_=myname//'::luflush'
Integer ier
Integer lu
If (SYSIRIX64 .or. CPRNAG .or. SYSUNICOS) then:
$\text{call flush}\left(lu,ier\right)$
Else if (SYSAIX .or. CPRXLF) then:
$\text{call flush}_\left(lu\right)$
Else if (SYSLINUX .or. SYSOSF1 .or. SYSSUNOS .or. SYST3E .or. SYSUNIXSYSTEMV .or. SYSSUPERUX) then:
$\text{call flush}\left(lu\right)$

var = var + seed * growing_new_fraction
SeedAddition = SeedAddition + seed * dwt(p)

### Equations:
$\frac{\partial \text{var}}{\partial t} = 0$ when the patch is not growing or shrinking
$\text{var} = \text{var} \cdot \left(\frac{\text{pwtgcell\_old}(p)}{\text{pwtgcell\_new}(p)}\right)$ for growing patches.
`p` (Index)
`c` (Index)
`pwtgcell_old` (Old patch weights on the grid cell)
`pwtgcell_new` (New patch weights on the grid cell)
`dwt` (Difference between old and new patch weights)
`cwtgcell_old` (Old column weights)
`veg_pp%wtgcell`
`col_pp%wtgcell`
`veg_pp%column(p)`
`mxpft`
p (Patch Number)

### Equations:
$\frac{\mathrm{d} var}{\mathrm{d}t} = \left(1 - \frac{\mathrm{d} wt_{new}}{\mathrm{d}t}\right) var + seed \frac{\mathrm{d}wt_{new}}{\mathrm{d}t}
$p$ (Patch index)
$c$ (Column index)
$var$ (patch-level state variable)
$\text{it}$ (pft type)

$\text{wgt}_{\text{new}}^{\text{cell}}(p)$ (new patch weights on the grid cell)
$\text{wgt}^c(p)$ (column weight of column c at location p)
$\text{dwt}(p)$ (change in patch weight between old and new values)
$\text{growing\_old\_fraction}(p)$ (fraction of old patch area that is growing into the new patch area)
$\text{growing\_new\_fraction}(p)$ (fraction of new patch area that was grown from the old patch area)
$p$ (Patch Index)
$c$ (Column Index)

$growing_new_fraction(p)$ (Fraction of New Weight for Growing Patches)

### Equations:
$\frac{d}{dt} \left( var \right) =  var \cdot f_{grow}$, where $f_{grow}$ is the growth factor for growing patches.
$\Delta flux = -flux \cdot f_{shrunk}$, where $f_{shrunk}$ is the fraction of area that has shrunk.

pwtgcell_new (p_wt')

growing_new_fraction (n_frac)
veg_pp%wtgcell (w_cell)
col_pp%wtgcell (c_cell)

c : Index of column
var : Patch-level state variable
dwt : Change in weight from last call to set_new_weights
growing_old_fraction: Fraction of old area that is still part of the patch, for growing patches
growing_new_fraction: Fraction of new area that is part of the patch, for growing patches

`growing_old_fraction` ((pwtgcell_old / pwtgcell_new) from last call to set_new_weights; only valid for growing patches)
`growing_new_fraction` ((dwt / pwtgcell_new) from last call to set_new_weights; only valid for growing patches)

c (Column Index)

===== File: nunv1.F90 =====

### Variables:
`lam` (Lam)
`phi` (Phi)
`dl` (Dl)
`sindl` (Sin(dl))
`cosdl` (Cos(dl))
`sinpa` (Sin(Phi))
`cospa` (Cos(Phi))
`sinpd` (Sin(Phidp))
`cospd` (Cos(Phidp))
`alphud` (Alpha_ud)
`alphvd` (Alpha_vd)
`betaud` (Beta_u_d)
`betavd` (Beta_v_d)
`termu` (Term_U)
`termv` (Term_V)
`gamma` (Gamma)
`grfu` (Grfu)
`grfv` (Grfv)
`coslat` (Cos(Lat))
`ud` (Ud)
`vd` (Vd)
`lamdp` (Lam_dp)
`phidp` (Phi_dp)

### Equations:
Sin(Phi) = Sin(Phidp) * Cos(Dl) - Cos(Phi) * Sin(Dl)
Cos(Phi) = Cos(Phidp) + Sin(Phi) * Sin(Dl)
Alpha_ud = Cos(Dl)
Alpha_vd = Sin(Phidp) \* Sin(Dl)
Beta_u_d = -Sin(Phi) \* Sin(Dl)
Beta_v_d = Cos(Phidp) \* Cos(Phi) + Sin(Phidp) \* Sin(Phi) \* Cos(Dl)
Term_U = Alpha_ud * Ud + Alpha_vd * Vd
Term_V = Beta_u_d * Ud + Beta_v_d * Vd
Gamma = sqrt(Ud^2 + Vd^2) / (Term_U^2 + Term_V^2)^0.5
Grfulat(i,k) = Grfu(i,k) + Gamma \* Term_U \* Cos(Lat)
Grfvlat(i,k) = Grfv(i,k) + Gamma \* Term_V \* Cos(Lat)

===== File: pft_module.F90 =====

### Variables:
`D0_5` (0.5)
`D1EM20` (1e-20)
`D1_0` (1.0)
`D1_01` (1.01)
`D2_0` (2.0)
`D4_0` (4.0)
`D8_0` (8.0)
`D180_0` (180.0)
`pi` (3.14159265358979323846)

### Equations:
$sc(j) = \left(\frac{\cos(z_c)}{\cos(p_j)}\right)^2$
where `sc` is the algebraic filter at center, `j` is an index, `z_c` is the critical value, and `p_j` is a cosine array
$se(j) = \left(\frac{\cos(z_c)}{\cos(s_e)}\right)^2$
where `se` is the algebraic filter at edge, `j` is an index, `z_c` is the critical value, and `s_e` is a cosine array
$dc(i,j) = \begin{cases} D1_0 + \frac{sc(j)-D1_0}{sc(j)+D1_0}, &  if sc(j) > D1_0 \\ damp, & otherwise \end{cases}$
$i$ (Index)
$j$ (Index)
$dc(i,j)$ (Polar filter at center)

### Equations:
$p(i,j) = rsc * ( ptmp(i) + bt*(ptmp(i-1)+ptmp(i+1)) )$
$i$ (Index)
$j$ (Index)
$p(i,j)$ (Array to be polar filtered)
$rsc$ (Scaling factor)
$bt$ (Bias term)
$ptmp(i)$ (Temporary array)

### Equations:
$\coszc = \cos(ycrit*pi/D180_0)$
$ycrit$ (Critical value) (Ycrit)

### Equations:
$dl = pi/real(im,r8)$
$im$ (Total X dimension) (IM)
$pi$ (Pi) ($\pi$)
$dl$ (Spacing between grid points)

### Equations:
$\cutoff = D1EM20$
$D1EM20$ (Small value close to 0) (D1EM20)
$\cutoff$ (Cutoff for damping factor)

### Equations:
$sc(j) = (coszc/cosp(j))**2$
$js2g0$ (j south limit ghosted 0) (JS2G0)
$jn2g0$ (j north limit ghosted 0) (JN2G0)
$sc(j)$ (Algebraic filter at center) (SC)

D0_0 (Zero real number)

pi (Pi constant)

### Equations:

$dc(i,j) = 1$ if $sc(j) > D_{1EM20}$, otherwise

$dc(i,j) = 1 + sc(j)/(D_8-sc(j))$ if $fft_flt = 0$ and $sc(j) > D_4$

### Equations:
$\text{se}(j) = 1 + \frac{\text{se}(j)-1}{\text{se}(j)+1}$ when $\text{se}(j)\leq 4$ and $fft\_flt=0$

===== File: RNG_MT.F90 =====

### Variables:
N (N)
M (M)
MATA (MATA)
UMASK (UMASK)
LMASK (LMASK)
TMASKB (TMASKB)
TMASKC (TMASKC)
mti (mti)
mag01(0) (mata_0)
mag01(1) (mata_1)
mt(kk) (y_kk)
kk (kk)
y (y)

### Equations:
N = 624
M = 397
Mata_0 = 0
Mata_1 = -1727483681
Umask = -1147483648
Lmask = 2147483647
Tmaskb = -1658038656
Tmaskc = -272236544
y_kk = iand(iand(mt(kk + M), LMASK), (69069 * mt(kk - 1)))
y_kk = ior(y_kk, mag01(ieor(iand(y, 1))))
y = ieor(y, ishft(y, -11))
y = ieor(y, iand(ishft(y, 7), TMASKB))
y = ieor(y, iand(ishft(y, 15), TMASKC))
y = ieor(y, ishft(y, -18))
grnd = (dble(y) + 0.5d0)/(2.0d0**32)
mt(kk) = ior(iand(mt(kk), UMASK), iand(mt(kk + 1), LMASK))
mt(N - 1) = ieor(ieor(mt(M - 1), ishft(y, -1)), mag01(iand(y, 1)))
grnd = (dble(y) + 2.0d0 ** 32) / (2.0d0 ** 32)

===== File: sat.F90 =====

### Variables:
`t` (T)
`dt` (ΔT)
`a0-a8` (Coefficients in equation of state)
`esatw_crm`, `esati_crm` (Saturation vapor pressure over water and ice, respectively)
`qsatw_crm`, `qsati_crm` (Mixing ratio for saturation over water and ice, respectively)
`dtesatw_crm`, `dtesati_crm` (Derivative of saturation vapor pressure over water and ice with respect to temperature, respectively)
`dtqsatw_crm`, `dtqsati_crm` (Derivative of mixing ratio for saturation over water and ice with respect to temperature, respectively)
`p` (Pressure)

### Equations:
esatw_crm = a0 + dt*(a1+dt*(a2+dt*(a3+dt*(a4+dt*(a5+dt*(a6+dt*(a7+a8*dt))))))) (T > -80°C)
esatw_crm = 2.D0*0.01D0*exp(9.550426D0 - 5723.265D0/t + 3.53068D0*Log(t) - 0.00728332D0*t) (T < -80°C)
qsatw_crm = 0.622D0 * esat_crm/max(esat_crm,p-esat_crm) (valid for all temperatures and pressures)
dtesatw_crm = a0 + dt* (a1+dt*(a2+dt*(a3+dt*(a4+dt*(a5+dt*(a6+dt*(a7+a8*dt))))))) (T > -80°C)
dtesatw_crm = esatw_crm(t+1.D0)-esatw_crm(t) (T < -80°C)
dtqsatw_crm = 0.622D0*dtesatw_crm(t)/p (valid for all temperatures and pressures)
esati_crm = a0 + dt*(a1+dt*(a2+dt*(a3+dt*(a4+dt*(a5+dt*(a6+dt*(a7+a8*dt))))))) (T > -80°C)
esati_crm = 0.01D0*exp(9.550426D0 - 5723.265D0/t + 3.53068D0*Log(t) - 0.00728332D0*t) (T < -80°C)
qsati_crm = 0.622D0 * esat_crm/max(esat_crm,p-esat_crm) (valid for all temperatures and pressures)
dtesati_crm = a0 + dt*(a1+dt*(a2+dt*(a3+dt*(a4+dt*(a5+dt*(a6+dt*(a7+a8*dt))))))) (T > -80°C)
dtesati_crm = esati_crm(t+1.D0)-esati_crm(t) (T < -80°C)
dtqsati_crm = 0.622D0*dtesati_crm(t)/p (valid for all temperatures and pressures)

===== File: vdiff_lu_solver.F90 =====

### Variables:
`pcols` (Nc)
`pver` (Nl)
`ncol` (Nc)
`ntop` (Nl)
`nbot` (Nl)
`ksrf` (Kd)
`kv` (Km^2/s)
`tmpi` (`delta-t * g/R`)^2 * `.5 * tm(k+1)**2 / tm(k)**2 * pdel`
`rpdel` (`.5 * pdel(k+1) + pdel(k)`^-1)
`ztodt` (2*delta-t)
`gravit` (g)
`cc_top` (Cd)
`cpairv` (`Cp` * `.5 * tm(k+1)**2 / tm(k)**2`)

### Equations:
`ca(k) = kv(i,k+1) * tmpi(i,k+1) * rpdel(i,k)`
$ca_k$ = $\frac{kv_{im} \cdot tm_i^{2\star} \cdot rpdel_m}{tmpi_im}$
`cc(k) = kv(i,k+1) * tmpi(i,k+1) * rpdel(i,k+1)`
$cc_k$ = $\frac{kv_{im} \cdot tm_i^{2\star} \cdot rpdel_m}{tmpi_im}$
`dnom(k) = 1 / (1 + ca(k) + cc(k))`
$dnom_k$ = $\frac{1}{1+ca_k+cc_k}$
`ze(k) = cc(k) * dnom(k)`
$z_k$ = $cc_k \cdot dnom_k$
`zf(k) = [q(k) + ca(k)*zf(k+1)] / dnom(k)`
$zf_k$ = $\frac{q_{k}+ca_k\cdot zf_{k+1}}{dnom_k}$

`array(:,jm)` is sent to PE_right
`array(:,0)` is received from PE_left
\[ \mathbf{A}^{\text{(m)}}_{\text{:},\text{jm}} = \frac{1}{N_PES} P_E \left( T_A \right) A^{\text{(P_{E,right})}}_{\text{:},0} + (1 - \delta_{P_E,P_{E,left}}) A^{\text{(m)}}_{\text{:},\text{jm}} \]
`array(:,1)` is sent to PE_left
`array(:,jm+1)` is received from PE_right
\[ \mathbf{A}^{\text{(m)}}_{\text{:},\text{1}} = \frac{1}{N_PES} P_E \left( T_B \right) A^{\text{(P_{E,left})}}_{\text{:},\text{jm}+1} + (1 - \delta_{P_E,P_{E,right}}) A^{\text{(m)}}_{\text{:},\text{1}} \]

LSX = LSX - (MLSSF + MLXf) * Δt
DSX = DSX - (MDSSF + MDXF) * Δt
LCRX = LCRX - (MLCSF + MLXCf) * Δt
DCRX = DCRX - (MDCSF + MDCXF) * Δt
RT = RT - Mrtf * Δt

===== File: dcmip2016-terminator.F90 =====

### Variables:
`cly_constant` (cL_y Constant)
`pi` (π)
`half_pi` (π / 2)
`degrees_to_radians` (° to rad)
`k1_lat_center` (k1 Lat Center)
`k1_lon_center` (k1 Lon Center)
`lat` (Latitude, ° or rad)
`lon` (Longitude, ° or rad)
`cl` (Molar Mixing Ratio of Cl, dimensionless)
`cl2` (Molar Mixing Ratio of Cl2, dimensionless)
`dt` (Physics Time Step)
`r` (Useful Algebraic Quantity)
`det` (Determinant)
`el` (Exponential of dt)
`k1` (Reaction Rate 1)
`k2` (Reaction Rate 2)

### Equations:
`k1 = 1.0_8 * max(0.d0, sin(lat) * sin(k1_lat_center) + cos(lat) * cos(k1_lat_center) * cos(lon - k1_lon_center))`
`k2 = 1._8`
`r = k1 / (4._8*k2)`
`cly = cl + 2._8*cl2`
`det = sqrt(r*r + 2._8*r*cly)`
`expdt = exp(-4._8*k2*det*dt)`
`if ( abs(det * k2 * dt) .gt. 1e-16 ) then`
`cl_f = -el * (cl - det + r)*(cl + det + r) / (1._8 + expdt + dt*el*(cl + r))`
`cl2_f = -cl_f / 2._8`
`det = sqrt(r*r + 2._8*cly_constant*r)`
`cl = (det-r)`
`cl2 = cly_constant/2._8 - (det-r)/2._8`

### Variables:

qa (q_air_specific)
L (latent_heat)
rhoa (r_hoa)
ta (t_air)

### Equations:
e_(saturation) = const06 * t_a^(5/2)
or equivalently
q_saturated = ((e_saturation / r_gas) / (t_air + k_elvin))^0.622
e_actuation = (q_specific * p_total) / (r_gas * t_air)
l_(latent) = c_p_a * l_(evaporation) + 2.5 * g * r_gas * t_air^3 / (t_saturation + k_elvin)

===== File: trb_mtn_stress.F90 =====

### Variables:
horomin (h_o)
z0max (z_0^max)
dv2min (Δv^2_min)
orocnst (o_c)
z0fac (z_0_f)
karman (κ)
gravit (g)
rair (R_a)
pcols (P_c)
pver (P_v)
ncol (N_c)
u (u)
v (v)
t (T)
pmid (p_m)
exner (ε)
zm (z_m)
sgh (σ_h)
landfrac (L_f)
ksrf (k_s)
taux (τ_x)
tauy (τ_y)

### Equations:
h_o = o_c σ_h(i) [ m ]
Δv^2_min = 0.01 m^2/s^2
τ_x = -k_s u
τ_y = -k_s v
k_s = ρ c_d v_m |L_f|
c_d = (κ / log((z_m(i,P_v) + z_0)))^2
z_0 = min(o_c σ_h, z_0^max)
ϵ = p_m(i,P_v) / (R_a T(i,P_v))
Δv^2 = max(u(i,K_t)^2 + v(i,K_t)^2 - u(i,K_b)^2 - v(i,K_b)^2, 0.01 m^2/s^2)
R_i = 2 g (T(i,K_t) ε(i,K_t) - T(i,K_b) ε(i,K_b)) z_m(K_t - K_b) / ((T(i,K_t) ε(i,K_t) + T(i,K_b) ε(i,K_b)) Δv^2)
c_d' = c_d * max(0, min(1, 1 - R_i))
σ_h(i): standard deviation of orography at column i
u, v: layer mid-point zonal and meridional wind at layer k
T: layer mid-point temperature at layer k
p_m: layer mid-point pressure at layer k
ε: layer mid-point exner function at layer k
z_m(k): layer mid-point height at layer k
R_i: Richardson number over the lowest 2 layers
K_t, K_b: bottom and top of source region

### Equations:

Equation: lenval = len_trim(val)
Equation: if (lenpath < 0) then
Equation: write(s_logunit, F00)"ERROR: seconds must be > 0, sec=" (sec)

Equation: rcode = isec
Equation: call system(str,rcode)
Equation: lenval = len_trim(tmpval)
Equation: val = trim(tmpval)
Equation: if (len_trim(val) == 0) then
Equation: rcode = 1
Equation: if (len_trim(val) > SHR_KIND_CL) then
Equation: rcode = 2
Equation: last_count = count
Equation: SHR_SYS_IRTC = count + count_offset
Equation: isec = nint(sec)
Equation: call sleep(isec)
Equation: write(s_logunit,F00) 'ERROR: seconds must be > 0, sec=',sec
Equation: call system(str,rcode)
Equation: flush(unit)

===== File: shear.F90 =====

### Variables:
M^2 (M_squared)
U (U)
V (V)
z (z)
h (h)
i (i)
nlev (nlev)
cnpar (cnpar)
u (u)
v (v)
vo (vo)
o (uo)

### Equations:
$\boxed{M^2 = \left( \frac{\partial U}{\partial z} \right)^2 + \left( \frac{\partial V}{\partial z} \right)^2}$
$M_squared = \left( \frac{U_z}{z} \right)^2 + \left( \frac{V_z}{z} \right)^2$
$\boxed{\left( \frac{\partial U}{\partial z} \right)^2 \approx \frac{\left(\bar U_{j+1}-\bar U_j\right)\left(\tilde U_{j+1}-\tilde U_j\right)}{(z_{j+1}-z_j)^2}}$
$\left( \frac{U_z}{z} \right)^2 \approx \frac{\left(U_{{i+1}}-U_i\right)\left(\frac12(\hat U_i+\tilde U_i)-\frac12(\hat U_{i+1}+\tilde U_{i+1})\right)}{(h_i)^2}$
$\boxed{\left( \frac{\partial V}{\partial z} \right)^2 \approx \frac{\left(\bar V_{j+1}-\bar V_j\right)\left(\tilde V_{j+1}-\tilde V_j\right)}{(z_{j+1}-z_j)^2}}$
$\left( \frac{V_z}{z} \right)^2 \approx \frac{\left(V_{{i+1}}-V_i\right)\left(\frac12(\hat V_i+\tilde V_i)-\frac12(\hat V_{i+1}+\tilde V_{i+1})\right)}{(h_i)^2}$
$\boxed{P_{j+1/2} = P_{j+1/2}^l + P_{j+1/2}^u}$
$P_{{i+1/2}} = \left(D_{kin}\right)_{i+1/2}^l + \left(D_{kin}\right)_{i+1/2}^u$
$\boxed{\left(D_{kin} \right)_j  = \frac{\nu_{j+1/2}}{2} \left\{ \frac{\sigma \left(\hat U_{j+1}-\hat U_j\right)\left(\hat U_{j+1}-U_j\right)+ (1-\sigma)\left(U_{j+1}-U_j\right)\left(U_{j+1}-\hat U_j\right)}{(z_{j+1/2}-z_{j-1/2})(z_{j+1}-z_j)} + \frac{\nu_{j-1/2}}{2} \frac{\sigma \left(\hat U_{j}-\hat U_{j-1}\right)\left(U_{j}-\hat U_{j-1}\right)+ (1-\sigma)\left(U_{j}-U_{j-1}\right)\left(\hat U_{j}-U_{j-1}\right)}{(z_{j+3/2}-z_{j+1/2})(z_{j+1}-z_j)} \right\}}$
$\left(D_{kin} \right)_{{i+1/2}} = \frac{\nu_{{i+1/2}}}{2} \left\{ \frac{\sigma (U_{{i+1}}-U_i)(U_{{i+1}}-\hat U_{{i+1}})+(1-\sigma)(\hat U_{i+1}-\hat U_i)(\hat U_{i+1}-U_i)}{(h_{{i}})(z_{{i+1}}-z_{i})} + \frac{\nu_{{i}}}{2} \frac{\sigma (U_i-U_{i-1})(U_i-\hat U_{i-1})+(1-\sigma)(\hat U_i-\hat U_{i-1})(U_i-U_{i-1})}{(h_{{i}})(z_{{i+1}}-z_{i})} \right\}$

### Equations:
`ρ_top = p_int / (rair * T_msis)` (Density of top interface)

`z_top = m2km * zi` (Height of top interface in km)
z_top(km) = 10^{-3} * zi(m)
`ρ_top = p_int / (rair * T_msis)` (Density of top interface)
ρ_top (kg/m^3) = pint(pcols,ntop_molec) / r_air(r_tmsis)
`z_top = m2km * zi` (Height of top interface in km)
z_top(km) = 10^{-3} * zi(m)

===== File: trisolve.F90 =====

### Variables:
`atri` (A), `btrie` (B), `ctri` (C), `dtri` (D)
`dnmn` (X)
`n`
`nn`, `nnm2`, `nnp2`
`tmp(1)`, `tmp(2)`
`denom(1)`, `denom(2)`
`numer(1)`, `numer(2)`
`e(1)`, `e(2)`
`f(1)`, `f(2)`

### Equations:
$\frac{A(1)*B(1)+A(2)*B(2)}{{B(1)}^2+{B(2)}^2}=E(1)$
$\frac{A(2)*B(1)-A(1)*B(2)}{{B(1)}^2+{B(2)}^2}=E(2)$
$\frac{D(1)*B(1)+D(2)*B(2)}{{B(1)}^2+{B(2)}^2}=F(1)$
$\frac{D(2)*B(1)-D(1)*B(2)}{{B(1)}^2+{B(2)}^2}=F(2)$
$(CTRI(2NN-1))*E(2NNM2-1)-(CTRI(2NN))*E(2NNM2) = TMP(1)$
$(CTRI(2NN-1))*E(2NNM2)+(CTRI(2NN))*E(2NNM2-1) = TMP(2)$
$B(2NN-1)-TMP(1)=DENOM(1)$
$B(2NN)-(TMP(2))=DENOM(2)$
$(CTRI(2NN-1))*F(2NNM2-1)-(CTRI(2NN))*F(2NNM2) = TMP(1)$
$(CTRI(2NN-1))*F(2NNM2)+(CTRI(2NN))*F(2NNM2-1) = TMP(2)$
$NUMER(1)=DTRI(2NN-1)+TMP(1)$
$NUMER(2)=DTRI(2NN)+TMP(2)$
$\frac{A(2NN-1)*DENOM(1)+A(2NN)*DENOM(2)}{{DENOM(1)}^2+{DENOM(2)}^2}=E(2NN-1)$
$\frac{A(2NN)*DENOM(1)-A(2NN-1)*DENOM(2)}{{DENOM(1)}^2+{DENOM(2)}^2}=E(2NN)$
$\frac{NUMER(1)*DENOM(1)+NUMER(2)*DENOM(2)}{{DENOM(1)}^2+{DENOM(2)}^2}=F(2NN-1)$
$\frac{NUMER(2)*DENOM(1)-NUMER(1)*DENOM(2)}{{DENOM(1)}^2+{DENOM(2)}^2}=F(2NN)$
$X(2N-1)=F(2N-1)$
$X(2N)=F(2N)$

### Variables:

### Equations:
$w_{i,j} = \left( 0.3 (c^2) + j \right)^{1/100}$, where c is the column index.
$s_{i,j,k} = 10(c^2) + j$ and $b_{i,j,k} = 0.5(c^2) + j$, where k is the level index.
$e_{i,j} = \left( 20(c^2) + j \right)$, where e is the evapotranspiration flux.
$E_{i} = 30(c^2)$, where E is the heat flux from soil to atmosphere.
$C_{i,j,k} = C_{i-1,j,k} + 1$, where c is a counter variable and C is the carbon state.
$f(hydrologyc) = i$ and $g(nolakec_and_nourbanc) = i$, where f and g are filter variables.

===== File: tstep.F90 =====

### Variables:
lm (Local Fourier Wavenumber Index)
m (Global Wavenumber Index)
n (2-D Wavenumber Index)
k (Level Index)
lmr, lmc (Real and Imaginary Spectral Indices)
ir, ii (Real and Imaginary Spectral Indices)
nn (Real and Imaginary Spectral Indices)
zdt (Timestep in seconds)
ztdtsq (Dt*(n(n+1)/a^2 where n is 2-D Wavenumber))
hhref (Href/2 - Reference Hydrostatic Matrix / 2)
hbps (Bps/2 - Reference Coeff. for lnps Term in Div. Eq. / 2)
ztemp (Temporary Workspace)
d(ir,k) (Spectral Array D, Real Part)
d(ii,k) (Spectral Array D, Imaginary Part)
t(ir,k) (Temperature, Real Part)
t(ii,k) (Temperature, Imaginary Part)
alps(ir) (Ln(pstar), Real Part)
alps(ii) (Ln(pstar), Imaginary Part)
href(k,k) (Href - Hydrostatic Matrix)
bps(k) (Bps - Coeff. for lnps Term in Div. Eq.)
bm1(kk,k,m+n-1) (Inverse Matrix Element)
tau(kk,k) (Tau - Matrix Element)

### Equations:
d(ir,k) = d(ir,k) + ztdtsq(n+m-1)*(hhref*t(ir,k) + hbps*alps(ir))
d(ii,k) = d(ii,k) + ztdtsq(n+m-1)*(hhref*t(ii,k) + hbps*alps(ii))
d(ir,k) = d(ir,k) + ztdtsq(n+m-1)*hhref*t(ir,kk)
d(ii,k) = d(ii,k) + ztdtsq(n+m-1)*hhref*t(ii,kk)
z(2*n-1,k) = z(2*n-1,k) + bm1(kk,k,m+n-1)*d(ir,kk)
z(2*n,k) = z(2*n,k) + bm1(kk,k,m+n-1)*d(ii,kk)
d(ir,k) = z(2*n-1,k)
d(ii,k) = z(2*n,k)
alps(ir) = alps(ir) - ztemp*d(ir,k)
alps(ii) = alps(ii) - ztemp*d(ii,k)
t(nn,k) = t(nn,k) - zdt*tau(kk,k)*d(nn,kk)

===== File: SphericalCoordinates.F90 =====

### Variables:
Latitude (lat)
Longitude (lon)
MAX_LAT (MAXLAT)
MIN_LAT (MINTAT)
MAX_LON (MAXLON)
MIN_LON (MINLON)
adjustLat (adjustLat)
numPoleCrossing (numPoleCrossing)
quadrant (quadrant)
absLat (absLat)
absLon (absLon)

### Equations:
$$ \text{adjustLat} = |\text{lat}| - 360\left\lfloor\frac{|\text{lat}|}{360}\right\rfloor $$
$$ \text{quadrant} = \mod\left(\left\lfloor\frac{\text{adjustLat}}{90}\right\rfloor, 4\right) $$
Case 0: $$ \text{lat} = \text{sign}(\text{adjustLat}, \text{lat}) $$
Case 1: $$ \text{lat} = \text{sign}(180 - \text{adjustLat}, \text{lat}),\qquad \text{lon} = \text{lon} + 180 $$
Case 2: $$ \text{lat} = -\text{sign}(180 - \text{adjustLat}, \text{lat}),\qquad \text{lon} = \text{lon} + 180 $$
Case 3: $$ \text{lat} = -\text{sign}(\text{adjustLat} - 360, \text{lat}) $$
$$ \text{absLon} = |\text{lon}|,\qquad \text{lon} = \text{sign}(|\text{lon}| - 360 \left\lfloor\frac{|\text{lon}|}{360}\right\rfloor, \text{lon}) $$
$$ \text{if }(\text{lon} < 0),\qquad \text{lon} = \text{lon} + 360 $$

### Variables:

p (Pressure)

phis (Surface Geopotential)
u (Zonal wind)
v (Meridional wind)
w (Vertical Velocity)

### Equations:
$\rho = \frac{p}{R_d T}$  (density equation)
$\phi_s = g \cdot z_s$     (surface geopotential equation)
$T = Teq \exp\left( -\frac{c\cdot u_{eq}^2}{g}\sin^2(\theta) \right)$   (temperature equation)
$p_s = Peq\exp\left( -\frac{u_{eq}^2}{2R_dTe_q}\sin^2(\theta) - \frac{\phi_s}{R_d Te_q}  \right)$     (surface pressure equation)
$\zeta = \frac{R_d T_e q}{g}\log\left( \frac{Peq}{p} \right) - \frac{u_{eq}^2}{2 g} \sin^2(\theta)$      (height and pressure equation)
$u = u_{eq}\cos(\theta)\sqrt{1+2c\zeta}$   (zonal wind equation)
$\rho = p/(R_d T)$  (density equation)
