### Equations:
No explicit equations are present in the given Fortran file. However, we can infer some mathematical operations from the code.
`p = MPI_Comm_size(MPI_COMM_WORLD, p)`: This operation retrieves the total number of processors.
`allocate(data_in(p), stat = stat)`: This operation allocates memory for the data_in array with size equal to the total number of processors.
`nf90_open(FILE_NAME, IOR(NF90_NOWRITE, NF90_MPIIO), ncid)`: This operation opens the netCDF file in read-only mode.
`start = (/ 1, my_rank + 1/)`: This operation sets the start indices for reading data from the netCDF file. The first index is always 1, and the second index is the local rank of the processor plus one.
`count = (/ p, 1 /)`: This operation sets the count of elements to read from each dimension. In this case, we are only interested in reading data from a single row (first dimension) with size equal to the total number of processors.
`if (data_in(x) .ne. my_rank) then`: This operation compares the value of the xth element of data_in array with the local rank of the processor.
`nf90_close(ncid)`: This operation closes the netCDF file.
`deallocate(data_in)`: This operation deallocates the memory allocated for the data_in array.
`p = MPI_Comm_size(MPI_COMM_WORLD, p)`
`data_in(x) = 0` (initialization)
`allocate(data_in(p), stat = stat)` => No explicit equation
`nf90_open(FILE_NAME, IOR(NF90_NOWRITE, NF90_MPIIO), ncid)` => No explicit equation
`start = (/ 1, my_rank + 1/)`: This operation can be represented as an equation:
`s_1 = 1`
`s_D(my_rank+1) = my_rank + 1` where D is the dimension index
`count = (/ p, 1 /)`: This operation can be represented as an equation:
`c_D(p) = p`
`c_2(1) = 1`
`if (data_in(x) .ne. my_rank) then`: This operation can be represented as an inequality:
`d_x ≠ r` where x is the index of data_in array, d_x is its value, and r is the local rank of the processor.
`nf90_close(ncid)` => No explicit equation
`deallocate(data_in)`: This operation can be represented as an equation:
`data_in = 0` (reinitialization)
`deallocate(data_in)`

===== File: mo_airglow.F90 =====

### Variables:

### Equations:

τ = 86400 s
∂τ = 1/86400 s⁻¹
h̄c = 6.62608 × 10⁻³⁴ Js × 2.9979 × 10⁸ m/s / (1 × 10⁹ m) = 1.9865 × 10⁻²³ Jm

===== File: mo_heating_rates.F90 =====

### Variables:
rho (Density of the atmosphere)
cp (Heat capacity of dry air)
grav (Gravity acceleration)
f_net (Net flux)
z (Altitude)
H (Heating rate)

### Equations:
$\rho = \frac{p}{z}$
where p is pressure and is not present in this file. However, since p is present as `plev` which is used in the calculation of the heating rate equation below, we will replace it with `plev`.
$H = \frac{1}{\rho cp} \frac{\partial f_{net}}{\partial z}$
where $\frac{\partial f_{net}}{\partial z}$ is calculated using hydrostatic equation.
Since the hydrostatic equation for density is used in this code, we will use it to express $\frac{\partial f_{net}}{\partial z}$ as a function of net flux and its derivatives with respect to pressure.
$\frac{\partial \rho}{\partial p} = -\frac{1}{g}$
$H = - \left( \frac{1}{cp} \frac{\partial f_{net}}{\partial p} \right)$
$\frac{\partial f_{net}}{\partial p} = \left( \frac{\partial flux_up}{\partial p} - \frac{\partial flux_dn}{\partial p} \right) / (2 * (ncol))$
$H = \frac{1}{cp} * \frac{1}{g} * \left( (\flux_up_{i+1} - \flux_up_i + \flux_dn_i - \flux_dn_{i+1}) / (2 * (ncol)) \right) * (\plev_{i+1} - \plev_i)$

================================================================================

===== File: mkdiagnosticsMod.F90 =====

### Variables:
`garea_i` (A)
`garea_o` (A)
`gdata_i` (D)
`gdata_o` (D)
`ns_i` (N)
`ns_o` (N)
`ni` (I)
`no` (I)
`k` (J)
`re` (R)
`gridmap%area_src(ni)` (A)
`data_i(ni)` (D)
`data_o(no)` (D)
`gwt_i` (W)
`gwt_o` (W)

### Equations:
$\boxed{garea_i = \sum_{ni=1}^{ns_i} gridmap\%area_src(ni) \cdot re^2}$ (A)
$\boxed{gdata_i = \sum_{ni=1}^{ns_i} data_i(ni) \cdot gridmap\%area_src(ni) \cdot gridmap\%frac_src(ni) \cdot re^2}$ (D)
$\boxed{garea_o = \sum_{no=1}^{ns_o} gridmap\%area_dst(no) \cdot re^2}$ (A)
$\boxed{gdata_o = \sum_{no=1}^{ns_o} data_o(no) \cdot gridmap\%area_dst(no) \cdot gridmap\%frac_dst(no) \cdot re^2}$ (D)
$\boxed{gwt_i = \sum_{ni=1}^{ns_i} gridmap\%area_src(ni) \cdot gridmap\%frac_src(ni)}$ (W)
$\boxed{gwt_o = \sum_{no=1}^{ns_o} gridmap\%area_dst(no) \cdot gridmap\%frac_dst(no)}$ (W)
$\boxed{\frac{gdata_i}{gwt_i} = \frac{\sum_{ni=1}^{ns_i} data_i(ni) \cdot gridmap\%area_src(ni) \cdot gridmap\%frac_src(ni)}{\sum_{ni=1}^{ns_i} gridmap\%area_src(ni) \cdot gridmap\%frac_src(ni)}}$ (D)
$\boxed{\frac{gdata_o}{gwt_o} = \frac{\sum_{no=1}^{ns_o} data_o(no) \cdot gridmap\%area_dst(no) \cdot gridmap\%frac_dst(no)}{\sum_{no=1}^{ns_o} gridmap\%area_dst(no) \cdot gridmap\%frac_dst(no)}}$ (D)
$\boxed{gdata_i = \begin{cases} \frac{gdata_i}{100._r8}, & \text{if } \text{percent} \\ gdata_i, & \text{otherwise} \end{cases}}$ (D)
$\boxed{gdata_o = \begin{cases} \frac{gdata_o}{100._r8}, & \text{if } \text{percent} \\ gdata_o, & \text{otherwise} \end{cases}}$ (D)

===== File: interp.F90 =====

### Variables:
ncidi (NCDI)
ncido (NCIDO)
i (Index)
j (Index)
k (Index)
numxis (Number of X grid size in input)
numxin (Number of X grid points in input)
numxo (Number of X grid points in output)
jj, jjs, jjn (Indices)
count (Count)
x_in(i) (Input X coordinate)
y_in(j) (Input Y coordinate)
z_in(k) (Input Z coordinate)
x_out(i) (Output X coordinate)
y_out(j) (Output Y coordinate)
z_out(k) (Output Z coordinate)
f_in(i,j,k) (Input field value)
f_out(i,j,k) (Output field value)

### Equations:
∆z_in = z_out(k+1) - z_out(k)  (change in input Z coordinate between consecutive output levels, subscript k)
Δz_in = z_in(k+1) - z_in(k)    (change in input Z coordinate between consecutive output levels, superscript k)
if (|Δz_in| > 1.e-4) then do_vert_interp = .true. (condition for vertical interpolation based on the change in Z coordinates)
col_out(nzo) = 0.0_r8          (initialization of output column values)
col_in(k) = f_in(i,j,k)        (input column values at index k)
f_z(i,j,k) = col_out(k)       (output field value at grid point (i, j, k))
|x_in(i) - x_out(i)| > 1.e-4    (condition for horizontal interpolation based on the difference in X coordinates between input and output grids)
|y_in(j) - y_out(j)| > 1.e-4    (condition for horizontal interpolation based on the difference in Y coordinates between input and output grids)
∆x_in = x_out(i+1) - x_out(i)   (change in X coordinate between consecutive grid points, subscript i)
Δx_in = x_in(i+1) - x_in(i)     (change in X coordinate between consecutive grid points, superscript i)
∫w(x) dx = 0                    (linear interpolation weights for variable w over the input grid, integral of a function over a continuous interval)
w(x_i) = (∑(x_j^2 - x_i^2))/Δx_in if i != j   (interpolation weight at point x_i based on the quadratic term and change in X coordinate between consecutive points)
w(x_i) = 1.0_r8 if i == j          (interpolation weight for the same grid point)
f_out(i,j,k) = ∑(f_in(j,k) * w(x_j))     (output field value at grid point (i, j, k) based on linear interpolation in X and Y coordinates)
call lininterp(f_z(:,:,k), nxi, nyi, f_out(:,:,k), nxo, nyo, xwgts, ywgts)  (horizontal interpolation for a single output level, subscript k)

`X_CHAR_MIN` and `X_CHAR_MAX` have a difference that represents the range of characters (`x_char_max - x_char_min`)
`INRANGE(x, lower_bound, upper_bound)` function checks if value `x` is within a given range `[lower_bound, upper_bound]`
`INDEX2NCINDEXES(index)` returns an index based on `index` which can be interpreted as a calculation involving `index`, however the actual equation depends on how it's used.
`HASH(x)` and other hashing functions (`HASH4`, `HASH_TEXT`, etc.) perform calculations that depend on the input value `x`.

===== File: mkgdpMod.F90 =====

### Variables:
`ldomain`
`mapfname`
`datfname`
`ndiag`
`gdp_o`
`tgridmap`
`tdomain`
`data_i`
`ncid`
`varid`
`ier`
`min_valid`

### Equations:
$\mathrm{data\_i} = \mathrm{nf\_get\_var\_double}\left(\mathrm{ncid}, \mathrm{varid}, \mathrm{data\_i}\right)$
data_i is the result of reading a double precision array from the file with netCDF ID ncid and variable ID varid.
$\mathrm{gdp\_o} = \mathrm{gridmap\_areaave}\left(\mathrm{tgridmap}, \mathrm{data\_i}, \mathrm{gdp\_o}, \mathrm{nodata}=0._\mathrm{r8}\right)$
gdp_o is the average of data_i over the areas defined by tgridmap.
$\mathrm{if}\left(\mathrm{min\_bad}\left(\mathrm{gdp\_o}, \mathrm{min\_valid}, \mathrm{'gdp'}\right)\right) \Rightarrow \mathrm{stop}$
if any value in gdp_o is less than min_valid, stop the program.
$\mathrm{output\_diagnostics\_continuous}\left(\mathrm{data\_i}, \mathrm{gdp\_o}, \mathrm{tgridmap}, \mathrm{"GDP"}, \mathrm{"x1000 US$ per capita"}, \mathrm{ndiag}\right)$
output the diagnostic data.

===== File: interpolate_1d.F90 =====

### Variables:
`xs` (X Values)
`ys` (Y Values)
`x_pt` (Interpolated x value at a particular point)
`y_pt` (Interpolated y value at a particular point)
`i` (Index Counter)
`weight` (Weight for interpolation)

### Equations:
$y_{pt} = \boxed{y_s(1)}$   if  $x_{pt} < x_s(1)$
$y_{pt} = y_s(i-1) + \frac{x_{pt}-x_s(i-1)}{x_s(i)-x_s(i-1)} \cdot (y_s(i)-y_s(i-1))$   if  $x_{pt} < x_s(i)$
$y_{pt} = y_s(size(x_s))$   otherwise

### Equations:
1._r8 + exp((195000._r8-stk)/rtk)+exp((stk-222500._r8)/rtk) = f_actv(stk, rtk)
tfnx = exp(25.227_r8 - 62500._r8/rtk)/actv
tfny = exp(0.0742_r8*(tcsr-25._r8))
wfng = exp(0.05_r8 * psism)
tcs = tks - 273.15_r8
tkso = tks + offset_local
stk = 710._r8 * tkso
rtk = 8.3143_r8 * tkso
ans = h2osoi_liqvol + c1 * rc/(rm+filmt) * (filmt/rm*h2osoi_liqvol+1._r8/max(tauw,1.e-10))
ans = (h2osoi_liqvol+air_vol/bunsencef)*(1._r8+c1*rc/(rm+filmt) * &
ans = 4._r8 * pi/(diffusb*(rm+filmthk))
ans = 4._r8 * pi * vm * filmthk/(diffusw*rm*(rm+filmthk))
ikappa = Rs_soil + Rs_mic
ans = 1._r8 + k1w * Ncell * ikappa
k1w = 4._r8*pi*diffusw*rc*Np*rp/(Np*rp+pi*rc)
K0 = k2p*(Np*rp+pi*rc)/(4._r8*pi*diffusw*rc*rp*Na)
Np_eff = Np * ftn
call ref_Km_k1w_bacteria(k2p, Np_eff, rp, rc, diffusw, K0, k1w)
ans = exp(-13.65_r8 - 0.857_r8 * log(-psiMPa))
ans = max(1.e-8_r8, psiMPa)
gamma=get_bacteria_gamma(Ncell, k1w, rm,  filmthk, diffusw, diffusw)
call get_bacteria_K0(ftn, diffusw, K0, k1w)
diffus = diffusw0 * agg_tor
k0, k1w = get_bacteria_K0(ftn, diffus,  K0, k1w)
gamma=get_bacteria_gamma(Ncell, k1w, rm,  filmthk, diffusb, diffusw)
ans=gamma * K0

===== File: SoilWaterRetentionCurveClappHornberg1978Mod.F90 =====

### Variables:
hksat (HK_SAT)
imped (IMPED)
s (S)
bsw (BSW)
hk (HK)
dhkds (DHKDS)
smpsat (SMPSAT)
smp (SMP)
dsmpds (DSMPDS)
smp_target (SMP_TARGET)

### Equations:
HK = IMPED \* HK_SAT \* S^(2\_BSW + 3) (HK = imped * hksat * s^(2*bsw+3))
DHKDS = (2\_BSW + 3) * HK / S (dhkds=(2*bsw+3)*hk/s)
SMP = -SMPSAT \* S^(-BSW) (smp=-smpsat*s^(-bsw))
DSMPDS = -BSW \* SMP / S (dsmpds=-bsw*smp/s)
S_TARGET = (-SMP_TARGET/SMPSAT)^(-1/BSW) (s_target=(-smp_target/smpsat)^(-1/bsw))

### Equations:
None explicitly present in the given Fortran code.
Aboveground Net Primary Production (AGNPP) is likely to be related to annual sum of NPP (ANNSUM_NPP). However, this relationship is not explicitly stated in the given code.
Belowground Net Primary Production (BGNPP) is also likely to be related to ANNSUM_NPP. Again, this relationship is not explicitly stated in the given code.
TAVG_AGNPP = AGNPP / (24 * 3600)   ! average aboveground net primary production over time
TAVG_BGNPP = BGNPP / (24 * 3600)   ! average belowground net primary production over time
ANNAVG_AGNPP = (1/365) * ANNSUM_NPP   ! annual average aboveground net primary production
ANNAVG_BGNPP = (1/365) * ANNSUM_NPP   ! annual average belowground net primary production

### Equations:
$\Delta t = 1 / dt$ ($\frac{1}{\Delta t}$)
$rnd_var_inv = 1 / rnd_var$ ($\frac{1}{r}$)
$z_i^{n+1} = z^n_i + \partial_z \nu_t (z^n_i) \Delta t + R \left\{2 r^{-1} \nu_t (z^n_i + \frac12  \partial_z \nu_t (z^n_i) \Delta t)\right\}^{1/2}$ ($z_{i+1}^n = z_n^i + \frac{\partial v}{\partial z}|_i \Delta t + R \left\{2 r^{-1} v|_i + \frac12  \frac{\partial v}{\partial z}|_i \Delta t\right\}^{1/2}$)
$R = (2 rnd - 1)$ ($R = \pm \sqrt{2} Rnd$)
$\nu_t(z^n_i) = r^{-1} \nu_t (z^n_i + \frac12  \partial_z \nu_t (z^n_i) \Delta t)$ ($\nu_t|_i = \frac{v}{r}|_{i+\frac{\partial v}{\partial z}|_i \Delta t / 2}$)
$\nu_t(z^n_i) = r^{-1} (\nu_t (z^n_i + \frac12  \partial_z \nu_t (z^n_i) \Delta t))^{1/2}$ ($\nu_t|_i = \frac{v}{r}|_{i+\frac{\partial v}{\partial z}|_i \Delta t / 2}^{1/2}$)

### Equations:
$m_w = \text{adv\_mass}(spc_idx)$
$\text{sflx}(\text{i},\text{n}) = \sum_{isec=1}^{n_sector} \text{emissions}(\text{m})\%fields(isec)\%\data(\text{i},1,\text{lchnk})$
$\text{mfactor} = 1.65979e{-23}\text{_r8} \times m_w$
$\text{sflx}(\text{i},\text{n}) = \text{flux}(\text{i}) \times \text{mfactor}$ when units of emissions is not 'kg/m2/s' or 'kg/m2/sec'
$dayfrac = \frac{\text{sunoff}}{\pi}$
$\text{sflx}(\text{i},\text{c10h16\_ndx}) = \frac{\text{sflx}(\text{i},\text{c10h16\_ndx})}{0.7 + 0.3 \times dayfrac}$ when c10h16_ndx is not in polar night
$\text{sflx}(\text{i},\text{c10h16\_ndx}) = \text{sflx}(\text{i},\text{c10h16\_ndx}) \times 0.7$ when loc_angle is between sunoff and sunon
$iso\_off = 2\pi - 1.2\pi$
$iso\_on = 1.8\pi$
$\text{sflx}(\text{i},\text{isop\_ndx}) = 0$ when loc_angle is between iso_off and iso_on
$\text{sflx}(\text{i},\text{isop\_ndx}) = \frac{2}{iso\_off} \times \pi \times (\sin(\pi \times \frac{\text{loc\_angle}-iso\_on}{iso\_off}))^2$ when loc_angle is not between iso_off and iso_on

### Equations:
Flux in kg/m^2/s needs to be converted into kg/m^2/s using molecular weight.
Day fraction is calculated from the terminator longitude.
Alpha-pinene flux is adjusted for diurnal variation.
Alpha-pinene flux is further adjusted if the location is in polar day.
Isoprene flux is set to zero if the location is in polar day.
A factor is calculated for the isoprene flux adjustment based on the location's longitude.
If the calculated factor is less than or equal to zero, it is adjusted by adding 2*pi.
The calculated factor is divided by twice the iso_off value plus a small positive number.
Sets the isoprene flux to zero.
Calculates the adjusted factor for isoprene flux.
Calculates the isoprene flux.

### Equations:
amufac = 1.65979e-23_r8         ! 1.e4* kg / amu
dayfrac = sunoff / pi
sflx(i,c10h16_ndx) = sflx(i,c10h16_ndx) / (.7_r8 + .3_r8*dayfrac)
sflx(i,c10h16_ndx) = sflx(i,c10h16_ndx) * .7_r8
iso_off = .8_r8 * pi
iso_on  = 1.2_r8 * pi
iso_off = .8_r8 * sunoff
iso_on  = 2._r8 * pi - iso_off
factor = loc_angle - iso_on
factor = (factor <= 0._r8) ? factor + 2._r8*pi : factor
factor = factor / (2._r8*iso_off + 1.e-6_r8)
sflx(i,isop_ndx) = sflx(i,isop_ndx) * 2._r8 / iso_off * pi * (sin(pi*factor))**2

dec_max

### Equations:
sflx(:ncol,n) = flux(:ncol)
mfactor = amufac * emissions(m)%mw
sflx(:ncol,n) = flux(:ncol) * mfactor
sunoff = acos( -tan(declination)*tan(latitude) )
sunon  = twopi - sunoff
dayfrac = sunoff / pi
sflx(i,c10h16_ndx) = sflx(i,c10h16_ndx) / (.7_r8 + .3_r8*dayfrac)
factor = loc_angle - iso_on
if( factor <= 0._r8 ) then
end if
factor = factor / (2._r8*iso_off + 1.e-6_r8)
sflx(i,isop_ndx) = sflx(i,isop_ndx) * 2._r8 / iso_off * pi * (sin(pi*factor))**2

================================================================================

### Equations:
$\frac{\partial s_{min\_nh4}}{\partial t} = \frac{1}{dtime} (\mathbf{tracer\_flx\_vtrans\_patch}(p, id\_trc\_nh3x) * natomw + \sum_{j=1}^{ubj} \mathbf{plant\_minn\_nh4\_active\_yield\_flx\_vr\_patch}(p,j))$
$\frac{\partial s_{min\_no3}}{\partial t} = \frac{1}{dtime} (\mathbf{tracer\_flux\_vtrans\_col}(c, id_trc_no3x) * natomw + \sum_{j=1}^{ubj} \mathbf{plant\_minn\_no3\_active\_yield\_flx\_vr\_patch}(p,j))$
$\frac{\partial}{\partial t} s_{min NH4\_to\_plant\_patch} =  \frac{\mathbf{tracer\_flux\_vtrans\_patch}(p, id_trc_nh3x) * natomw}{dtime} + \sum_{j=1}^{ubj} \mathbf{plant\_minn\_nh4\_active\_yield\_flx\_vr\_patch}(p,j)$
$\frac{\partial}{\partial t} s_{min NO3\_to\_plant\_patch} =  \frac{\mathbf{tracer\_flux\_vtrans\_patch}(p, id_trc_no3x)}{\mathbf{natomw}} * \mathbf{dt} + \mathbf{dot\_product}(\mathbf{plant\_minn\_no3\_active\_yield\_flx\_vr\_patch}(p,:), \mathbf{dz}(c,:))$
$\frac{\partial}{\partial t}\left( \begin{array}{c} s_{\mathrm{NH}_4} \\ s_{\mathrm{NO}_3} \\ s_{\mathrm{P}} \end{array} \right) = \mathbf{biogeo\_fluxes}\%n14flux\_vars\smin\_nh4\_to\_plant\_patch - \mathbf{biogeo\_fluxes}\%n14flux\_vars\smin\_no3\_to\_plant\_patch + \mathbf{biogeo\_fluxes}\%p31flux\_vars\sminp\_to\_plant\_patch$
plant_minn_nh4_active_yield_flx_vr_patch (MNAP)
plant_minn_no3_active_yield_flx_vr_patch (MNP)
plant_minp_active_yield_flx_vr_patch (MP)
plant_minn_nh4_passive_yield_flx_vr_patch (MNPP)
plant_minn_no3_passive_yield_flx_vr_patch (MNPS)
tracerflux_vars%tracer_flx_vtrans_patch (TFP)
natomw (MW)
dtime (DT)
dz (DZ)

### Equations:
MNAP = TFP \* MW / DT + ∑ DZ \* YIELD
$$\text{MNAP} = \frac{\text{TFP} \cdot \text{MW}}{\text{DT}} + \sum_{j=1}^{n} \text{DZ}_j \cdot \text{YIELD}_j$$
MNAP = MNAP / WTCOL
$$\text{MNAP} = \frac{\text{MNAP}}{\text{WT COL}}$$
SMIN_NO3_TO_PLANT_PATCH = TFLX_VTRANS_PATCH * NATOMW / DT + ∑ DZ * YIELD
$$SMIN_{NO3\_TO\_PLANT\_PATCH}=\frac{TFLX\_VTRANS\_PATCH \times NATOMW}{DT}+\sum DZ \times YIELD$$
SMIN_NO3_TO_PLANT_PATCH = SMIN_NO3_TO_PLANT_PATCH / WT_COL
$$SMIN_{NO3\_TO\_PLANT\_PATCH}=\frac{SMIN_{NO3\_TO\_PLANT\_PATCH}}{WT\_COL}$$
SMIN_NH4_TO_PLANT_PATCH = (TFLX_VTRANS_PATCH × NATOMW / DT) + Σ DZ × YIELD
SMIN_NH4_TO_PLANT_PATCH = SMIN_NH4_TO_PLANT_PATCH / WT_COL
SMIN_NH4_TO_PLANT_PATCH (smin_nh4)
TFLX_VTRANS_PATCH (tflx_vtrans)
NATOMW (natomw)
DTIME (dt)
WT_COL (wtcol)
ID_TRC_NH3X (id_trc_nh3x)
ID_TRC_NO3X (id_trc_no3x)
BIOGEO_FLUXES (%n14flux_vars%smin_nh4_to_plant_patch) (betr_biogeo_flux_type%n14flux_vars%smin_nh4_to_plant_patch)
THIS %plant_minn_nh4_active_yield_flx_vr_patch  (this%plant_minn_nh4_active_yield_flx_vr_patch)

### Equations:
$\dot{\text{smin}_\text{NH}_4 \text{to plant patch}} = \frac{\text{natomw}}{\Delta t} \cdot \left( \sum_{j=1}^{u_b j} \text{tracer\_flx\_vtrans\_patch}(p, \text{id\_trc\_nh3x}) + \int_c^{\infty} \text{plant\_minn\_nh4\_active\_yield\_flux\_vr\_patch}(p, j) \cdot \Delta z_c \right)$

### Equations:

### Equations:
**Summation of som1n_vr_col over depth**: ∑[j=lbj:ubj] dz(c,j) * this%som1n_vr_col(c,j) = this%som1n_col(c)
**Summation of som2n_vr_col over depth**: ∑[j=lbj:ubj] dz(c,j) * this%som2n_vr_col(c,j) = this%som2n_col(c)
**Summation of som3n_vr_col over depth**: ∑[j=lbj:ubj] dz(c,j) * this%som3n_vr_col(c,j) = this%som3n_col(c)
**Summation of cwdn_vr_col over depth**: ∑[j=lbj:ubj] dz(c,j) * this%cwdn_vr_col(c,j) = this%cwdn_col(c)
**Summation of totlitn_vr_col over depth**: ∑[j=lbj:ubj] dz(c,j) * this%totlitn_vr_col(c,j) = this%totlitn_col(c)
**Calculation of totsomn vr_col**: this%totsomn_vr_col(c,j) = this%som1n_vr_col(c,j) + this%som2n_vr_col(c,j) +  &
**Summation of totsomn vr_col over depth**: ∑[j=lbj:ubj] dz(c,j)*this%totsomn_vr_col(c,j) = this%totsomn_col(c)
**Summation of domn_vr_col over depth**: ∑[j=lbj:ubj] this%domn_vr_col(c,j)*dz(c,j) = this%domn_col(c)
**Calculation of total soil nitrogen (totalsoin)**:  this%totsoin(c) = this%totlitn_col(c) + this%totsomn_col(c) + &
**Summation of sminn_vr_col over depth**: ∑[j=lbj:ubj] dz(c,j)*this%sminn_vr_col(c,j) = this%sminn_col(c)
**Summation of sminn_nh4_vr_col over depth**: ∑[j=lbj:ubj] dz(c,j)*this%sminn_nh4_vr_col(c,j) = this%sminn_nh4_col(c)
**Summation of sminn_no3_vr_col over depth**: ∑[j=lbj:ubj] dz(c,j)*this%sminn_no3_vr_col(c,j) = this%sminn_no3_col(c)

### Equations:
$ DOY \geq 259 : FY = c_0 $

===== File: press_grad.F90 =====

### Variables:
p (Pressure)
rdx (Short Notation for ∂x)
rdy (Short Notation for ∂y)
rdz (Short Notation for ∂z)
dudt (short notation for ∂u/∂t)
dvdt (short notation for ∂v/∂t)
dwdt (short notation for ∂w/∂t)

### Equations:
p'(i,j,k) = rho(i,k)p(i,j,k)  ! Equation in standard mathematical format
∂u/∂t(icrm,i,j,k,na) = -[p(icrm,i,j,k) - p(icrm,ib,j,k)]rdx  !
∂v/∂t(icrm,i,j,k,na) = -[p(icrm,i,j,k) - p(icrm,i,jb,k)]rdy  !
∂w/∂t(icrm,i,j,k,na) = -[p(icrm,i,j,k) - p(icrm,i,j,kb)]rdz  !

===== File: ActiveLayerMod.F90 =====

### Variables:
`c` (i)
`fc` (i)
`g` (i)
`j` (i)
`alt` (h)
`alt_indx` (n)
`altmax` (h)
`altmax_lastyear` (h)
`altmax_indx` (n)
`t_soisno` (θ)
`zsoi` (z)
`SHR_CONST_TKFRZ` (TKFRZ)
`nlevgrnd` (n)

### Equations:
h = z | θ > TKFRZ
z = z1 + ((θ - TKFRZ) / (∆θ)) * Δz
θ: soil temperature
z: active layer thickness (h)
TKFRZ: freezing point of water
z1 and z2: depths of two adjacent soil layers
∆θ: temperature difference between two adjacent soil layers
Δz: depth difference between two adjacent soil layers

===== File: advect2_mom_z.F90 =====

### Variables:
`u` (U)
`v` (V)
`w` (W)
`rho` (\(\rho\))
`rhow` (\(\rho_w\))
`adz` (\(dz\))
`adzw` (\(dzw\))
`dx` (DX)
`dy` (DY)
`dz25` (\(4D\))
`icrm` (ICRM)
`i` (I)
`j` (J)
`k` (K)
`kb` (KB)
`kc` (KC)
`na` (NA)
`ncrms` (NCRMS)

### Equations:
\(\text{UWLE}(icrm,k) = UWLE(icrm,k) + \rho_i(U(icrm,i,j,k) + U(icrm,i,j,kb))\left[W(icrm,i,j,k) + W(icrm,i-1,j,k)\right]\)
\(\text{VWLE}(icrm,k) = VWLE(icrm,k) + \rho_i(V(icrm,i,j,k) + V(icrm,i,j,kb))\left[W(icrm,i,j,k) + W(icrm,i,j-1,k)\right]\)
\(F_{uz}(icrm,i,j,k) = \frac{1}{4D}[\rho_i(W(icrm,i,j,k) + W(icrm,i-1,j,k))](U(icrm,i,j,k) + U(icrm,i,j,kb))\)
\(F_{vz}(icrm,i,j,k) = \frac{1}{4D}[\rho_i(W(icrm,i,j,k) + W(icrm,i,j-1,k))](V(icrm,i,j,k) + V(icrm,i,j,kb))\)
\(F_{uz}(icrm,i,j,k) = WW\left(U(icrm,i,j,k) + U(icrm,i,j,kb)\right)\)
\(F_{vz}(icrm,i,j,k) = WW\left(V(icrm,i,j,k) + V(icrm,i,j,kb)\right)\)
\(\frac{D}{dt}U(icrm,i,j,k,na) = \frac{D}{dt}U(icrm,i,j,k,na) - (F_{uz}(icrm,i,j,kc) - F_{uz}(icrm,i,j,k))\rho_i\)
\(\frac{D}{dt}V(icrm,i,j,k,na) = \frac{D}{dt}V(icrm,i,j,k,na) - (F_{vz}(icrm,i,j,kc) - F_{vz}(icrm,i,j,k))\rho_i\)
\(F_wz(icrm,i,j,k) = \frac{1}{4D}\left[W(icrm,i,j,kc)\rho_w(kc) + W(icrm,i,j,k)\rho_w(k)\right]\left(W(icrm,i,j,kc) + W(icrm,i,j,k)\right)\)
\(\frac{D}{dt}W(icrm,i,j,k,na) = \frac{D}{dt}W(icrm,i,j,k,na) - (F_wz(icrm,i,j,k) - F_wz(icrm,i,j,kb))\rho_i\)

$\frac{dA}{dx} = \left\{\begin{array}{ll} 0 & x < a1\\ \frac{(y(k+1) - y(k))}{(x(k+1) - x(k))}(b2-b1)/(a2-a1) & a1 \leq x \leq a2 \\ 0 & x > a2\end{array}\right.$ (Equation 1)

===== File: stats.F90 =====

### Variables:
`psurf` (Psrf)
`rmsz` (RmsZ)
`rmsd` (RmsD)
`rmst` (RmSt)
`stq` (StQ)
`pstar` (Pstr)
`prat` (Prat)
`w` (W)
`lat` (Lat)
`i` (I)
`k` (K)
`nlon` (Nl)

### Equations:
$\boxed{\text{Psrf}( \text{Lat} ) = \frac{1}{\text{Nl}} \sum_{\text{i}=0}^{\text{Nl}-1} \text{Pstr}(\text{i})}$
Psurf (at latitude index Lat) equals the average of surface pressure values Pstar(i), for all i from 1 to nlon.
$\boxed{\text{RmsZ}( \text{Lat} ) = \frac{\text{W}(\text{Lat})}{\text{Nl}} \sum_{\text{i}=0}^{\text{Nl}-1} \left( \text{Vort}(\text{i}, \text{k})^{2} \right) \cdot \frac{\text{Pdel}(\text{i}, \text{k})}{\text{Pint}(\text{i}, \text{Plevp})}}$
RmsZ (at latitude index Lat) equals the weighted average of the squared vorticity values, for all i and k.
$\boxed{\text{RmsD}( \text{Lat} ) = \frac{\text{W}(\text{Lat})}{\text{Nl}} \sum_{\text{i}=0}^{\text{Nl}-1} \left( \text{Div}(\text{i}, \text{k})^{2} \right) \cdot \frac{\text{Pdel}(\text{i}, \text{k})}{\text{Pint}(\text{i}, \text{Plevp})}}$
RmsD (at latitude index Lat) equals the weighted average of the squared divergence values, for all i and k.
$\boxed{\text{RmSt}( \text{Lat} ) = \frac{\text{W}(\text{Lat})}{\text{Nl}} \sum_{\text{i}=0}^{\text{Nl}-1} \left( \text{T}(\text{i}, \text{k})^{2} \right) \cdot \frac{\text{Pdel}(\text{i}, \text{k})}{\text{Pint}(\text{i}, \text{Plevp})}}$
RmSt (at latitude index Lat) equals the weighted average of the squared temperature values, for all i and k.
$\boxed{\text{StQ}( \text{Lat} ) = \frac{\text{W}(\text{Lat})}{\text{Nl}} \sum_{\text{i}=0}^{\text{Nl}-1} \left( \text{q}(\text{i}, \text{k}) \cdot \text{Pdel}(\text{i}, \text{k}) \right)}$
StQ (at latitude index Lat) equals the weighted average of moisture values multiplied by pressure differences, for all i and k.

### Equations:
$ prod = \exp_prod_loss(base_sol, reaction_rates, het_rates)$
This equation is represented by the subroutine call `call exp_prod_loss( prod, loss, base_sol, reaction_rates, het_rates )`.
$ \frac{\partial base_sol}{\partial t} = (prod + ind_prd - loss) \cdot delt $
This equation is represented by the loop over chemical species:
$ prod + ind_prd = \exp_prod_loss(base_sol, reaction_rates, het_rates)$
This equation is represented by the call to `indprd` and `exp_prod_loss`.
$ wrk = (prod + ind_prd) \cdot xhnm $
This equation is represented by the assignment statement:
$ wrk = loss \cdot xhnm $

===== File: limdz.F90 =====

### Variables:

### Equations:
$\frac{\partial f}{\partial z} \bigg\rvert_{k+1,m} - \frac{\partial f}{\partial z} \bigg\rvert_{k,m} = R\cdot \Delta f(k,m)$  (equation for `deli`)
$FST(i,k,m) = TANH(3\epsilon F)\cdot DELI(i)$  (limiter function)
$FSB(i,k,m) = TANH(3\epsilon F)\cdot DELI(i)$  (limiter function)
$\text{if} \left(\frac{\partial f}{\partial z}\right\rvert_{k+1,m}\cdot FST(i,k,m) \leq 0)\: FS1 = 0$ (limiting `fst` to zero when derivative changes sign)
$\text{if} \left(\frac{\partial f}{\partial z}\right\rvert_{k+1,m}\cdot FSB(i,k,m) \leq 0)\: FS2 = 0$ (limiting `fsb` to zero when derivative changes sign)
$FST(i,k,m) = \text{max}\left(\left|FST\right|, TANH(3\epsilon F)\cdot DELI(i)\right)$ (limiter function for `fst`)
$FSB(i,k,m) = \text{max}\left(\left|FSB\right|, TANH(3\epsilon F)\cdot DELI(i)\right)$ (limiter function for `fsb`)

`krg = kr * C` [equation 2]
`dkr_dP_up = dkr_up_dP_up * C` [equation 3]
`dkr_dP_dn = dkr_dn_dP_dn * C` [equation 4]
`dkrg_dP_up = dkr_dP_up * krg` [equation 5]
`krg_up          = kr_up * Cu`   [equation 6]
`krg_dn          = kr_dn * Cd`   [equation 7]
`denom           = krg_up + krg_dn`  [equation 8]
`dkrg_dP_up = (krg_dn/denom)**2.d0 * dkr_up_dP_up * Cu` [equation 9]
`dkrg_dP_dn = (krg_up/denom)**2.d0 * dkr_dn_dP_dn * Cd` [equation 10]

================================================================================

### Equations:
$T_{sf} = sst + T_{fresh}$ (Temperature of the ocean surface)
$swabs = \left(1 - albedo\right) * swvdr + \left(1 - albocn\right) * swidr + \left(1 - albedo\right) * swvdf + \left(1 - albocn\right) * swidf$ (Shortwave radiative flux absorbed by the ocean surface)
$flwout_ocn = - \sigma_{SB} T_{sf}^4$ (Longwave radiative flux leaving the ocean surface)
$fsens_ocn = shcoef \cdot delt$ (Downward sensible heat flux)
$flat_ocn = lhcoef \cdot delq$ (Downward latent heat flux)
$\frac{\partial sst}{\partial t} = \left(\frac{fsens_ocn + flat_ocn + flwout_ocn + flw + swabs}{c_{prho}h_{mix}} - aice \cdot f_{hocn} - aice \cdot f_{swthru}\right) c_{prho} h_{mix}$ (Heat balance equation for the ocean mixed layer)
$qdp = 0$ if $\frac{\partial sst}{\partial t} \leq 0$
$T_f \geq sst$ implies $\frac{\partial sst}{\partial t} = - qdp c_{prho} h_{mix}$ (Heat exchange between the ocean mixed layer and deeper layers)
$frzmlt = T_f - sst$ if $T_f > sst$
$frzmlt = \frac{-c_{prho}h_{mix}}{dt}\left(T_f - sst\right)$
$frzmlt = min(max(frzmlt, frzmlt\_max), -frzmlt\_max)$ (Maximum magnitude of potential to freeze or melt ice)

===== File: lcdbas.F90 =====

### Variables:
x1 (x_1)
x2 (x_2)
x3 (x_3)
x4 (x_4)
x1mx2 (x_1-x_2)
x1mx3 (x_1-x_3)
x1mx4 (x_1-x_4)
x2mx3 (x_2-x_3)
x2mx4 (x_2-x_4)
x3mx4 (x_3-x_4)

### Equations:
$\frac{\left(x_{2} - x_{3}\right)\left(x_{2} - x_{4}\right)}{x_{1}x_{2}\left(x_{1} - x_{3}\right)\left(x_{1} - x_{4}\right)} = \frac{x_2}{x_1mx2}$ (dbas2(1))
$\frac{1}{\left(x_{1} - x_{2}\right)} + \frac{1}{\left(x_{3} - x_{2}\right)} + \frac{1}{\left(x_{4} - x_{2}\right)} = 2(\frac{x_1}{x_2mx3}+\frac{x_1}{x_2mx4})$ (dbas2(2))
$\frac{\left(x_{1} - x_{2}\right)\left(x_{2} - x_{4}\right)}{x_{1}\left(x_{3} - x_{2}\right)\left(x_{3} - x_{4}\right)} = \frac{x_2}{x_3mx4}$ (dbas2(3))
$\frac{\left(x_{1} - x_{2}\right)\left(x_{3} - x_{2}\right)}{x_{1}x_{3}\left(x_{4} - x_{2}\right)} = \frac{x_2}{x_4mx3}$ (dbas2(4))
$\frac{\left(x_{3} - x_{2}\right)\left(x_{3} - x_{4}\right)}{x_{1}x_{2}\left(x_{1} - x_{3}\right)} = \frac{x_3}{x_1mx2}$ (dbas3(1))
$\frac{\left(x_{1} - x_{3}\right)\left(x_{3} - x_{4}\right)}{x_{1}x_{2}\left(x_{2} - x_{3}\right)} = \frac{x_3}{x_2mx3}$ (dbas3(2))
$\frac{1}{\left(x_{1} - x_{3}\right)} + \frac{1}{\left(x_{3} - x_{2}\right)} - \frac{1}{\left(x_{4} - x_{3}\right)} = 2(\frac{x_3}{x_1mx4}-\frac{x_3}{x_3mx4})$ (dbas3(3))
$\frac{\left(x_{1} - x_{3}\right)\left(x_{2} - x_{3}\right)}{x_{1}x_{2}\left(x_{4} - x_{3}\right)} = \frac{x_3}{x_4mx2}$ (dbas3(4))

P(:,:,1) = 0

P(1) = 0

P(4) = 0

### Equations:
$\boxed{\frac{d}{dt}u3s_{i,j,k} = dua_{i,k,j} + dua_{i,k,j-1}}$
Equation in standard mathematical format, using subscript and superscript
Note: This equation is represented as a simple addition of `dua` values at different grid points.
$\boxed{\frac{d}{dt}v3s_{i,j,k} = dva_{i,k,j} + dva_{i-1,k,j}}$
Equation in standard mathematical format, using subscript and superscript
Note: This equation is represented as a simple addition of `dva` values at different grid points.
$\boxed{\frac{d}{dt}u3s_{i,jfirstxy,k} = dua_{i,k,jfirstxy} + duasouth_i,k}$ (only valid when jfirstxy > 1)
Equation in standard mathematical format, using subscript and superscript
Note: This equation is represented as a simple addition of `dua` values at different grid points.
$\boxed{\frac{d}{dt}v3s_{ifirstxy,j,k} = dvawest_k,j + dva_{ifirstxy,k,j}}$
Equation in standard mathematical format, using subscript and superscript
Note: This equation is represented as a simple addition of `dvawest` and `dva` values at different grid points.
$\boxed{u3s(i,j,k) = u3s(i,j,k) + dt5 \left( dua(i,k,j)+dua(i,k,j-1)\right)}$
Equation in standard mathematical format, using subscript and superscript
Note: This equation is a simple update of `u3s` value based on the addition of `dua` values at different grid points.
$\boxed{v3s(ifirstxy,j,k) = v3s(ifirstxy,j,k) + dt5 \left( dvawest_k,j+dva_{ifirstxy,k,j}\right)}$
Equation in standard mathematical format, using subscript and superscript
Note: This equation is a simple update of `v3s` value based on the addition of `dvawest` and `dva` values at different grid points.
$\boxed{fu3s(i,j,k) = \frac{u3s(i,j,k)-tmp}{2\cdot dt5}}$
Equation in standard mathematical format, using subscript and superscript
Note: This equation is a simple update of `fu3s` value based on the difference of `u3s` and `tmp` values divided by `2*dt5`.
$\boxed{fv3s(ifirstxy,j,k) = \frac{v3s(ifirstxy,j,k)-tmp}{2\cdot dt5}}$
Equation in standard mathematical format, using subscript and superscript
Note: This equation is a simple update of `fv3s` value based on the difference of `v3s` and `tmp` values divided by `2*dt5`.

===== File: moist_planar.F90 =====

### Variables:
Hybrid Vertical Coordinate (HVCOORD)
`h` (height)
`p` (pressure)
`T` (temperature)
`q` (specific humidity)
`u` (zonal wind component)
`v` (meridional wind component)

### Equations:
Hydrostatic Balance
$\frac{\partial p}{\partial z} = -\rho g$
where $g$ is the acceleration due to gravity
Continuity Equation
$\frac{\partial \rho}{\partial t} + \nabla \cdot (\rho \mathbf{v}) = 0$
where $\mathbf{v}$ is the wind vector
Thermodynamic Relations
$p = p_0 \exp\left(-\int\limits_z^{\eta} \frac{\partial \ln T}{\partial z'} dz'\right)$
where $p_0$ is a reference pressure and $\eta$ is the height of the tropopause
Moisture Advection Equation
$\frac{\partial q}{\partial t} + u\frac{\partial q}{\partial x} + v\frac{\partial q}{\partial y} = \frac{1}{\rho}\nabla \cdot (K_{h}\nabla q)$
where $K_{h}$ is the eddy diffusivity coefficient for humidity

===== File: decomp_def.F90 =====

### Variables:

### Equations:
$il\_imjm = Im \cdot Jm$
$I_pj = \frac{Jm}{Npe}$
$if \: Rk < Npe - 1$:
$P_{11} = P_{21} = 1$

$P_{31} = I_pj \cdot Im$

$P_{21} = Rk \cdot I_{pj} \cdot Im$
$P_{31} = Im^2 - Rk \cdot I_{pj} \cdot Im$
$if \: Rk < Npe - 1$:

$else$:

$P_2 = P_1 = 2$
$P_3 = P_5 = Im$
$P_5 = Npe$

===== File: dynpkg.F90 =====

### Variables:
`ztodt` (Δt)
`etadot` (∂η/∂t)
`vcour` (Courant number)
`vmax2d` (Maximum wind speed at each level and latitude)
`vmax2dt` (Truncated maximum wind speed at each level and latitude)

### Equations:
∂η/∂t = wfldh [at single column mode]
Equation in standard mathematical format: η̇ = wfldh
∂(u,v)/∂t = grfu1, grfv1 [from dyndrv subroutine]
Equation in standard mathematical format: u̇ = ∑[grfu1], v̇ = ∑[grfv1]
Courant number: vcour = (Δx/Δt) / a
Where: Δx is grid spacing, Δt is time step, and 'a' might be wind speed or another parameter depending on the context.
Maximum wind speed at each level and latitude: vmax2d = max[|u|, |v|]
Equation in standard mathematical format: vmax2d = max(|u|, |v|)
Truncated maximum wind speed at each level and latitude: vmax2dt = max[|ū|, |v̄|]
Where: ū and v̄ are truncated wind speeds. The exact definition of 'truncated' is not provided in the code.

### Equations:
$\tau_{mc} = 0$
$cwp = ciwpmc + clwpmc$
$\text{if } cldfmc \geq \text{cnd}\text{ and } (\text{cwp} \geq \text{cnd} \lor \tau_{mc} \geq \text{cnd})$
$\text{if } inflag = 0$
$cwp = ciwpmc + clwpmc$
$\text{if } inflag = 1$
$\text{if } inflag = 2$
$radice = reicmc(lay)$
$\text{if } ciwpmc(ig,lay) = 0.0_r8$
$\text{if } inflag = 2\text{ and } iceflag = 0$
$abscoice(ig) = absice0(1) + absice0(2)/radice$
$\text{if } inflag = 2\text{ and } iceflag = 1$
$ncbands = 5$
$abscoice(ig) = absice1(1,ib) + absice1(2,ib)/radice$
$\text{if } inflag = 2\text{ and } iceflag = 2$
$factor = (radice - 2._r8)/3._r8$
$index = int(factor)$
$fint = factor - float(index)$
$ib = ngb(ig)$
$abscoice(ig) = absice2(index,ib) + fint * (absice2(index+1,ib) - (absice2(index,ib)))$
$\text{if } inflag = 2\text{ and } iceflag = 3$
$ncbands = 16$
$abscoice(ig) = absice3(index,ib) + fint * (absice3(index+1,ib) - (absice3(index,ib)))$
$abscoice(ig) = absice0(1) + absice0(2)/radice$
$abscoice(ig) = absice1(1,ib) + absice1(2,ib)/radice$
$abscoice(ig) = absice2(index,ib) + fint * (absice2(index+1,ib) - (absice2(index,ib)))$
$abscoice(ig) = abscoice(ig) + fint * (abscoice(index+1,ig) - (abscoice(index,ig)))$
$abscoliq(ig) = absliq0$
$abscoliq(ig) = absliq1(index,ib) + fint * (absliq1(index+1,ib) - (absliq1(index,ib)))$
$taucmc(ig,lay) = ciwpmc(ig,lay) * abscoice(ig) + clwpmc(ig,lay) * abscoliq(ig)$

if $H \neq ''$ then:
if $\text{TL} = 'gz'$ then:
$\text{FLN} = \text{HD}$
else:
$\text{FLN} = B$
If $H \neq ''$ then:
if $\text{TL} = 'gz'$ then:
execute command $\text{CMD} = 'gunzip -f F'$
else:
if $F$ does not exist:
execute command $\text{CMD} = \text{remote_cp} + ' F . H'$
Else:
If $F$ does not exist then:
execute command $\text{CMD} = \text{gunzip} + ' F > HD'$

$\sum_{i=1}^{NPEsComp-1} \frac{BlockLen}{2} + BlockLen\left(1 - NPEsComp^{-1}\right) = Nactual$ (Blen)
$Dist(i) = \lfloor \frac{NPEsComp-1-i+1}{NPEsComp-1} \cdot BlockLen + 1 \rfloor, \quad i=1,...,NPEsComp-1$
$\sum_{i=1}^{NPEsComp} Dist(i) = Nactual$ (Dist)
$Xdist(i) = Ydist(i) = \lfloor \frac{BlockLen}{2} + 1 \rfloor, \quad i=1,...,NPEsComp-1$
$\sum_{i=1}^{NPEsComp} Xdist(i) = BlockLen$ (Xdist)
$\sum_{i=1}^{NPEsComp} Ydist(i) = Nactual$ (Ydist)
$Perm(i) = NPEsComp - i + 1, \quad i=1,...,NPEsComp$
$Local = \left\lfloor \frac{K-1}{NPEsComp-1} \cdot Dist(Pe+1) \right\rfloor$
$Global = Local \cdot (NPEsComp - 1) + Pe + 1$
$Iglobal = \mod(Kglobal-1,Nactual)+1$
$Jglobal = \left\lfloor \frac{Kglobal-1}{Nactual} \right\rfloor+1$

### Equations:
Retrieves the type of an object.
The type is stored in `ty` which can be one of the following: `H5I_FILE_F`, `H5I_GROUP_F`, `H5I_DATATYPE_F`, `H5I_DATASPACE_F`, `H5I_DATASET_F`, `H5I_ATTR_F`, or `H5I_BADID_F`.
Gets a name of an object specified by its idetifier.
The length of the returned name is stored in `namesz` and truncated if `buf` buffer is not big enough.
Increments the reference count of an ID.
The new reference count is stored in `refc`.
Decrements the reference count of an ID.
The new reference count is stored in `refc` and may be zero.
Retrieves the reference count of an ID.
The current reference count is stored in `refc`.
Obtains file identifier from the object identifier.
The obtained file identifier is stored in `fid`.
Check if an ID is valid without producing an error message.
If the ID is valid then `val` is `.TRUE.` otherwise it is `.FALSE.`.

### Equations:
`leaf_long(t+1) = leaf_long(t) - (senleaf_long_fdrought * leaf_long(t))`
`root_long = turnover_nitr_retrans(:,1)`
`branch_long = (turnover_phos_retrans(:,2) * root_long)`
`grperc = grperc_organ`
`nitr_stoich_p1(:,i) * leaf_long(i) + nitr_store_ratio * senleaf_long_fdrought = 0`
`phos_stoich_p1(:,i) * leaf_long(i) + phos_store_ratio * senleaf_long_fdrought = 0`
`alloc_priority(:,i) = alloc_priority_max - alloc_priority_min * (leaf_stor_priority * leaf_long(i))`
`dbh_repro_threshold = dbh_reprod_threshold_func(alloc_priority)`
`nitr_recr_stoich(i) * leaf_long(i) + phos_recr_stoich(i) * senleaf_long_fdrought = 0`
`allom_d2bl1(i) * dbh_repro_threshold(i)^2 + allom_d2bl2(i) * dbh_repro_threshold(i) + allom_d2bl3(i) = 0`
`allom_d2ca_coefficient(i) = allom_d2ca_coefficient_max(i) - (allom_d2ca_coefficient_min(i) * (crown_depth_frac(i)^2))`

### Equations:
$A \cdot X = B$
Variables: A (csr_dbl_a), X (dbl_soln), B (dbl_rhs)
Equation: $A_{i, j} X_j = B_i$
$L \cdot Y = B$
Variables: L (prev_lu), Y (tempvec)
Equation: $L_{i, j} Y_j = B_i$
$M \cdot Y = A \cdot X$
Variables: M (csr_dbl_a), X (dbl_soln), Y (temp)
Equation: $M_{i,j} Y_j = (A_{i,k} X_k)_{i=1}^{numeqns}, j=1^{tempsize}$
$(I-M) \cdot Y = B$
Variables: I (identity matrix), M (csr_dbl_a), Y (tempvec), B (dbl_rhs)
Equation: $((\delta_{ij}-M_{i,j})Y_j)_{i=1}^{numeqns} = (\delta_{ik}B_k)_{k=1}^{numeqns}$

### Equations:
**Matrix Equation:** `A^{-1}v = w`, where `A` is the preconditioner matrix, `v` is the input vector, and `w` is the output vector.

### Equations:

numeqns (numeqns)

### Equations:
A is an $n\times n$ matrix, where $n=numeqns$
dbl_rhs
dbl_soln

### Equations:
$\sum\limits_{i=1}^{n} a_i x_i = b \quad \rightarrow \quad A \mathbf{x} = \mathbf{b}$

### Equations:
$TR = 0 \rightarrow ES = SUCC$
$TR < 0 \rightarrow ES = FAIL$
$\text{DIM1} = 1 \rightarrow SDT(DID, DSID) = -1$
$\text{DIM2} = 2 \rightarrow SDT(DID, DSID) = 0$
$\text{DIM1} \neq 20 \rightarrow \text{No Error}$
$SDT(DID, DSID, DIM1) = 0 \rightarrow IS_ATTACHED = .TRUE.$
$IS_ATTACHED = .FALSE. \land SDT(DID, DIM2) < 0 \rightarrow ERR = -1$
$\text{DIM1} > 0 \rightarrow NS = 1$
$NS > 1 \lor \text{DIM1} \neq 2 \rightarrow ERR = FAL$
$SDT(DID, DSID, DIM1) < 0 \land IS_ATTACHED = .FALSE. \rightarrow ERR = -1$
$\text{DIM1} > 0 \land SDT(DID, DSID, DIM1) < 0 \rightarrow IS_SCALE = .TRUE.$
$IS_SCALE \neq .TRUE. \lor SDT(DID, DSID) < 0 \rightarrow ERR = FAL$
$SDT(DID, DIM2) < 0 \land LEN(NM) \leq 21 \rightarrow ERR = -1$
$LEN(NM) > 21 \land SDT(DID, DIM2) < 0 \rightarrow ERR = -1$
$\text{LEN}(NM) < 5 \lor SDT(DID, DIM2) < 0 \rightarrow ERR = FAL$
$\text{LEN}(LB) \leq 7 \lor LB \neq "Label12" \land SDT(DID, DIM2) < 0 \rightarrow ERR = -1$

===== File: mean_module.F90 =====

### Variables:
D0_0 (0)
grid%im (n)
grid%jm (m)
i (index, n)
j (index, m)
im (number of x cells)
jm (number of y cells)
jfirst (first y index)
jlast (last y index)
q (2D field, real(r8))
qmean (mean value, real(r8))
xsum (partial sum, real(r8))
w (weight array, real(r8))

### Equations:
∑[w(j) * ∑[q(i,j)] from j=jfirst to jlast] = xsum (j)
qmean = ∑[xsum(j)] from j=1 to jm
∑[w(j) * ∑[q(i,j)] from j=jfirst to jlast] = xsum (global)
qmean = (∑[xsum(j)] from j=1 to jm) / (2*im)

### Variables:

### Equations:
$\text{Allocate}~\text{this}\%qflx\_tran\_veg(begp:endp) = \text{nan}$
$\text{Allocate}~\text{this}\%qflx\_rootsoi(begp:endp,lbj:ubj) = \text{nan}$

`T` indicates that the variable is a type (e.g. `pf_waterflux_type`)
`B` indicates that the variable is a bounds type
`BP` indicates that the variable is an integer representing a range of points (`begp`, `endp`)
`LU` indicates that the variables are integers representing layer or block indices (`lbj`, `ubj`)
`(subscript)` indicates that the variable is used as a subscript for arrays (e.g. `n` in `qflx\_tran\_veg}(n)`)

===== File: GrowthRespMod.F90 =====

### Variables:
`num_soilp` (N)
`filter_soilp`
`ivt` (Iv)
`woody` (W)
`grperc` (Gp)
`grpnow` (Gr)
`np cropmin` (Nc)
`cpool_to_leafc` (Cl)
`cpool_to_frootc` (Cf)
`cpool_to_livestemc` (Clv)
`cpool_to_deadstemc` (Cd)
`cpool_to_livecrootc` (Clr)
`cpool_to_deadcrootc` (Cdcr)
`cpool_to_grainc` (Cg)
`grainc_xfer_to_grainc` (Xgg)
`leafc_xfer_to_leafc` (Xcl)
`frootc_xfer_to_frootc` (Xcf)
`livestemc_xfer_to_livestemc` (Xclv)
`deadstemc_xfer_to_deadstemc` (Xcd)
`livecrootc_xfer_to_livecrootc` (Xclr)
`deadcrootc_xfer_to_deadcrootc` (Xdcr)
`cpool_grain_gr` (Cgg)
`cpool_leaf_gr` (Clg)
`cpool_froot_gr` (Cgf)
`cpool_livestem_gr` (Clgv)
`cpool_deadstem_gr` (Cdgv)
`cpool_livecroot_gr` (Clrvg)
`cpool_deadcroot_gr` (Cdcrvg)

### Equations:
$\boxed{Gp \cdot C_{leaf} = Cl_{leaf}}$
$Gp$: growth proportionality constant, $C_{leaf}$: leaf carbon pool
$\boxed{Gr \cdot Gp \cdot Cl_{leaf\_storage} = C_{leaf\_storage}}$
$Gr$: grain storage proportionality constant, $C_{leaf\_storage}$: leaf carbon pool storage
$\boxed{(1 - Gr) \cdot X_{cl} \cdot Gp \cdot Cl_{leaf} = G_{leaf}}$
$X_{cl}$: leaf carbon growth rate from storage, $G_{leaf}$: leaf growth respiration flux
$\boxed{Gp \cdot C_{froot} = Cf_{froot}}$
$Cf$: fine root carbon pool
$\boxed{Gr \cdot Gp \cdot Cf_{froot\_storage} = C_{froot\_storage}}$
$Cf_{froot\_storage}$: fine root carbon pool storage
$\boxed{(1 - Gr) \cdot X_{cf} \cdot Gp \cdot C_{froot} = G_{froot}}$
$X_{cf}$: fine root carbon growth rate from storage, $G_{froot}$: fine root growth respiration flux
$\boxed{W \cdot Iv \rightarrow (Gp \cdot C_{livestem})_{woody} = Clv_{livestem}}$
$W$: woody flag, $Iv$: pft vegetation type, $Clv$: live stem carbon pool (for woody types)
$\boxed{Gr \cdot Gp \cdot Clv_{livestem\_storage} = C_{livestem\_storage}}$
$C_{livestem\_storage}$: live stem carbon pool storage
$\boxed{(1 - Gr) \cdot X_{clv} \cdot Gp \cdot Clv_{livestem} = G_{livestem}}$
$X_{clv}$: live stem carbon growth rate from storage, $G_{livestem}$: live stem growth respiration flux
$\boxed{W \cdot Iv \rightarrow (Gp \cdot C_{deadstem})_{woody} = Cd_{deadstem}}$
$Cd$: dead stem carbon pool (for woody types)
$\boxed{Gr \cdot Gp \cdot Cd_{deadstem\_storage} = C_{deadstem\_storage}}$
$C_{deadstem\_storage}$: dead stem carbon pool storage
$\boxed{(1 - Gr) \cdot X_{cd} \cdot Gp \cdot Cd_{deadstem} = G_{deadstem}}$
$X_{cd}$: dead stem carbon growth rate from storage, $G_{deadstem}$: dead stem growth respiration flux
$\boxed{W \cdot Iv \rightarrow (Gp \cdot C_{livecroot})_{woody} = Clv_{livecroot}}$
$Clv$: live coarse root carbon pool (for woody types)
$\boxed{Gr \cdot Gp \cdot Clv_{livecroot\_storage} = C_{livecroot\_storage}}$
$C_{livecroot\_storage}$: live coarse root carbon pool storage
$\boxed{(1 - Gr) \cdot X_{clv} \cdot Gp \cdot Clv_{livecroot} = G_{livecroot}}$
$X_{clv}$: live coarse root carbon growth rate from storage, $G_{livecroot}$: live coarse root growth respiration flux
$\boxed{W \cdot Iv \rightarrow (Gp \cdot C_{deadcroot})_{woody} = Cd_{deadcroot}}$
$Cd$: dead coarse root carbon pool (for woody types)
$\boxed{Gr \cdot Gp \cdot Cd_{deadcroot\_storage} = C_{deadcroot\_storage}}$
$C_{deadcroot\_storage}$: dead coarse root carbon pool storage
$\boxed{(1 - Gr) \cdot X_{cd} \cdot Gp \cdot Cd_{deadcroot} = G_{deadcroot}}$
$X_{cd}$: dead coarse root carbon growth rate from storage, $G_{deadcroot}$: dead coarse root growth respiration flux

===== File: mktopostatsMod.F90 =====

### Variables:
`ldomain` (LDOMAIN)
`mapfname` (MAPFNAME)
`datfname` (DATFNAME)
`ndiag` (NDIAG)
`topo_stddev_o` (TSDO)
`slope_o` (SLPO)
`tgridmap` (TGGRIDMAP)
`tdomain` (TDOMAIN)
`data_i` (DI)
`ncid` (NCID)
`varid` (VARIID)
`ier` (IER)
`min_valid_topo_stddev` (MVTSD)
`min_valid_slope` (MVSLP)
`max_valid_slope` (MXVSL)
`subname` (SUBNAM)

### Equations:
The standard deviation of elevation is calculated as $\text{TSDO}_i = \sqrt{\frac{1}{\text{DI}_i}} \sum_{j=1}^{\text{TDOMAIN}\%ns} (\text{ELEVATION}_j - \bar{\text{ELEVATION}})^2$ where $\bar{\text{ELEVATION}}$ is the mean elevation.
Since this equation does not directly appear in the code, we assume that it is used by `gridmap_areastddev` function: $\text{TSDO}_i = \sqrt{\frac{1}{\text{DI}_i}} \sum_{j=1}^{\text{TDOMAIN}\%ns} (\text{ELEVATION}_j - \bar{\text{ELEVATION}})^2$
The slope is calculated as $\text{SLPO}_i = \frac{\Delta\text{SLOPE}}{\Delta x}$ where $\Delta\text{SLOPE}$ and $\Delta x$ are the differences in slope and $x$ coordinates.
Since this equation does not directly appear in the code, we assume that it is used by `gridmap_areaave` function: $\text{SLPO}_i = \frac{\Delta\text{SLOPE}}{\Delta x}$

===== File: mkurbanparCommonMod.F90 =====

### Variables:
`MIN_DENS` (min_dens)
`sum_fldi` (sumfldi)
`sum_fldo` (sumfldo)
`relerr` (relerr)
`ni` (ni), `no` (no), `k` (k), `n` (n)
`gurbn_i` (gurbi)
`garea_i` (gaiai)
`gurbn_o` (gurbo)
`garea_o` (gaeao)

### Equations:
$MIN\_DENS = 0.1$ $\quad\left(\text{Minimum urban density}\right)$
$\sum_{ni=1}^{tdomain%ns} tgridmap\%area_src(ni) \cdot tgridmap\%frac_src(ni) = sum_fldi$
$\sum_{no=1}^{ldomain%ns} tgridmap\%area_dst(no) \cdot tgridmap\%frac_dst(no) = sum_fldo$
$\left| \frac{\sum_{no=1}^{ldomain%ns} tgridmap\%area_dst(no) \cdot tgridmap\%frac_dst(no)}{\sum_{ni=1}^{tdomain%ns} tgridmap\%area_src(ni) \cdot tgridmap\%frac_src(ni)} - 1 \right| < relerr$
$gurbn_i = \sum_{ni=1}^{tdomain%ns} urbn_i(ni) \cdot (tgridmap\%area_src(ni)/100._r8) \cdot tgridmap\%frac_src(ni) \cdot re^2$
$gurbn_o = \sum_{no=1}^{ldomain%ns} urbn_o(no) \cdot (tgridmap\%area_dst(no)/100._r8) \cdot tgridmap\%frac_dst(no) \cdot re^2$
$garea_i = \sum_{ni=1}^{tdomain%ns} (tgridmap\%area_src(ni)) \cdot re^2$
$garea_o = \sum_{no=1}^{ldomain%ns} tgridmap\%area_dst(no) \cdot re^2$

===== File: cpslec.F90 =====

### Variables:
`ncol` (n)
`pmid` (p_mid)
`phis` (φ)
`ps` (p_s)
`t` (T)
`gravit` (g)
`rair` (R_d)
`xlapse` (γ)
`alpha`
`i`
`Tstar`
`TT0`
`alph`
`beta`
`psl` (p_sl)

### Equations:
$\alpha = \frac{R_d \gamma}{g}$
$T^* = T \left(1 + \alpha \left(\frac{p_s}{p_{mid}} - 1\right)\right)$
$T_0 = T^* + \frac{\gamma \phi}{g}$
If $T^* \leq 290.5 K$ and $T_0 > 290.5 K$: $a = R_d / (\phi (290.5 - T^*))$
If $T^* > 290.5 K$ and $T_0 > 290.5 K$: $a = 0$
If $T_0 < 255 K$: $T^* = \frac{1}{2} (255 + T)$
$\beta = \frac{\phi}{R_d T^*}$
$p_{sl} = p_s \exp(\beta(1 - a\beta/2 + (a\beta)^3/3))$

NATM (natomw)

R    (real(r8))
I    (integer)

### Equations:
$\frac{\partial NHAR}{\partial t} = \frac{TRFP_{NH3}}{NATM} \cdot \frac{1}{DTIME} + \sum_{c=1}^{C} DZ_c \cdot \sum_{j=1}^{J} TRFP(c,j)$
$\frac{\partial NHAR}{\partial t}$ is the time derivative of nitrogen yield to plant
$TRFP_{NH3}$ is the flux of ammonia through plant roots
$NATM$ is atomic mass of nitrogen
$DTIME$ is simulation time step
$DZ_c$ is depth of column c
$C$ is number of columns
$J$ is number of patches in a column
$\frac{\partial NO3AR}{\partial t} = \sum_{c=1}^{C}\sum_{j=1}^{J}(NO3FLX_{c,j}-MINPFLX_{c,j})$
$NO3AR$ is the time derivative of nitrate yield to plant
$\sum_{c=1}^{C}$ denotes summation over all columns
$\sum_{j=1}^{J}$ denotes summation over all patches in a column
$NO3FLX_{c,j}$ is the depth-resolved flux of nitrate into soil at column c and patch j
$MINPFLX_{c,j}$ is the depth-resolved flux of mineral phosphorus into soil at column c and patch j
VariableName (Short Notation)
bounds%begc, bounds%endc, begp, endp (BEGC, ENDC, BEGP, ENDP)

===== File: elm_initializeMod.F90 =====

### Variables:
`bounds` (B)
`elm_inst` (I)
`decompMod` (D)
`elm_instMod` (E)
The `elm_inst` subroutine call likely has its own set of internal variables and calculations. If we assume that this subroutine involves some mathematical operations with the provided variables (`bounds`) from the `decompMod` and `elm_instMod`, we can try to extract those equations by looking at the implementation details of `elm_inst`.
The use of modules like `decompMod` and `elm_instMod` might also involve various calculations or operations that could be represented as equations. However, without more information about these modules' contents, it's difficult to provide a direct representation.

===== File: spehox.F90 =====

### Variables:

### Equations:
$logp = log_{10}(ionp)$
$\alpha_1(z) = \frac{z - alts(i)}{alts(i+1) - alts(i)}$
$\alpha_2(z) = 1 - \alpha_1(z) = \frac{alts(i+1) - z}{alts(i+1) - alts(i)}$
$fact(z, i) = \alpha_1(z)f_{i}(z) + \alpha_2(z)f_{i+1}(z)$
$\beta_1(ionp) = \frac{log_{10}(ionp) - log_{ion_prod}(i)}{log_{ion_prod}(i+1) - log_{ion_prod}(i)}$
$\beta_2(ionp) = 1 - \beta_1(ionp) = \frac{log_{ion_prod}(i+1) - log_{10}(ionp)}{log_{ion_prod}(i+1) - log_{ion_prod}(i)}$
$interp_factor_tbl(ionp, z) = \beta_1(ionp)f(i)(z) + \beta_2(ionp)f(i+1)(z)$
$hox\_prod\_factor(k) = interp\_factor\_tbl(ion\_pairs(k), zmid(k), lastk)$

===== File: examplePio.f90 =====

### Variables:
LEN (Short Notation for length of the data array)
VAL (Value used for writing to netcdf file)
myRank (Processor rank running the code)
ntasks (Number of processors participating in MPI communicator)
niotasks (Number of processors performing I/O)
stride (Stride in mpi rank between io tasks)
numAggregator (Number of aggregator)
optBase (Start index of I/O processors)
pioIoSystem (The ParallelIO system set up by @ref PIO_init)
pioFileDesc (Contains data identifying the file)
pioVar (The netCDF variable ID)
iodescNCells (An io descriptor handle that is generated in @ref PIO_initdecomp)
iotype (Specifies the flavor of netcdf output)
pioDimId (The netCDF dimension ID)
ista (1-based index of start of this processors data in full data array)
isto (Size of data array for this processor)
arrIdxPerPe (Number of elements handled by each processor)
dimLen (The length of the dimension of the netcdf variable)
compdof (Array describing the decomposition of the data)
dataBuffer (Buffer to hold sample data that is written to netcdf file)
readBuffer (Buffer to read data into)

### Equations:
LEN = 16 (Length of the data array is set to 16)
VAL = 42 (Value used for writing to netcdf file is set to 42)
ista = myRank * arrIdxPerPe + 1 (Start index of this processor's data in the full data array)
isto = ista + (arrIdxPerPe - 1) (End index of this processor's data in the full data array)
compdof(i, i=ista,isto) = (/i, i=ista,isto, 1/) (Decomposition of the data is calculated using a loop)
dataBuffer(i, i=ista,isto) = myRank + VAL (Sample data to be written to netcdf file is set to the processor rank plus the value used for writing)
start(1) = ista (Start index of the decomposition in the io descriptor handle)
count(1) = arrIdxPerPe (Size of the decomposition in the io descriptor handle)
PIO_initdecomp(pioIoSystem, PIO_int, dimLen, compdof(i=ista:isto), iodescNCells) (Decomposition is initialized using the ParallelIO library)
PIO_def_dim(pioFileDesc, 'x', dimLen(1), pioDimId) (Netcdf dimension is defined using the ParallelIO library)
PIO_def_var(pioFileDesc, 'foo', PIO_int, (/pioDimId/), pioVar) (Netcdf variable is defined using the ParallelIO library)
PIO_enddef(pioFileDesc) (Define mode is ended using the ParallelIO library)
PIO_write_darray(pioFileDesc, pioVar, iodescNCells, dataBuffer(i=ista:isto), retVal) (Data is written to netcdf file using the ParallelIO library)
start(1) = ista (Start index of the decomposition in the io descriptor handle for reading)
count(1) = arrIdxPerPe (Size of the decomposition in the io descriptor handle for reading)
PIO_read_darray(pioFileDesc, pioVar, iodescNCells, readBuffer, retVal) (Data is read from netcdf file using the ParallelIO library)
deallocate(compdof) (Memory allocated for the decomposition is deallocated)
deallocate(dataBuffer) (Memory allocated for the sample data to be written is deallocated)
deallocate(readBuffer) (Memory allocated for the buffer to read data into is deallocated)
PIO_freedecomp(pioIoSystem, iodescNCells) (Decomposition is freed using the ParallelIO library)
PIO_finalize(pioIoSystem, ierr) (ParallelIO library resources are finalized)
MPI_Finalize(ierr) (MPI library resources are finalized)

===== File: lcbas.F90 =====

### Variables:

### Equations:
X01 = G(1) - G(2)
X02 = G(1) - G(3)
X03 = G(1) - G(4)
X12 = G(2) - G(3)
X13 = G(2) - G(4)
X23 = G(3) - G(4)
B1(i) = G(i) for i=1:4
B2(1) = 1 / (X01 * X02 * X03)
B2(2) = -1 / (X01 * X12 * X13)
B2(3) = 1 / (X02 * X12 * X23)
B2(4) = -1 / (X03 * X13 * X23)

===== File: dadadj.F90 =====

### Variables:
lchnk (LCHNK)
ncol (NCOL)
pmid (PMID)
pint (PINT)
pdel (PD)
t (T)
q (Q)
i, jiter, k (indices)
c1dad (C1DAD)
c2dad (C2DAD)
c3dad (C3DAD)
c4dad (C4DAD)
gammad (GAMMAD)
zeps (ZEPS)
rdenom (RDENOM)
dtdp (DTD)
zepsdp (ZEPSDP)
zgamma (ZGAMMA)
qave (QAVE)
ilconv (ILCONV)
dodad (DODAD)

### Equations:
gammad = cappa*0.5_r8*(t(i,2) + t(i,1))/pint(i,2)
dtdp = (t(i,2) - t(i,1))/(pmid(i,2) - pmid(i,1))
dodad(i) = (dtdp + zeps) .gt. gammad
c1dad(k) = cappa*0.5_r8*(pmid(i,k+1)-pmid(i,k))/pint(i,k+1)
rdenom = 1._r8/(pdel(i,k)*c2dad(k) + pdel(i,k+1))
c3dad(k) = rdenom*pdel(i,k)
c4dad(k) = rdenom*pdel(i,k+1)
zepsdp = zeps*(pmid(i,k+1) - pmid(i,k))
t(i,k+1) = t(i,k)*c3dad(k) + t(i,k+1)*c4dad(k)
qave = (pdel(i,k+1)*q(i,k+1) + pdel(i,k)*q(i,k))/(pdel(i,k+1)+pdel(i,k))
q(i,k) = qave

===== File: bshr_timer_mod.F90 =====

### Variables:
`n` (N)
`str` (STR)
`cycles` (CYCLES)
`s_loglev` (S_LOGLEV)
`s_logunit` (S_LOGUNIT)
`stat_free` (STAT_FREE)
`stat_inuse` (STAT_INUSE)
`stat_started` (STAT_STARTED)
`stat_stopped` (STAT_STOPPED)
`max_timers` (MAX_TIMERS)
`cycles1` (CYCLES1)
`cycles2` (CYCLES2)
`cycles_max` (CYCLES_MAX)
`dt` (DT)
`calls` (CALLS)
`clock_rate` (CLK_RATE)

### Equations:
$CYCLES = system\_clock(count\_rate=cycles, count\_max=cycles\_max)$
This equation is used to get the count rate and max count of the system clock.
$if (cycles /= 0) then$
This equation is used to calculate the clock rate in seconds per cycle, given that a valid count rate is provided.
$dt(n) = dt(n) + CLK\_RATE \times (CYCLES2(n) - CYCLES1(n))$
This equation is used to update the accumulated time for a timer after it has been stopped and started again. If `CYCLES2` is less than `CYCLES1`, it indicates that the system clock has wrapped around, so we add `CYCLES_MAX + CYCLES2 - CYCLES1` instead of just `CYCLES2 - CYCLES1`.
$calls(n) = calls(n) + 1$
This equation is used to increment the number of samples in the accumulation for a timer after it has been stopped and started again.
$if (s\_loglev > 0)$
This equation is used to write an error message to the log unit if `S_LOGLEV` is greater than zero.

### Equations:
$\frac{z}{\Delta z} = \frac{\text{layer depth}}{\text{layer thickness}}$ ($\frac{z}{dz}$)
$\frac{\partial z_i}{\partial i} = 0$ (constant layer depth, subscript $i$)
$\int_{begc}^{endc} \Delta b_{d, col} d i = \int_{begp}^{endp} b_{d, col} d p$ (integration of bulk density over layers and grid points, subscript $p$)
$\frac{\partial hksat_col}{\partial i} = 0$ (constant hydraulic conductivity at saturation, subscript $i$)
$\int_{begc}^{endc} \Delta bsw_{col,i} d i = \int_{begp}^{endp} bsw_{col,i} d p$ (integration of Clapp and Hornberger "b" over layers and grid points, subscript $i$, $p$)
$\frac{\partial watsat_col}{\partial i} = 0$ (constant volumetric soil water at saturation, subscript $i$)
$\int_{begc}^{endc} \Delta watmin_{col,i} d i = \int_{begp}^{endp} watmin_{col,i} d p$ (integration of minimum volumetric soil water over layers and grid points, subscript $i$, $p$)
$\frac{\partial suc_col}{\partial i} = 0$ (constant minimum soil suction, subscript $i$)
$\int_{begc}^{endc} \Delta watfc_{col,i} d i = \int_{begp}^{endp} watfc_{col,i} d p$ (integration of volumetric soil water at field capacity over layers and grid points, subscript $i$, $p$)
$\frac{\partial porosity_col}{\partial i} = 0$ (constant soil porosity, subscript $i$)
$\int_{begc}^{endc} \Delta eff_porosity_{col,i} d i = \int_{begp}^{endp} eff_porosity_{col,i} d p$ (integration of effective porosity over layers and grid points, subscript $i$, $p$)
$\frac{\partial cellorg_col}{\partial i} = 0$ (constant organic matter for gridcell containing column, subscript $i$)
$\int_{begc}^{endc} \Delta rootfr_col{i} d i = \int_{begp}^{endp} rootfr_col{i} d p$ (integration of fraction of roots in each soil layer over layers and grid points, subscript $i$, $p$)
$\frac{\partial tkwet_col}{\partial i} = 0$ (constant thermal conductivity, wet soil, subscript $i$)
$\int_{begc}^{endc} \Delta tkdry_col{i} d i = \int_{begp}^{endp} tkdry_col{i} d p$ (integration of thermal conductivity, dry soil over layers and grid points, subscript $i$, $p$)
$\frac{\partial tkfrz_col}{\partial i} = 0$ (constant thermal conductivity, frozen soil, subscript $i$)
$\int_{begc}^{endc} \Delta csol_col{i} d i = \int_{begp}^{endp} csol_col{i} d p$ (integration of heat capacity, soil solids over layers and grid points, subscript $i$, $p$)

### Variables:

### Equations:
$\frac{\partial q_m}{\partial t} = \frac{1}{L_x L_y} \sum_{i=1}^{N} [q(i) - q_m]$
$t_vt_pert = t - qm$
$q_vt_pert = qv + qcl + qci - qm$
$\frac{\partial t_vt}{\partial t} = \sum_{i=1}^{N_x N_y} [t_vt_pert(i,j,k)]^2$
$\frac{\partial q_vt}{\partial t} = \sum_{i=1}^{N_x N_y} [q_vt_pert(i,j,k)]^2$
$t_pert_scale = \sqrt{ 1 + \frac{\Delta t}{t_vt} \cdot t_vt_tend }$
$q_pert_scale = \sqrt{ 1 + \frac{\Delta t}{q_vt} \cdot q_vt_tend }$

orce_prognostic_true = .false.
nunit = shr_file_getUnit()
open (nunit,file=trim(filename),status="old",action="read")
read (nunit,nml=drof_nml,iostat=ierr)
close(nunit)
call shr_file_freeUnit(nunit)
err > 0
write(logunit,F01)'ERROR: reading input namelist, '//trim(filename)//' iostat=',ierr
call shr_sys_abort(subName//': namelist read error '//trim(filename))
write(logunit,F00)' decomp     = ',trim(decomp)
write(logunit,F00)' restfilm   = ',trim(restfilm)
write(logunit,F00)' restfils   = ',trim(restfils)
write(logunit,F0L)' force_prognostic_true = ',force_prognostic_true
call shr_mpi_bcast(decomp  ,mpicom,'decomp')
all shr_mpi_bcast(restfilm,mpicom,'restfilm')
call shr_mpi_bcast(restfils,mpicom,'restfils')
call shr_mpi_bcast(force_prognostic_true,mpicom,'force_prognostic_true')
rest_file = trim(restfilm)
rest_file_strm = trim(restfils)
call shr_strdata_readnml(SDROF,trim(filename),mpicom=mpicom)
datamode = trim(SDROF%dataMode)

if (trim(datamode) == 'NULL' .or. &
    trim(datamode) == 'COPYALL') then
   if (my_task == master_task) then
      write(logunit,F00) 'drof datamode = ',trim(datamode)
   end if
else
rite(logunit,F00) ' ERROR illegal drof datamode = ',trim(datamode)
 call shr_sys_abort()
end if
rof_present    = .false.
rofice_present = .false.
rof_prognostic = .false.
flood_present  = .false.
orce_prognostic_true
of_present
ofice_present
of_prognostic
flood_present
if (trim(datamode) /= 'NULL') then
   rof_present = .true.
   rofice_present = .true.
end if

### Equations:
$\boxed{ps = p00 - dp \cdot e^{-(gr/Rp)^{exppr}}}$ (PS equation)
$\boxed{p = \left\{
$\boxed{\psi s = 0}$ (PHIS equation, constant surface geopotential)
$\boxed{\rho = p / (Rdt(1 + constTvq))}$ (RHO equation, density of moist air)
$\boxed{t = \left\{
$\boxed{q = \left\{
$\boxed{w = 0}$ (W equation, vertical velocity, zero for non-hydrostatic models)
$\boxed{u = ufac * (-fgr/2 + sqrt((fgr/2)^2 - exppr*(gr/Rp)^{exppr}*Rd(T0-gammaz)/(exppz*zh*Rd(T0-gammaz)/(gzp^{exppz}*(1-p00/dp*e^{(gr/Rp)^{exppr}}e^{(z/zp)^{exppz}})))))}$ (U equation, zonal wind)
$\boxed{v = vfac * (-fgr/2 + sqrt((fgr/2)^2 - exppr*(gr/Rp)^{exppr}*Rd(T0-gammaz)/(exppz*zh*Rd(T0-gammaz)/(gzp^{exppz}*(1-p00/dp*e^{(gr/Rp)^{exppr}}e^{(z/zp)^{exppz}})))))}$ (V equation, meridional wind)
$\boxed{fpiF = phere - (p00 - dp \cdot e^{-(gr/Rp)^{exppr}}*e^{-(zhere/zp)^{exppz})) * ((T0-gamma zhere)/T0)^{(g/(Rdgamma))}}$ (Fixed point iteration function)
$\boxed{fpidFdz = - exppz*zhere*dp*e^{-(gr/Rp)^{exppr}}*e^{-(zhere/zp)^{exppz)) / (zp^2) * ((T0-gamma zhere)/T0)^{(g/(Rdgamma))} + g/(Rd*T0) * (p00-dp*e^{(gr/Rp)^{exppr}}*e^{-(zhere/zp)^{exppz))) * ((T0-gamma zhere)/T0)^{(g/(Rdgamma)-1))}$ (Fixed point iteration derivative function)

`use_smliq_pnd` (Use_SmLiq_PND)

### Equations:
$\frac{\partial V_{pond}}{\partial t} = \frac{r_{frac}}{\rho_{freshwater}} \left( \dot{m}_{t op} + \dot{m}_{s now} + F_{rain} \right) A_{ice}$ (1)
$T_{p} = T_{melt} - T_d$ (2)
$\frac{\partial V_{pond}}{\partial t} = V_{pond} \left( 1 - \exp\left(-\frac{T_{d}-T_{sfc}}{T_{p}} rexp \right) \right)$ (3)
$bdt = \frac{-2k_{ice}\Delta T \Delta t}{\rho_i L_{fresh}}$ (4)
$\dot{h}_{lid} = bdt^{1/2}$ (5)
$\dot{h}_{lid} = p_5bdt/h_{lid}$ (6)
$dV_{pond} = -\dot{h}_{lid} A_{pond} \rho_i / \rho_f$ (7)
$A_{pond} = A_{ice} \min \left( 1, \frac{\sqrt{V_{pond}/(p_{aspect} A_{ice})}}{A_{ice}} \right)$ (8)
$h_{pond} = p_{aspect} A_{pond}$ (9)
$dV_{pond} = -\Delta t \frac{k_{ice}\Delta T}{\rho_i L_{fresh}}$ (10)
$\Phi = S_{br}/(1-e^{-3}-Depress_T/T)$ (11)
$\Phi = salinity/S_{br}$ (12)
$perm = 3.0e-8 \Phi^{3}$ (13)

===== File: cloud.F90 =====

### Variables:
`tbgmax` (Tbgmax)
`tbgmin` (Tbgmin)
`tprmax` (Tprmax)
`tprmin` (Tprmin)
`gamaz` (Γz)
`fac_cond` (Fcond)
`fac_fus` (Ffus)
`fac_sub` (Fsub)
`pres` (P)
`qp` (Qp)
`q` (Q)
`qn` (Qn)
`tabs` (T)

### Equations:
\(\an = \frac{1}{(tbgmax - tbgmin)}\)
\(bn = tbgmin \cdot an\)
\(ap = \frac{1}{(tprmax - tprmin)}\)
\(bp = tprmin \cdot ap\)
\(fac1 = fac_cond + (1+bp) \cdot fac_fus\)
\(fac2 = fac_fus \cdot ap\)
\(ag = \frac{1}{(tgrmax - tgrmin)}\)
\(tabs1 = \left(\frac{t + qp \cdot fac1}{1 + qp \cdot fac2}\right)\)
\(qsatt = \begin{cases} qsatw_crm(tabs1, pres), & \quad tabs1 \geq tbgmax \\ om \cdot qsatw_crm(tabs1, pres) + (1 - om) \cdot qsati_crm(tabs1, pres), & \quad tbgmin \leq tabs1 < tbgmax \\ qsati_crm(tabs1, pres), & \quad tabs1 \leq tbgmin \end{cases}\)
\(om = \begin{cases} 1, & \quad tabs1 \geq tbgmax \\ an \cdot tabs1 - bn, & \quad tbgmin \leq tabs1 < tbgmax \\ 0, & \quad tabs1 \leq tbgmin \end{cases}\)
\(lstarn = \begin{cases} fac_cond, & \quad tabs1 \geq tprmax \\ fac_sub, & \quad tabs1 \leq tprmin \\ fac_cond + (1 - om) \cdot fac_fus, & \quad tbprmin < tabs1 < tprmax \end{cases}\)
\(dlstarn = \begin{cases} 0, & \quad tabs1 \geq tprmax \\ 0, & \quad tabs1 \leq tprmin \\ an \cdot fac_fus, & \quad tbprmin < tabs1 < tprmax \end{cases}\)
\(dqsat = \begin{cases} dtqsatw_crm(tabs1, pres), & \quad tabs1 \geq tbgmax \\ om \cdot dtqsatw_crm(tabs1, pres) + (1 - om) \cdot dtqsati_crm(tabs1, pres), & \quad tbgmin \leq tabs1 < tbgmax \\ dtqsati_crm(tabs1, pres), & \quad tabs1 \leq tbgmin \end{cases}\)
\(lstarp = \begin{cases} fac_cond, & \quad tabs1 \geq tprmax \\ fac_sub, & \quad tabs1 \leq tprmin \\ fac_cond + (1 - om) \cdot fac_fus, & \quad tbprmin < tabs1 < tprmax \end{cases}\)
\(dlstarp = \begin{cases} 0, & \quad tabs1 \geq tprmax \\ 0, & \quad tabs1 \leq tprmin \\ ap \cdot fac_fus, & \quad tbprmin < tabs1 < tprmax \end{cases}\)
\(fff = t - tabs1 + lstarn \cdot (q - qsatt) + lstarp \cdot qp\)
\(dfff = dlstarn \cdot (q - qsatt) + dlstarp \cdot qp - lstarn \cdot dqsat - 1\)
\(dtabs = -\frac{fff}{dfff}\)

===== File: mo_aero_settling.F90 =====

### Variables:
ad (air density)
avo_num (Avogadro's number)
Cc_nat (Cunningham Correction Factor)
c1 (constant, 2/9)
cond_hno3 (condensed phase HNO3 concentration)
const_nat (constant, exp(8*ln^2 sigma))
dep (molecules/cm**3 deposited)
dt_dz (dt/dz, sec cm-1)
flux_nat (aerosol flux, Cond-phase molecules cm-2 sec-1)
gravity (gravity, cm/s^2)
gravit (gravity, g cm/s^2)
hno3_cond (Condensed Phase HNO3 concentration)
lon_mask (longitude logic mask)
mean_vel (mean molecular velocity, cm sec-1)
mfp (Mean Free Path, cm)
MW_air (molecular weight of air, grams/mole)
nat_dens (density of NAT aerosol, g/cm^3)
press (pressure, Pa)
rad_nat (radius of NAT aerosol, cm)
radius_nat (mean radius of NAT aerosol, cm)
sigma_nat (width of the log normal distribution for NAT)
temp (temperature, K)
timestep (model time step, s)
vel_nat (terminal velocity of NAT aerosol, cm sec-1)
zstar (altitude, km)

### Equations:
mean_vel  = sqrt(av_const*t/MW_air)*100_r8
$\bar{v}$ is the mean molecular velocity (cm sec-1)
$a_1$ is a constant (av_const)
$t$ is temperature (K)
$M$ is the molecular weight of air (g mol^-1)
visc = (t*1.458e-6)**1.5 / (t + 110.4)*10000
$\mu$ is the dynamic viscosity of air (g cm^-1 sec^-1)
$b_1$ is a constant (1.458e-6)
$b_2$ is a constant (110.4)
$c_1$ is a constant (10000)
mfp = visc* avo_num / (.499*atm_dens*MW_air*mean_vel)
$\lambda$ is the Mean Free Path (cm)
$A$ is Avogadro's number
$N$ is the atmospheric density (molecules cm^-3)
dt_dz = timestep / ((zstar(i,k-1) - zstar(i,k))*km2cm)
$\frac{dt}{dz}$ is the dt/dz (sec cm^-1)
$\Delta t$ is the model time step (s)
$\Delta z$ is the altitude difference between two levels (km)
vel_nat = c1*rad_nat**2 * nat_dens*gravity*Cc_nat/visc
$v_{nat}$ is the terminal velocity of NAT aerosol (cm sec^-1)
$r$ is the radius of NAT aerosol (cm)
$\rho$ is the density of NAT aerosol (g cm^-3)
$g$ is gravity (cm s^-2)
$C_c$ is the Cunningham Correction Factor
flux_nat = cond_hno3*vel_nat* const_nat
$F_{nat}$ is the aerosol flux of NAT (Cond-phase molecules cm^-2 sec^-1)
$N$ is the atmospheric density (molecules cm^-3)
$v_{nat}$ is the terminal velocity of NAT aerosol (cm sec^-1)
$C$ is a constant (const_nat)
depos = min(cond_hno3,flux_nat*dt_dz)
$D$ is the number of molecules deposited per unit area (molecules cm^-2)
$N$ is the atmospheric density (molecules cm^-3)
$F$ is the aerosol flux of NAT (Cond-phase molecules cm^-2 sec^-1)
$\frac{\Delta t}{\Delta z}$ is the dt/dz (sec cm^-1)

===== File: intpressure.F90 =====

### Variables:
$P$ (Pressure)
$\rho_0$ (Mean density)
$g$ (Gravitational acceleration)
$\zeta$ (Surface elevation)
$B$ (Mean buoyancy)
$x$, $y$ (Horizontal coordinates)
$\Delta x$, $\Delta y$ (Small length scales)
$\partial_xS$, $\partial_yS$ (Salinity gradients)
$\partial_x\Theta$, $\partial_y\Theta$ (Temperature gradients)
$\Delta_xS$, $\Delta_yS$ (Finite difference salinity increments)
$\Delta_x\Theta$, $\Delta_y\Theta$ (Finite difference temperature increments)

### Equations:
$\frac{\partial P}{\partial z} + g \overline{\rho} = 0$ [HydroStat]
$-\frac{1}{\rho_0} \frac{\partial P}{\partial x} = -g \frac{\partial \zeta}{\partial x} + \int_{z}^{\zeta} \frac{\partial B}{\partial x} dz'$ [InternalPressurex]
$-\frac{1}{\rho_0} \frac{\partial P}{\partial y} = -g \frac{\partial \zeta}{\partial y} + \int_{z}^{\zeta} \frac{\partial B}{\partial y} dz'$ [InternalPressurey]
$\frac{\partial B}{\partial x} \approx \frac{B(S+\Delta_xS,\Theta+\Delta_x\Theta,P) - B(S,\Theta,P)}{\Delta x}$ [BuoyancyGradientx]
$\frac{\partial B}{\partial y} \approx \frac{B(S+\Delta_yS,\Theta+\Delta_y\Theta,P) - B(S,\Theta,P)}{\Delta y}$ [BuoyancyGrady]
$int = 0.5h(nlev)*dxB(nlev)$ [InternalPressureGradientx]
$idpdx(i) = int + 0.5h(i+1)*dxB(i+1) + 0.5h(i)*dxB(i)$ [InternalPressureGradientxRecursion]
$int = 0.5h(nlev)*dyB(nlev)$ [InternalPressureGrady]
$idpdy(i) = int + 0.5h(i+1)*dyB(i+1) + 0.5h(i)*dyB(i)$ [InternalPressureGradyRecursion]

### Equations:
V >= L(N) => R = N (V ≥ L⁽ᴿ⁾ )
V ≤ L(1) => R = 1 (V ≤ L¹ )
otherwise, M := {L < V}, T = minloc(M) ⇒ {if |Lᵗˡ⁻¹ - V| < |Lᵗˡ - V| then R = ᵗˡ - 1 else R = ᵗˡ} (otherwise, R = ⌊T⌋ )

===== File: coriolis.F90 =====

### Variables:
v_av (Vavg)
w_av (Wavg)
u_av (Uavg)
dudt(icrm,i,j,k,na) (DUDT)
dvdt(icrm,i,j,k,na) (DVDT)

### Equations:
Vavg = 0.25*(v(icrm,i,j,k) + v(icrm,i,jc,k) + v(icrm,ib,j,k) + v(icrm,ib,jc,k))
Wavg = 0.25*(w(icrm,i,j,kc) + w(icrm,ib,j,kc) + w(icrm,i,j,k) + w(icrm,ib,j,k))
dudt = DUDT + fcory(icrm,j)*(v_av - vg0(icrm,k)) - fcorzy(icrm,j)*w_av
u_av = 0.25*(u(icrm,i,j,k) + u(icrm,ic,j,k) + u(icrm,i,jb,k) + u(icrm,ic,jb,k))
dvdt = DVDT - 0.5*(fcory(icrm,j) + fcory(icrm,jb))*(u_av - ug0(icrm,k))
dudt = DUDT + fcory(icrm,j)*(v(icrm,i,j,k) - vg0(icrm,k)) - fcorzy(icrm,j)*w_av
dvdt = DVDT - fcory(icrm,j)*(u(icrm,i,j,k) - ug0(icrm,k))

### Equations:
$C=\sum_{i=1}^{N}\left(C_i+R_i\right)$
Where $C$ is the total cumulative runtime, $C_i$ is the initial cumulative runtime of timer $i$, and $R_i$ is the runtime of timer $i$. This equation is used in `reset_timers` subroutine when resetting all timers.
$\text{runtime}=\text{get\_time}-\text{timer}\%\text{cur\_start}$
Where $\text{runtime}$ is the time elapsed since timer started, and $\text{get\_time}$ is the current wall clock time. This equation is used in `stop_timer` subroutine when stopping a timer.
$\text{get\_time}=\left\{\begin{aligned}
Where $\text{get\_time}$ is the current wall clock time, and $t_\text{stampf}$, `MPI_Wtime()`, and `cpu_time` are functions that return different types of timing data. This equation defines the `get_time` function.
$\text{cumulative\_runtime}=\text{cumulative\_runtime}+\text{runtime}$
Where $\text{cumulative\_runtime}$ is the cumulative runtime of a timer, and $\text{runtime}$ is the time elapsed since the timer was started. This equation is used in `stop_timer` subroutine when stopping a timer.

$\epsilon_b$ (eps_b, not explicitly defined in this file but used from another module)

### Equations:
$P_b = \epsilon_b \quad \text{(kbEquilibrium)}$
$k_b = r \dfrac{k}{\epsilon} P_b = c_b \dfrac{k}{\epsilon} P_b \quad \text{(kbAgebraic)}$

================================================================================

===== File: genericeq.F90 =====

### Variables:
$k$ (TKE)
$\epsilon$ (Rate of dissipation)
$l$ (Dissipative length-scale)
$\psi$ (Generic variable)
$\omega$ (Inverse turbulent time scale)
$\nu_t$ (Eddy viscosity)
$\sigma_\psi$ (Diffusivity for $\psi$)
$\kappa$ (von Kármán constant)
$d$ (Temporal decay rate for homogeneous turbulence)
$B(i)$ (Buoyancy frequency squared at layer $i$)

### Equations:
$\label{tkeA}$: Not explicitly defined in the code, assumed to be a general equation for TKE.
$\label{epsilon}$: $\epsilon = c_\mu^0 \frac{k^{3/2}}{l}$
$\psi_l$: $\psi = (c_\mu^0)^p k^m l^n$
$\label{PandG}$: Not explicitly defined in the code, assumed to be a general equation for production terms $P$ and $G$.
$\label{Ri_st}$: Not explicitly defined in the code, assumed to be a general equation for Richardson number.
$\label{diffusionTKE}$: Not explicitly defined in the code, assumed to be a general diffusion equation for TKE.
$\label{diffusionGeneric}$: ${\cal D}_\psi = \frac{\partial}{\partial z} \left( \frac{\nu_t}{\sigma_\psi} \frac{\partial \psi}{\partial z} \right)$
$\label{diffusionMYTKE}$: Not explicitly defined in the code, assumed to be a diffusion equation for TKE used in Mellor-Yamada model.
$\label{diffusionMYlength}$: Not explicitly defined in the code, assumed to be a diffusion equation for length-scale used in Mellor-Yamada model.
$\eq{generic}$: $\dot{\psi} = {\cal D}_\psi + \frac{\psi}{k} ( c_{\psi_1} P + c_{\psi 3} G - c_{\psi 2} \epsilon )$
$\eq{KW}$: $\dot{\omega} = {\cal D}_\omega + \frac{\omega}{k} ( c_{\omega_1} P + c_{\omega 3} G - c_{\omega 2} \epsilon )$

===== File: simple_map_mod.F90 =====

### Variables:
`n_overlaps` (N)

### Equations:
N = size(overlap_weights) (1)
size(source_indices) = N (2)
size(dest_indices) = N (3)
this%n_overlaps = N (4)
n_source_points = maxval(source_indices) (5)
n_dest_points = maxval(dest_indices) (6)
overlap_found(source_index, dest_index) = .true. when overlap_index = 1 to N and source_index = 1 to n_source_points and dest_index = 1 to n_dest_points (7)
this%overlap_weights(overlap_index) > 0 when overlap_index = 1 to N (8)
In equations 5 and 6, `maxval` is a function that returns the maximum value in an array. The notation `maxval(source_indices)` means "the maximum value of source_indices".

### Equations:
(1) N = size(overlap_weights)
(2) size(source_indices) = N
(3) size(dest_indices) = N
(4) this%n_overlaps = N
(5) n_source_points = max[1 ≤ i ≤ N](source_indices(i))
(6) n_dest_points = max[1 ≤ i ≤ N](dest_indices(i))
(7) overlap_found(source_index, dest_index) = ∃ 1 ≤ j ≤ N : source_indices(j) = source_index and dest_indices(j) = dest_index
(8) this%overlap_weights(overlap_index) > 0 ∀ 1 ≤ i ≤ N

Equation: `size = dims * stride` [equation 1]
Equation: `memory_usage = size * element_size` [equation 2]
Equation: `max_index = (string_length + stride - 1) / stride` [equation 3]
Equation: `attribute_size = dims * data_type_size` [equation 4]
Equation: `total_elements = prod(dims)` [equation 5]

===== File: compare_var.F90 =====

### Variables:

`varo` (O)

### Equations:
V ≠ O
f_i ∨ f_o ⇒ V ∉ {NF_FLOAT, NF_DOUBLE}
(V ∉ {NF_FLOAT, NF_DOUBLE}) ∧ (O ∉ {NF_FLOAT, NF_DOUBLE}) ∨ (f_i ∧ f_o)

===== File: scrip_iounitsmod.f90 =====

### Variables:
iunit (iu)
n (n)
alreadyInUse (au)
SCRIP_IOUnitsInitialized (.iniu)
SCRIP_stdin (sstd)
SCRIP_stdout (ssout)
SCRIP_stderr (sserr)
SCRIP_IOUnitsMinUnits (minus)
SCRIP_IOUnitsMaxUnits (maxu)
filename (fn)

### Equations:
.iniu = .false. (.iniu = F)
.iniu = .true. (.iniu = T)
iunit = n (iu = n)
if (.not. .iniu) then (.not. iniu)
SCRIP_IOUnitsInUse(n) = .false. (!inuse[n] = 0)
SCRIP_IOUnitsInUse(iunit) = .true. (!inuse[iu] = 1)
stop 'SCRIP_IOUnitsGet: No free units' (stop "No free units")
if (.not. au) then (.not. au)
alreadyInUse = .true. (au = T)
SCRIP_IOUnitsReserve(iunit) = .false. (!reserv[iu] = 0)
SCRIP_IOUnitsReserve(iunit) = .true. (!reserv[iu] = 1)
stop 'SCRIP_IOUnitsRelease: bad unit' (stop "bad unit")
stop 'SCRIP_IOUnitsReserve: invalid unit' (stop "invalid unit")
stop 'SCRIP_IOUnitsReserve: unit already in use by SCRIP' (stop "unit already in use by SCRIP")
stop 'SCRIP_IOUnitsReserve: unit already in use by others' (stop "unit already in use by others")
SCRIP_IOUnitsRedirect(iunit, filename) = .true. (!redirect[iu] = 1)
open(unit=iunit, file=filename, status='old', form='formatted') (open(iu, fn, ool, ffmt))
open(unit=iunit, file=filename, status='unknown', form='formatted') (open(iu, fn, unk, ffmt))

### Equations:
Equation in standard mathematical format (use subscript and superscript)
a2x = mapper_Sa2W * atm
o2x = mapper_So2W * ocn
i2x = mapper_Si2W * ice
x2w = a2x + o2x + i2x * fractions_wx
x2w (output) is calculated using the following formula:
For each field, it's a combination of all three input wave states (`a2x_w`, `o2x_w`, and `i2x_w`) weighted by their corresponding fractions from `fractions_wx`.

This equation is used in `prep_wav_calc_a2x_wx` subroutine.

### Equations:

i2x_wx(eii) = mapper_si2w%map(i2x_ix, norm=.true.)

===== File: SystemOfEquationsThermalAuxType.F90 =====

### Variables:
Temperature (T)
Liquid water areal density (h2osoi_liq) -> liq_areal_den
Ice areal density (h2osoi_ice) -> ice_areal_den
Fraction (-) -> frac
Snow water (mm H2O) -> snow_water
Number of snow layers (n) -> num_snow_layer
Thermal diffusion coefficient (kappa) is not present but dhsdT can be related to it, and 1/dz * dist_up is used in an equation for thermal conduction
Distances up (m) -> dist_up
Distances down (m) -> dist_dn
Tuning factor (-) -> tuning_factor
Thermal conductivity (W/(m K)) -> therm_cond
Heat capacity per unit volume (J/(m3 K)) -> heat_cap_pva

### Equations:
dhsdT = 0 (No thermal diffusion at initial state, which is the case here)
liq_areal_den * frac + ice_areal_den * frac + snow_water / dist_up^2 = 0
num_snow_layer * (dist_dn - dist_up) = 0
liq_areal_den + ice_areal_den + snow_water / dist_up^2 + tuning_factor * thermal_conduction / L^-1 = 0
heat_cap_pva * condition_value + therm_cond * dhsdT = 0

$\text{ierr} = 0 \implies \text{do while ( ierr /= 0 ) ... }$
$\text{ndep\_fields} = 'Faxa_' // \text{trim(ndep_list(i))}$

$\text{i} \geq 1 \implies \text{exit }$

$\frac{\partial \text{col\_ns}\%smin\_no3\_vr}{\partial t} = -(\text{col\_nf}\%smin\_no3\_leached\_vr + \text{col\_nf}\%smin\_no3\_runoff\_vr) \cdot dt$
$\frac{\partial \text{col\_ns}\%sminn\_vr}{\partial t} = \frac{\partial \text{col\_ns}\%smin\_no3\_vr}{\partial t} + \frac{\partial \text{col\_ns}\%smin\_nh4\_vr}{\partial t}$
$\frac{\partial \text{col\_ns}\%decomp\_npools\_vr}{\partial t} = \text{col\_ns}\%decomp\_npools\_vr + \text{col\_nf}\%fire\_mortality\_n\_to\_cwdn \cdot dt$
$\frac{\partial \text{col\_ns}\%decomp\_npools\_vr}{\partial t} = \text{col\_ns}\%decomp\_npools\_vr + \text{col\_nf}\%m\_n\_to\_litr\_met\_fire \cdot dt$
$\frac{\partial \text{col\_ns}\%decomp\_npools\_vr}{\partial t} = \text{col\_ns}\%decomp\_npools\_vr + \text{col\_nf}\%m\_n\_to\_litr\_cel\_fire \cdot dt$
$\frac{\partial \text{col\_ns}\%decomp\_npools\_vr}{\partial t} = \text{col\_ns}\%decomp\_npools\_vr + \text{col\_nf}\%m\_n\_to\_litr\_lig\_fire \cdot dt$
$\frac{\partial \text{col\_ns}\%decomp\_npools\_vr}{\partial t} = -\text{col\_nf}\%m\_decomp\_npools\_to\_fire\_vr \cdot dt$
$\frac{\partial \text{veg\_ns}\%leafn}{\partial t} = -\text{veg\_nf}\%m\_leafn\_to\_fire \cdot dt$
$\frac{\partial \text{veg\_ns}\%frootn}{\partial t} = -\text{veg\_nf}\%m\_frootn\_to\_fire \cdot dt$

$\frac{\partial \text{vegNsLivestemn}}{\partial t} = -\left(\text{vegnFmortalityFluxFromLiveStemNToFire} + \text{vegnFmortalityFluxFromLiveStemNToLitterFire}\right) \Delta t$

### Equations:
$T_{mid,i,k} = T_{i,p}$ for $k=k_{top}$ to $k=k_{bot}$ (Eq. 1)
$P_{mid,i,k} = P_{i,p}$ for $k=k_{top}$ to $k=k_{bot}$ (Eq. 2)
$P_{int,i,k} = 0.5 \cdot P_{i, k+1}$ for $k=k_{top}$ (Eq. 3)
$t_{int,i,1} = T_{i,1}$ (Eq. 4)
$t_{int,i,k} = T_{i,k} - \frac{L_{k}}{L_{k-1}} (T_{i,k} - T_{i,k-1})$ for $2 \leq k \leq p_{ver}$ (Eq. 5)
$t_{int,i,p+1} = \sqrt{\sqrt{LW_{up,i}/\sigma}}$ (Eq. 6)

===== File: LinearAlgebraMod.F90 =====

### Variables:
`a` (A)
`b` (B)
`dxdt` (X)
`ii` (Ii)
`ij` (Ij)
`tiny_val` (ε)

### Equations:
X[Ii] = 0 for all Ii
X = a * b, where a is a matrix and b is a vector, in transpose mode 'N'
X = a' * b, where a' is the transpose of matrix a and b is a vector, in non-transpose mode
dy[i] = dy[i] + da*dx[i], where i ranges from 1 to m and m is the remainder of n modulo 5
dy[i+k] = dy[i+k] + da*dx[i+k], where k ranges from 0 to 4 and (i,k) ranges from (mp1,0) to (n,4)
dy[iy] = dy[iy] + da*dx[ix], where ix and iy are computed based on incx and incy
Δx = |incx|
Δy = |incy|
x0 and y0 are computed as x0 = (-n+1)Δx + 1 and y0 = (-n+1)Δy + 1 if incx or incy is negative. Otherwise, they are set to 1.
n is the length of vector dy or dx
m is the remainder of n modulo 5
j ranges from 6 to n with a step of 5

================================================================================

### Equations:
qbot(n) = qsat(Ts(n)) / rbot(n)         (1)
delt   = thbot(n) - Ts(n)                (2)
delq   = qbot(n) - ssq                     (3)
cp     = loc_cpdair*(1.0_R8 + loc_cpvir*ssq)(4)
ltheat = loc_latvap + loc_latice           (5)
ustar  = rdn * vmag                          (6)
tstar  = rhn * delt                         (7)
qstar  = ren * delq                         (8)

### Equations:
Equation in standard mathematical format (use subscript and superscript)

### Equations:
C_p * τ * T* / u* = S
ltheat * τ * q* / u* = L
loc_stebol * ts^4 = LWUP
L / ltheat = E
τ * v / vmag = TAUX
τ * w / vmag = TAUy
1/rdn = 1/sqrt(cn)
sqrt(cm)/ch = 1/rh
1/rh = 1/(sqrt(cn)*sqrt(cm))
log(1 + (ztref/zbot)(exp(bn)-1)) = ln0
log(1 + (ztref/zbot)(exp(bn-bh)-1)) = ln3
fac = ((ln0 - ztref/zbot*bn)/(bh) * stable +
tref = ts + (tbot-ts)*fac
qref = qbot - delq*fac

tstar (T*)

### Equations:

### Equations:

|v| ≥ vmin

ssq   = qsat(ts(n)) / rbot(n)
ρs ≈ ρqsat(Ts)

∂ρs/∂Ts ≈ ∂ρqsat/∂T
delt   = thbot(n) - ts(n)
delq   = qbot(n) - ssq
alz    = log(zbot(n)/zref)

### Equations:
$\text{USTAR} = \frac{\text{RDN}}{\left( 1 + \frac{\text{RDN}^2}{\text{KARMAN}} \cdot (\alpha - \psi_m) \right)} \sqrt{\text{VMAG}^2}$, where RDN = $\frac{\text{KARMAN}}{\log(\frac{\text{ZBOT}}{\text{ZREF}})}$

### Equations:

sen = c_p * τ * t*
lat = l_thermal * τ * q*
lw_up = -σ * T^4
evap = lat / l_thermal

Equation:

L_n     = L_heav tau qstar
